name: Quiche Lorraine (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  In a food processor (or with a fork) mix 2.5 C. flour with 6 T. chilled vegetable shortening and 6 T. chilled, unsalted butter until the mixture looks like coarse meal.
2.  With the motor running, add 6-7 T. ice water.  Process until the dough pulls away from the sides of the processor.  Divide in two, flatten into disks between wax paper or plastic wrap, and refrigerate at least 1 hr.  Brown in a preheated oven at 450°F for 10 min.
3.  Saut
1 small onion in 2 T. butter.
4.  Mix 3 eggs, 2 C. half and half, salt to taste, 1/2 -2/3 lb. Swiss cheese (grated), 1 package of Danish ham (chopped) and the onion.
5.  Fill crusts with mixture and dust with cayenne and nutmeg.
6.  Cook at 450°F for 10 min., then lower temp to 325

name: F and cook another 20 min.
instructions:
s Stroganoff (serves six)
Marinate 2 lb. cubed beef in 1 C. red wine for at least 2 hr.  Flour the beef and brown in 4 T. butter.  Add 2 large chopped onions, and sauté until clear.  Add a clove of garlic (crushed), 1/4 C. soy sauce, the marinate wine and pepper to taste.  Cover and simmer at least 1 hr.
Add 1/2 lb. sliced mushrooms and cook another 10 min.
Serve with rice and yogurt.

name: Piroshki (serves six)
instructions:
1.  In a food processor (or by hand) mix 2.5 C. flour with 6 T. chilled vegetable shortening and 6 T. chilled, unsalted butter until the mixture looks like coarse meal.
2.  With the motor running, add 6-7 T. ice water.  Process until the dough pulls away from the sides of the processor.  Divide in two, flatten into disks between wax paper or plastic wrap, and refrigerate at least 1 hr.
3.  Roll out pastry into 6-in. circles.  Fill with:
ham, hard-boiled egg, green onion and sour cream,
bacon, mushrooms and sour cream, or
wild rice, hard-boiled egg, mushrooms and sour cream.
4.  Fold pastry, using water to seal well.  Baste with butter and bake at 400°F until brown (~ 20 min.).

name: Rahmschnitzel and Sp
instructions:
tzle (serves four)
1.  Sprinkle four veal cutlets with salt and pepper, then brown in 4 T. butter.  Add 2 T. chopped chives, 1 t. chopped parsley and 1/2 C. dry white wine.  Cover and simmer 25 min. then remove to a warm oven.
2.  Beat 2 eggs and combine with 1.5 C. flour, 1/2 C. water, 1/2 t. salt, and 1/4 t. double-acting baking powder and a pinch of nutmeg.  Mix well.
3.  Bring 3 C. water and 3 C. chicken stock to a boil.  Spoon small balls of the dough and cook until the sp
tzle float to the top (don
t crowd the pot).
4.  Blend in 1 t. flour into the sauté liquid and stir until slightly thickened. Stir in 1/2 C. sour cream and heat thoroughly.  Serve of the cutlets with the sp

name: Short Ribs in Beer with Gingered Carrots  (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 275°F.  In a large ovenproof/flameproof casserole, heat 2 T. vegetable oil over high heat on the stove.  Season  four trimmed beef short ribs (~3 lb) with salt and pepper, then dust with 1T flour (total).  When the oil just starts to smoke, sear the ribs on all sides until golden brown.
2.  Remove ribs and pour off all but 1 T. of the oil/fat.  Add 1 small onion (diced), 2 medium carrots (diced), 1 stalk celery (diced) and 1 small leek (rinsed and thinly sliced), and return to medium heat.  Cook, stirring frequently until caramelized (6-7 min).  Add 1 T. brown sugar and cook until sugar is melted.
3.  Return ribs to pot with vegetables. Add 2 12-oz. cans of stout ale, 3 C. veal or beef broth, zest from half an orange and half a lemon, 1/2 cinnamon stick and a sprig of thyme.  Bring to a simmer, cover and place in oven and cook 1 - 1.5 hr, until meat is very tender.  (Check occasionally to make sure that the ribs continue to simmer).
4.  Remove ribs from casserole and set aside.  Strain casserole contents through a fine sieve, discard solids and return liquid to the pan.  Bring to a simmer, skim off the fat Cream together 1 T. flour and 1 T. butter, Whisk into the liquid and simmer uncovered until reduced and thickened enough to cover the back of a spoon (20-30 min).  Season with salt.
5.  Combine 2 T. butter, 1 T. sugar, 1 T. kosher salt and pepper to taste in a saucepan over medium heat.  Once the butter is melted, add 1 3-in piece of ginger (peeled and julienned) and 2 small shallots (minced), and cook with stirring until they are softeened but not browned (2-3 min).  Add 1 lb. medium carrots (peeled and sliced on the bias into 1/4 in slices).
6.  Add 1 C. water to the pan and bring to a simmer.  Cover pan, and simmer on low until carrots are just done (~5 min).  Season to taste with salt and pepper, 1 T. minced parsley.
7.  Return ribs to casserole and warm.  Transfer to deep plates, cover with sauce and serve with carrots.

name: London Broil
instructions:
1.  Combine 1/2 C. olive oil, 1/2 C. soy sauce, 1/4 C. balsamic or red wine vinegar, 8 minced cloves of garlic and 4 T. chopped pickle mix.  Season generously with black pepper and marinade a 2-in. thick sirloin steak overnight in the refrigerator.
2.  Grill or broil the steak the next day.  Serve with warmed up marinade.

name: Churrasco Steak
instructions:
Combine 1. C. chopped parsley, 1 C. olive oil, 3 T. sherry vinegar, 1.5 t. cayenne,  6-8 minced cloves of garlic, 1.5 t. black pepper, a dash of cumin and 1/2 t. kosher salt.  Mix wel land refrigerate.  This is chimichurri.
Pour 2/3 of the chimichurrri into a sealable plastic bag with two 1-lb. skirt steaks.  Marinate at least one hour, then grill to desired doneness (they will cook fast).  Season with salt and pepper to taste, and serve with remaining chimichurri sauce.
Roast Beef  with Gravy
Preheat the oven to 500°F.  Rub a rolled (or standing) rib roast with kosher salt and pepper.  Place in a roasting pan fat side up and put in the oven, immediately lowering the temperature to 350
F.  Roast for 18-20 min. per pound (or until internal temperature is 150°F for medium rare).
Remove the roast to the serving plate.  Deglaze the roasting pan with water (or water and dry red wine).  Simmer the liquid over medium-low heat, adding salt and pepper to taste (along with any liquid accumulating in the serving plate).  Thicken with 2 T. cornstarch dissolved in 4 T. water.

name: Chicken-Fried Steak
instructions:
1.  Sandwich each of six cube steaks betwen wax paper and pound till 1/4 in. thick.
2.  Combine 2 eggs and 12 oz. evaporated milk.  Soak steaks in mix for a few minutes, then dredge through flour seasoned with salt and pepper (coating thoroughly).  Dip in milk/egg mixture again, then place on a rack and dust with flour on both sides.
3.  Fill a deep skillet to 1/2 in. with cooking oil (or shortening or, worse, lard) and heat to 360°F.  Ease steaks two at a time into oil and cook until golden on the bottom (about 4 min.).  Turn and cook another 3 min. or so.
4.  Remove, drain on a paper towel and keep warm.
5.  Melt 2-3 T. of butter in a saucepan, add 2 T. of the dredging flour and cook for 1 min., stirring constantly.  Combine 1 C. milk and 1/4 C. beef  broth and add slowly to the roue, stirring constantly.  Add salt and pepper to taste and cook until thickened.  Serve with steaks.

name: Prime Rib with David Burke
instructions:
s Sauce
Remove prime rib (3-rib portion prime rib - ~6-8 lb. and trimed of excess fat; preferably dry-aged) from the refirgerator 2 hr. before cooking.  Preheat the oven to 375°F.  Combine 1/4 C cayenne, 1/2 C. kosher salt, white and black pepper to taste in a small bowl.  Rub the mixture over the entire surface of the meat.  Place meat on a wire rack in a roasting pan, place in oven and cook 13-15 min per pound (or until a meat thermometer reads 125

name: F).  Allow to rest 15-20 minutes before carving.
instructions: In a medium pot, combine 2 C. Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 C. ketchup, 2 T. Tabasco sauce and heat over medium heat.  Reduce for 20-25 min with occasional stirring until thickened.  Whisk in 3 T. butter and 2 t. sesame oil.  Allow to cool before serving.

name: Alsatian Beef and Mushroom Tart (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Place 2 C. flour with salt to taste in a food processor, then add 12 T. very cold butter (cut into pieces).  Add 2 egg yolks and 2-4 T. cold water in small portions, blending briefly after each addition.  Gather the dough into a ball, wrap in wax paper and chill.
2.  Preheat oven to 375
1/2 C. finely chopped onions in 1 T. of butter until wilted.  Add 1 lb. lean ground beef and cook, breaking up any lumps, until meat has lost its raw look.  Drain off any fat.
3.  Add another 2 T. butter, 1/4 lb. finely chopped mushrooms, 1/2 C. chopped green pepper, 1 stalk chopped celery, salt and pepper to taste.
4.  Combine 1 egg, 1 egg yolk, 1 C. heavy cream and 1/4 C. finely chopped chives or scallions.  Beat thoroughly, then add the meat mixture and stir to blend.
5.  Roll out the refrigerated pastry dough into a 9-in. round and place in a tart pan.  Fill with the  meat mixture and bake 30-35 min. until the custard is set.

name: Stuffed Pork Chops
instructions:
1.  Have a butcher cut a deep pocket into each of 6 bone-in porkchops cut 1-in. thick.
2.  Pre-heat oven to 350°F.  Melt 2 T. butter over medium heat in a saut
pan.  Add 1 finely chopped medium onion and 1 large clove of minced garlic and cook until softened.  Scrape into a bowl and combine with 3.5 C. soft rye bread crumbs, 1/2 t. carraway seeds, 3 T. chopped parsley, 3/4 t. salt, 1/4 t. pepper and 1 egg.  Mix well.
Fill chops with stuffing, sealing pockets with toothpicks.  Season on all sides with salt and pepper, then arrange in a shallow baking dish with a fitted lid.  Bake covered 40-45 minutes (or till meat is 155

name: F).  Brown under broiler and place on a platter.
instructions:
Reduce the juices in the baking dish on the stove over medium heat to a deep glaze.   Add 1 T. butter and stir in 1 T. flour, then cook for 1 minute.  Add 3/4 C. chicken broth and simmer, scraping up the pan drippings, unti lthick enough to coat the back of a spoon.  Strain, season to taste and serve with pork chops.
Roast Veal
Sprinkle a 2.5 lb veal roast with salt and pepper to taste.  Brown roast in  3 T. butter (~15 min total).
Place on a roasting rack and roast in an 425

name: F oven for 30 min.
instructions: Add 1/2 C. water to the roasting pan and cook another 10 min.  Remove roast and allow to stand 15 min.

name: Aubergine stew (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  THE DAY BEFORE SERVING, combine 5 lb. stewing beef (cut into large chunks), 4 peeled and sliced carrots, 3 coarsely chopped yellow onions, 2 cloves of garlic, 2 sprigs flat-leaf parsley, 1 stalk sliced celery, 3 bay leaves, 1 T. fresh thyme (or 1 t. dried), 1/4 C. Cognac, 4 C. red wine and 1 T. olive oil in a bowl. Tie 3 whole cloves and 1 t. peppercorns in cheesecloth and add to the mix. Toss well.and refrigerate overnight.
2.  Let mixture return to room temperature.  Remove meat and pat dry, then remove the vegetables and set them aside.  Transfer liquid to a large pan, add 2 C. beef broth and bring to a boil.
3.  In a large oven-proof casserole, heat 3 T. butter and 1/4 C. olive oil.  Dust the meat with flour seasoned with salt and pepper and, working in batches, on all sides. Set meat to one side.
4.  Add the vegetables to the casserole and sauté until brown (~ 7 min), then remove.  Add 4 C. fresh, quartered mushrooms (porcini, shiitake, c
until lightly browned (~5 min).
5.  Return the meat and vegetables to the caserole, stir in 1 T. tomato paste and cook briefly to combine flavors.  Add the liquid and simmer for 4 hr.
6.  Add salt and pepper to taste, the mushrooms,  and the juice and zest from an orange.  Serve with potatoes, pasta or rice.

name: Venison Chili
instructions:
1.  Warm 1 T. olive oil in a heavy casserole over medium heat.  Add 2 minced cloves of garlic, 1 large onion (minced), 1 green bell pepper (stemmed, seeded and minced), and 2 t. fresh minced chili pepper.  Cook until soft, 5-6 min.  Stir in 3/4 lb. ground venison and 1.5 lb. cubed venison stewing meat and cook until browned, stirring.
2.  Stir in 3 C. crushed tomatoes, 3 T. red-wine vinegar, 2 T. cumin, 2.5 T. chili powder, 3 t. salt, 1 t. ground black pepper, 2 T.  Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 t. cayenne, and simmer 30 min.
3.  Stir in 11 oz. canned kidney beans (drained) 3 T. cornmeal (made into a paste with 1 t. water) and simmer 5 min.  Serve in large bowls.

name: Brisket
instructions:
1.  Combine 1/2 C. vegetable oil, 1 can beer, 1 clove minced garlic, 2 T. lemon juice, 2 T. sugar, 1 t. salt and 3 whole cloves.  Marinate a 5-6 lb. brisket overnight in this.
2.  Discard marinade and rub 1 packet of dried onion soup mix on meat.  Wrap tightly in foil and bake 4-5 hr. at 275

name: Meat Loaf (serves 6)
instructions:
1.  Pre-heat oven to 350
2 cloves garlic (finely chopped) in 2 T. olive oil until browned and 1/2 green pepper (finely chopped) &  1 medium onion (chopped fine) until translucent.  Remove to a medium bowl and season with salt and pepper.
2.   Saut
1 large portobello mushrooms (chopped fine) in 2 T. olive until the water is released (~ 2 min.).  Remove to bowl and season.
3.  Combine and  mix well (but don
t overmix) in a large bowl: 1 lb. ground beef, 1/2 lb. pork sausage, 1/2 C. bread crumbs, 1/2 t. onion powder, 1 t. Dijon mustard, 1/2 t. Cajun seasoning, 1/3 C. grated Parmesan cheese, 4 T. ketchup, 1 T. mayonaisse and 2 T. whipped cream cheese.
4.  Mix in 1 large egg and the contents of the first bowl.  Form into a loaf and bake 1 hr.  After the first 30 min.  saut
1/2 lb. bacon till half-cooked and place over the loaf.

name: Pot Roast (serves six)
instructions:
Mix 1/4 C. flour, 1.67 T. kosher salt, 1.25 t. pepper and rub on a 4 lb. beef chuck roast.  Brown befe over high heat in 4 T. olive oil in a dutch oven.  Add 1 C. water, cover and simmer 3 hr.
Add 8 small, new potatoes (halved), 8 small onions and 8 cut-up carrots, then cook another hour.

name: Sauerbraten (serves six)
instructions:
The day before: Place 3.5-4-lb. rump roast in a large ziplock bag and marinate with 5 C. cider vinegar, 5 C .water, 3 sliced onions, 1 sliced lemon, 12 whole cloves, 6 bay leaves, 6 whole peppercorns and 2 T. salt.  Place in refrigerator for at least 36 hr.
The next day, brown meat in 2 T. olive oil and transfer to a roasting pan.  Add 1 T. of the marinade and cook at 325°F for 2.5 hr.
Once the meat is done, remove to a serving platter and return the roasting pan to the stovetop.  Combine 2 T. flour with a little water in a cup, then add to pan and scrape up the browned bits.  Add the remaining marinade and 2 buillion cubes, and cook with stirring until thickened.  Serve over meat with sp

name: Fricassee of Lamb with Artichokes
instructions:
1.  Brown two whole cloves of garlic in 4 T. olive oil until golden.  Discard garlic and saut
2 lbs. of cubed lamb (in batches) until well-browned.  Salt/pepper to taste.
2.  Add 1/4 C. fresh minced parsley and 2 minced cloves of garlic to the pan and stir continuously for 2 min.  Pour over the meat.
3.  Add 3/4 C. meat broth to the pan and scrape up the browned particles.  Add 9 oz. of (thawed) frozen artichoke hearts, cover and simmer for 5 min.  Return the meat to the pan and simmer another 10-15 min, or until tender.
4.  Remove meat and artichoke hearts from pan.  Add juice from one lemon w/ 2 egg yolks in a small bowl.  Add mixture to pan slowly, stirring continuously until slightly thickened.  Remove from heat, stir in the lamb and artichokes.
5.  Serve with parsley sprigs and lemon slices.

name: Charcoaled Loin of Lamb
instructions:
1.  Combine 1/2 C. pomegranate paste, 1/4 C. lime juice and 1/2 C. olive oil.  Place in a ceramic dish, add 2 trimmed lamb loins along with 2 T. cracked caradamom, 1/4 C. cracked coraiander seeds, 1/4 C. cumin seeds, 1 small sliced onion, 10 sliced cloves of garlic, 1 C. chopped cilantro leaves,  and three minced habanero chiles.  Refrigerate 8 hrs., turning once.
2.  Toast 2 t. coriander seeds and 1 t. cumin seeds in a dry skillet.  Allow to cool and grind into a powder.
3.  Combine the ground seeds in a small bowl with 1 t. curry powder, 1/4 C. olive oil, 2 T. lemon juice, 1/2 t. salt and 1 t. ground black pepper.  Set aside at least 4 hr.
4.  Remove lamb from marinade, dust both sides with 1/4 C. cracked peppercorns and salt lightly.  Grill 6-8 min on each side, then allow to cool.
5.  Whisk together 1 T. red wine vinegar and 2 T. olive oil.  Add 1 bunch of arugula and 1/2 C. shaved Romano cheese.  Toss well.
6.  Cut loins lengthwise into thin strips, sprinkle with curry oil and serve with the arugula salad.

name: Lamb Shanks
instructions:
1.  Boil one lamb shank per person for 1/2 hr.  Drain and sear in butter or olive oil.
2.  Add 2 cloves garlic, 1 t. thyme to 1 C. water.  Add the lamb shanks and simmer 1 hr. or more, adding water to keep moist.
3.  Wash 1.5 C. quick-cooking lentils, place in a saucepan and cover with 1 inch of water.  Add an onion stuck with 2 cloves, one bay leaf, and one clove of chopped garlic and 1 t. salt.  Simmer until tender but not mushy.
4.  Place lamb shanks and lentils in a casserole, cover and bake 1/2 hr. at 350°F, adding liquid as necessary.

name: Rosemary and Garlic Lamb Chops
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 375F.  Place 2 large potatoes (cut into 1/2-in. pieces) in a roasting pan and toss with 1 T. olive oil.  Roast 40 min.
2.  Combine 2 cloves minced garlic, 4 sprigs of rosemary, 1 T. olive oil and salt/pepper to taste.  Rub over a rack of lamb (scraped of all fat). and let stand for 30 min.
3.  Brown 1 can of artichoke hearts in 1 T. olive oil in a heavy skillet.  Remove from heat and add the roasted potatoes, 1 C. raw artichoke hearts (very thinly sliced), 1 C. cooked fava beans and 1 large tomato (cored, seeded and diced).  Toss well. and season with salt and pepper to taste.
4.  Increase oven temperature to 400°F and roast the lamb for 30 min.  Let stand 10 min.
5.  Whisk together  2 T. red wine vinegar with salt and pepper to taste and 6 T. olive oil.  Add to the salad and toss to coat.  Serve with the (carved) rack of lamb.

name: Lamb Chops with Artichoke Mint Salad
instructions:
1.  Thinly slice 2 C. (defrosted) frozen artichoke hearts and toss with 2 T. lemon juice, 3 T. minced fresh mint, 3 T. minced fresh parsley, salt and pepper to taste.  Set aside.
2.  Heat a heavy skillet over medium-high heat until very hot.  Sauté four lamb chops ~4 min on a side.  Top with salad and serve.

name: Greek Lamb (serves four to six)
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.  Separate 1 head of garlic into cloves (unpeeled), toss in 1 T. olive oil, wrap in foil and roast for 20 min.  Remove and allow to cool.
2.  Rub a 2.5-3 lb. shoulder of lamb (tied) with 1/4 C. olive oil, then with kosher salt and pepper and place on a roasting rack.  Make small slits in the surface of the lamb and insert the cloves of garlic (peeled).  Place sprigs of fresh thyme under the string tying the lamb.  Roast for 20 min, then reduce the temperature to 350°F, cover with foil and roast another 1 hr.  Turn off the oven and allow the lamb to rest for 15 min.
3.  Remove the lamb to a serving platter.  Place the roasting pan on over medium-high heat on the stove and sprinkle 1 t. flour over the drippings, stirring well to scrape up all the browned bits.  Add 1 C. red wine and cook down.  Add 4 T. beef broth and cook another 30 seconds or so.
4.  Remove the string and thyme from the lamb.  Slice and serve with the sauce.

name: Leg of Lamb with Rosemary and Garlic (serves 12)
instructions:
1.  Break an entire head of garlic into individual cloves.  Simmer unpeeled in 1 C. chicken stock for 30 min.
2. Drain, peel the garlic and puré e in a food processor with 1 t. dried rosemary.  Spread the pur
e inside an 8-lb. leg of lamb (boned and butterflied) and sew up the lamb.  Rub meat with 1/4 C. olive oil, salt and pepper; allow to stand for 2 hr.  at rm. T.
3. Preheat oven to 450°F.  Roast the lamb for 1 hr., turning once (surround with vegetable for roasting
potatoes, carrots, eggplant, onions, etc.).  Serve sliced on a bed of curly kale and surrounded with the vegetables.

name: Mushroom-stuffed Leg of Lamb (serves 12)
instructions:
1.  Cook 10 medium Swiss chard leaves in boiling, salted water until tender (~ 5 min.).  Drain, dry well and chop enough fro 1.5 C.  Place in a large bowl.
2.  Melt 1 T .butter and 2 T. extra-virgin olive oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.  Add 1.25 lb. assorted wild mushrooms (portobello, shiitake, crimini), 1/2 C. finely chopped onion and 1 minced clove of garlic.  Sauté until mushrooms are tender (~6 min.).  Allow to cool.
3.  Add mushroom mixture to chard.  Add 1 C. fresh breadcrumbs, 2 T. cream, 1 T. each rosemary and thyme, 1 t. each kosher salt and fresh ground pepper.  Blend well.
4.  Pre-heat oven to 425°F.  Open up 6-7 lb. leg of lamb (butterflied to 2in.) like a book and place cut side up on working surface.  Sprinkle with kosher salt and pepper.
5.  Add 4 oz. ground veal (or lamb) to the mushroom mixture along with 1 large egg., and mix well.  Press onto the lamb and, starting at the narrow end, roll the lamb up tightly, enclosing the stuffing.  Secure with poultry skewers.  Rub the outside with 2 T. extra virgin olive oil, then 1 t. each thyme and rosemary and salt and pepper to taste.  Tie lamb up with kitchen string and, using a small sharp knife, cut several 1-in. deep slits.  Slice 3 cloves of garlic and place in slits.  Cook until meat thermometer reads ~140°F, about 1.5 hr.  Remove skewers and string, carve into 1/2-in. slices and serve on a large platter with pissaladi

name: Lamb Breasts (serves 6)
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 450
1 C.chopped onion and 1 minced garlic.  When golden brown, add 1/4 lb. chopped mushrooms and 1.2 T. thyme and cook another 5 min.
2.  Mix in 1 lb. ground lamb and 1/4 C. chopped parsley with 1 egg.  Season with kosher salt and pepper to taste, then stuff into 2 firm lamb breasts.  Sew up the pocket with cooking thread, sprinkle with salt and pepper, brush with olive oil and bake 15 min.  Reduce heat to 400°F and bake another 15 min.
3.  Pour off the fat, baste with butter and sprinkle with parsley, then return to oven another 5 min.

name: Rabbit in Wine
instructions:
1.  Combine 2 C. red wine, 2 C. water, 1 bay leaf, 1/8 t. dried sage, 1/4 t. ground black pepper, 1/8 t. dried thyme, 1 small onion (chopped), 1 crushed clove garlic, 1 stalk celery (chopped coarsely) and 1/8 t. salt.  Simmer for 10 min.  Allow to cool.
2.  Strain and use to marinade a 3-lb. rabbit (quartered) for 24 hr. in the refrigerator.
3.  Combine 1 C. flour with salt and pepper to taste and dredge rabbit.  Sauté rabbit in 1/4 C. vegetable oil (or grease from four strips of bacon) till brown (10 min per side).
4.  Add 1 coarsely chopped onion, 2 T. chopped parsley, 1 chopped tomato, 1 minced clove garlic, 1 bay leaf, 1/2 chopped green pepper, 1 C. white wine and 1 C. chicken broth.  Cover and simmer for 1.5 hr.

name: Rabbit and Fennel
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 375°F.  Place 4 medium fennel bulbs (thinly sliced) in a large roasting pan and toss in  2 t. salt and ground pepper to taste.  Roast for 10 min.
2.  Rub two 2.5 lb. rabbits (cut into six pieces) with a mixture ot 2 t. salt, 2 T. cracked black pepper, 3 T. fennel seeds and 1 t. olive oil.  Place the rabbit over the fennel and roast for 30 min.; turn and roast another 10 min.  Serve immediately.

name: Turkey with herb rub
instructions:
1.  Mix 3 T. each of freshly chopped thyme, rosemary and tarragon (or 1.5 t. each dried), 1 t. ground pepper, and 2 t. salt.
2.  Pat turkey dry with paper towels and place on a roasting rack and stuff (put neck etc. on pan separately).  Place 4 C. canned chicken broth in bottom of pan.  Baste with 2 T. vegetable oil and rub thoroughly with herb mix.   Pre-heat oven to 425°F and cook bird for 45 min, then lower temperature to 325
F and cook bird for additional time according to the following table (basting occassionally with melted butter):
10-18 b.	3  - 3.5 hr		18-22 lb.	3.5 - 4 hr.
22-24 lb.	4 - 5 hr.		24-29 lb.	5 - 6 hr.

name: Labor-Intensive Roast Turkey
instructions:
The day before:
1.  Dissolve 2 C. kosher salt in 3 gallons cold water.  Submerge the turkey (having removed the internal organs) in the brine and refrigerate at least 12 hours.
2.  Remove the turkey and rinse well.  Pat dry and refrigerate ~ 7 hr. to dry the skin.
The day of cooking
3.  Deeply score the neck, giblets and removed wing-tips, and place in a medium pot.  Add a coarsely chopped carrot, stalk of celery and onion, and cover with 2 C. water.  Simmer 3 hr., adding more water as necessary to keep the parts covered.
4.  Remove the turkey from the refrigerator 1 hr. before cooking.  Heat oven to 450 °F.  Place a V-rack in a roasting pan and add a few T. of oil to the pan. Stuff the turkey (but do not truss), then rub one stick of softened butter into the skin.
5.  Place the turkey ON ITS SIDE on the rack and cook according to the following:         12 lb.
2 hr.; 16 lb., 2.5 hr.; 20 lb.
3 hr.  If too much smoke accumulates, reduc e the heat to 400, or even 375 °F.
6.  After 30 min., remove the turkey from the oven, baste the turkey with the pan drippings and turn it on its other side.  Return to the oven and baste every 20 min.
7.  At the end of the cooking time, remove the turkey from the oven and baste all over one last time.  Allow to stand 20 min. before carving.
8.  Deglaze the pan with 1 C. of the turkey stock, scraping up everything.  Taste that it is not too strong or salty, and dilute with more stock and/or water if necessary.  Add 1/2 C. Cognac, dry white wine or dry sherry and boil off the alcohol.  This makes a thin sauce for the turkey (not a gravy for the potatoes!).

name: Pheasant and Cabbage
instructions:
1.  In a deep casserole, saut
2 large chopped onions in 1 T. butter and 3 T. vegetable oil until golden.  Add one shredded white cabbage; cook 10 min uncovered, then 20 min with lid ajar, stirring occasionally.  Remove from heat, salt/pepper to taste.
2.  Preheat oven to 350°F.  Wash 1 medium pheasant in cold water and dry well.  Season cavity with salt and pepper and brush the inside with Dijon mustard.  Stud a whole onion with 6-10 cloves and place it inside the cavity.  Tie legs and turn wingtips under and dredge lightly in flour.
3.  Brown the pheasant in 1.5 T. vegetable oil over medium heat (6-8 min to the side).  Transfer to the casserole, breast up, cover and cook for 40 min, basting regularly with the pan juices.  Bake uncovered another 10 min.
4.  Remove bird to a large, warm platter to cool.  Untie and discard the onion; drain any juices back into the caserole.  Place cabbage and drippings over high heat and carefully add 1/2 C. bourbon; stir for 2-3 min.  Lower heat, add 1/2 C. heavy cream, cook for 3-5 min.  When pheasant is rested, carve and serve over cabbage.

name: Sage-Roasted Chicken
instructions:
Fry 1/4 lb. cubed pancetta in a skillet until it begins to render fat.  Add 1 minced onion and sauté until soft.  Add 3 C. sliced white mushrooms and saute until brown
Add 1 C. chicken livers and sauté until brown.  Add 3/4 C. hearty red wine and 2 T. balsamic vinegar, then simmer for 2 min.  Set aside.
Preheat oven to 400 °F.  Place a sage leaf under each of four pieces of chicken and add salt/pepper to taste.  Roast fro 20 min. or until skin has browned nicely.
Cook 1/2 lb. penne in salted water until al dente.
Remove the chicken and tent with foil to keep warm.  Pour off fat from orasting pan, place on oven and pour in mushroom mixture.  Bring to a simmer, scraping the bottom of the pan, and salt/pepper to taste.
Drain pasta and return to pot.  Pour in sauce over medium heat and toss well to coat.  Serve with chicken.
Roast Quail with Prosciutto (serves eight)
1.  Preheat oven to 450°F.  Defrost 8 quail (if frozen), then sprinkle inside and out with salt and pepper.  Insert one strip of prosciutto and 1/2 t. chopped rosemary into the cavity of each bird (4 t. rosemary total).  Truss with string, if preferred.
2.  Heat 3 T. vegetable oil and 1 T. butter.  Add the quails, turn to coat with the oil and bake 10 min, basting occasionally. Turn the quail to cook on the other side and bake another 10 min, again basting occasionally, then on their backs - AGAIN basting occasionally.  Remove the quails and untie, if trussed.
3.  Pour off the fat and place the pan in the stove.  Add 1/2 C. water and scrape to dissolve the drippings.  Serve each quail with a bit of the pan drippings.

name: Anguillan roast chicken (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Puré e 1 bunch of scallions, 1 shallot, 4 cloves of garlic, 1 small onion, 1.5 scotch bonnet chiles, 2 t. kosher salt, 2 t. fresh thyme and 1/3 C. olive oil.  Mix in 2 bay leaves, 1/4 C. rum and 1/2 t. pepper.
2.  Pour marinade into a freezer bag and add four chicken breasts.  Refrigerate overnight.
3.  Saut
1 minced onion, 1 clove of minced garlic and 1/2 scotch bonnet chile in 4 t. olive oil until just brown.  Add 1.5 lb. peeled, seeded tomatoes (chopped) and cook about 5 min with frequent stirring.
4.  Add 1/8 C. water, 1/8 C. tomato paste, 1/8 C. commercial barbecue sauce, 1 T. anguillan or other Caribbean hot sauce, 1/8 C. cider vinegar, 1/8 C. brown sugar, 1/8 C. dark rum, 1/2 t. oregano, 1/2 t. thyme, kosher salt and pepper to taste.
5.  Barbecue four chicken breasts over wood chips (soaked maple, mesquite, apple or cherry) 20 min per side.  Baste with the barbecue sauce (#4 above) after turning.

name: Chile and Coriander Chicken (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Toast 1.5 T. coriander seeds, 2 t. peppercorns and 1 t. cumin seeds in a dry skillet.  Grind to a fine powder.  Add to a blender and puré e with 6 cloves of garlic, a 2 in. piece of peeled, thinly sliced Ginger, 3 T. vegetable oil, 1/4 C. water, 1 T. cayenne pepper (or hot paprika), 2 T. lemon juice and 1.5 T. kosher salt.  Transfer paster to a bowl and add 1/2 C. chopped coriander.
2.  Marinate four chicken legs with the above for 4-6 hr. in the refrigerator, then grill.  Serve with sliced onions, lemon, lime and coriander sauce (below).
3.  Puré e 1 C. fresh coriander, 3 cloves of garlic, 1 jalape
o, 1/2 C. shelled walnuts, 1/3 C. fresh lemon juice, 1 t. kosher salt, 1/2 t. pepper, 1/4 t. cumin.  Add 1/4 C. water and add salt and lemon juice to taste.   Pour into a small serving dish and serve.
Roast chicken with Proven
al herbs (serves four)
1.  Preheat oven to 450°F.  Combine 6 T. unsalted butter, 1 T. chopped fresh rosemary, 1 T. chopped fresh thyme and 1 t. lemon zest.  Stuff the well-mixed butter mixture between the skin and meat of 3 lb boneless chicken breasts and sear 5 min in 2 T. olive oil over medium-high heat.  Turn the chicken and cook another 2 min.  Place skillet in the oven and cook another 25 min.
2.  Saut
1 diced white onion and 2 peeled and diced baking potatoes in 2 T. olive oil for 2 min.  Add 2 diced Japanese eggplants and sauté another 6 min., then add 2 diced tomatoes and 4 cloves minced garlic, then saut
another 2 min.  Finally, add 2 T. chopped Italian parsley, season with salt and pepper to taste, and divide into four portions.  Top with a piece of the chicken and serve.

name: Red Chile Chicken with potatoes and mushrooms (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Roast 8 cloves of garlic over high heat in a heavy roasting pan until soft (~15 min).  Cool and peel..  Open and flatten 4 medium dried anchos and 8 guajillo chiles (peeled and deseeded).  Toast two at a time on both sides in a dry skillet until they begin to smoke.  Place in  a bowl of water and keep submerged for 30 min.  Drain.
2.  Place the garlic, chiles, 1.5 t. oregano, 1/8 t. cumin and 2/3 C. chicken broth in a blender and blend to a puré e.  Press through a edium sieve and set aside.
3.  Brown a medium chicken (quartered) dry and brown in 1.5 T. olive oil over medium-high heat., 4-5 min to a side, in a large skillet.
4.  Slice a medium white onion and sauté all but two slices until brown, about 8 min.  Add the chile pur
e and cook, stirring constantly, until thick and dark (about 5 min).  Stir in 2 C. more of chicken broth and simmer 15 min more.
5.  Stir in 1 lb quartered red-skin potatoes and 2 C. thinly sliced shiitake mushrooms, cover and simmer for 10 min.  Push the chicken legs into the sauce and cook partly covered for 15 min.  Turn the chicken and cook partly covered another 30 min.
6.  Transfer chicken to a large serving dish.  Season with salt, spoon sauce over chicken and arrange vegetables.  Garnish chicken with remaining onion rings and serve.

name: Cholent Brisket (serves 10)
instructions:
1.  Pre-heat oven to 225°F.  Heat 1/2 C. vegetable oil in a Dutch oven with a tight-fitting lid over high heat.  Brown a 4-lb. brisket on both sides, then transfer to a platter.
2.  Drain the oil, add another 1/2 C. oil and reduce the heat to medium.  Add 2 medium Spanish onions (thinly sliced) and 1 T. paprika, and cook with occasional stirring for 6 min.  Add 6 cloves of garlic (coarsely chopped) and cook until fragrant (about 30 sec).  Stir in 2 T. honey and remove from the heat.
3.  Place the brisket on the onion/garlic mixture and surround with 2 lb. red Bliss potatoes (quartered), 3 medium carrots (peeled and cut into 1-in. thick slices), 1 C. dried Jacob
s cattle beans (or cranberry beans), 1 C. dried green lentils (soaked in water for an hour, then drained) and 1 C. barley.  Place 1 lb. cut marrowbones on top.  Cover with cold water by 4 inches, then stir in 1 t. each salt and pepper.
4.  Bring the stew to a boil on the stove, skimming off and scum that forms on the surface.  Cover, place in the oven and cook 8 hr. or overnight.
5.  To serve, moisten with water as necessary and reheat if necessary.  Transfer to a large platter and serve.

name: Deb's Chicken and Wine
instructions:
1.  Shake 4 boneless chicken breasts in 1 C. flour with 1 t. salt and 1/4 t. pepper.
2.  Brown in butter and remove to casserole.
3. Saut
1/2 C. mushrooms with 1 bunch chopped green onions in 4 T. butter.
4.  Add 1 C. dry white wine, a dash of Worchester sauce, 2 T. parsley, a pinch each of thyme and marjoram and one bay leaf.  Simmer together for 2 min then pour over the chicken in casserle.  Cover and bake 1 hr .at 325

name: Chicken Paillards  (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Split 4 whole chicken breasts (~ 3 lb.) to make 8 pieces total.  Slice each piece horizontally to make paillards.  Place between wax paper and pound lighlty with a mallet to give an even thickness (no more than 1/4 in.).  Place in a zip-lock bag.
2.  Whisk together 1/2 C. olive oil, 1 T. lemon juice, 1 T. chopped rosemary,1 t. kosher salt and pepper to taste.  Add to the chicken and refrigerate 1-2 hr.
3.  Cook the chicken over high heat in a non-stick skillet until nicely browned (1-2 min. per side).  Transfer to a serving platter and repeat until all the chicken is cooked.
4.  Thinly slice 2 red bell peppers and 20 dried apricots.  Add 1/2 C. chopped black olives and salt.  Mix well and serve on top of the chicken.

name: Chickens under a brick (serves 6)
instructions:
1.  Rinse six chickens (3-4 weeks old, 1.5-2 lbs each) inside and out, and pat dry with a paper towel.  Buttterfly the chickens by removing their spines, flattening the birds with your hand and removing the breast bone with a sharp knife or your fingers.  Firmly but gently pound them flat with something heavy.  Cut off the wingtips and fold the wings back.  Tuck the legs into the skin surrounding the vent by making small slit where the leg can easily be inserted.
2.  Heat a stove-top grill large enough to cover two burners over high heat.  Lightly baste the chickens lightly with vegetable oil and season with salt and pepper to taste.  Reduce the heat to medium and lay the chickens skin side down, two at a time, on the grill.  Move the chickens gently with tongs until the skin begins to sear and not stick.  Cover with a heavy, flat-bottomed object (like foil-covered bricks or a skillet filled with water) lage enough to cover the entire chicken.  Brown (5-6 min.), then turn and cook another 5-6 min on the other side.  The color should be a rich mahogany.  Keep warm in a 250

name: Chicken with Rice and Chili-Ginger Sauce (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Combine five fresh red Thai chiles, three cloves of garlic, five pieces of fresh, peeled ginger, 2 T. rice-wine vinegar, 2 T. vegetable oil, 4 t. sugar, 3/4 T. salt, 2 T. chicken stock, and blend (pulsing) until smooth but not puré ed.
2.  Rub a 3-4 lb whole chicken with salt, stuff with five scallions and two slices of peeled ginger.  Place in a kettle, add 8 C. chicken stock and bring to a boil, uncovered for 40 to 45 min (skim off any foam that accumulates).  Remove.
3.  Transfer chicken to platter and allow to cool slightly.  Discard stuffing and skin.  Carve bird and discard carcass.  Cover and keep warm.
4.  Heat 1 T. sesame oil and 1 T. vegetable oil in a large saucepan, when hot add three cloves of thinly cliced garlic and three slices of peeled ginger and cook two min.  Add 2 C. Thai fragrant rice and stir until coated, then add 4 C. of the warm chicken broth, bring to a boil and simmer until water is absorbed (about 15 min).
5.  Transfer rice to serving platter, garnish with sliced garlic and scallions.  Top with the chicken and serve with the sauce on the side.

name: Mango Chutney Chicken (serves four)
instructions: 1.  Preheat oven to 450°F.  Lay four boneless, skinless chicken breasts flat in a glass roasting dish.  In a small bowl, whisk 1 C. mayonaisse and 1 C. sour cream.  Add 4 T. mango chutney and 2 t. curry poowder and stir well, then add the juice from 1 lemon with stirring.  Spread on the chicken and bake 15-20 min. until just done.

name: Cioppino (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Saut
2 C. chopped onion in 1/3 C. olive oil until golden.  Add 1 green pepper (sliced into thin strips), 4 minced cloves of garlic, and cook until pepper is softened.  Add 3 C. canned chopped tomatoes, 4 C. white fish stock, 1 C. chopped fresh parsley, 1/2 C. dry white wine, 1 can tomato paste, 1 t. thyme, 1 t. basil, 1 bay leaf, 1/2 t. oregano, 1/2 t. red pepper flakes and kosher salt to taste.  Simmer covered ~45 min.
2.  Bring soup to a boil and add 1 lb. firm-fleshed fish (striped bass or red snapper) cut into 1.5-in. strips, 1/2 lb. sea scallops, and 1 lb. shelled (raw) shrimp.  Return to a boil and add 6 clams and 6 scrubbed mussels.  Simmer until shellfish have opened and the fish just flakes.  Discard bay leaf and any unopened shellfish.

name: Scallops with Broccoli Rabe
instructions:
Peel the fibrous stems of 1.25 lb. broccoli rabe and cut into 1.5-2 in. pieces.
Heat 3 T. olive oil, then add 1 t. finely chopped garlic and 1/4 t. red pepper flakes and stir for just a few seconds.  Add the broccoli, 1/3 C. water and 1/2 t. salt, cover and simmer for 5 min.
Remove the small sinew from 30 small scallops.  Rinse and mix with 1 t. olive oil, 1/8 t. salt, 1/4 t. dried thyme and 1/4 t. fresh ground pepper.
At serving time, heat a skillet until very hot, add scallops in a single layer and cook over high heat for 2 min, stirring occasionally.  Remove from heat and allow to stand, covered, for another min.
Mix 2 T. balsamic vinegar, 4 T. olive oil, 1/2 t. salt & 1/4 t. fresh ground black pepper.  Arrange broccoli rape on plates, cover with scallops and sauce; serve.

name: Quenelles (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Place 1.5 lb. seafood (pike, sole, shrimp or lobster) in a food processor and pulse with the chopper 3 or four times.  Remove to a chilled bowl in an ice bath and work with a wooden spoon until is a paste.  Gradually work in 2 eggwhites and season with nutmeg, salt, white pepper, cayenne and cognac to taste.
2.  Fold in 2 C. very cold whipping cream.  Form into balls with a pair of spoons, place in a buttered saucepan and gently pour in 2 C. chicken stock (enough to half cover the quenelles).  Bring to a simmer and cook 8-10 min.  They should eventually turn over on their own, but may need some help with a spatula after ~5 min.
3.  Remove, drain and serve with a

name: Fish Cakes with Tartar Sauce
instructions:
Preheat oven to 300°F, and sprinkle a baking pan with olive oil and salt.  Lay 1 lb. of striped bass (or other fish cutlets) on pan skin-side down.  Bake until just cooked (20-30 min), and allow to cool.
Skin and bone the fish and break into small pieces in a small mixing bowl.    Add 2 T. fresh parsley (chopped) and 1 C. cup mashed potatoes, mixing gently.  Whisk together 1 egg, 1/4 C. mayonaise and 1 T. Dijon mustard.  Lift and fold the mixture into the fish (but don
t over-mix).
Pat the fish into 8 3-in. cakes and coat with dried breadcrumbs.  Sauté in 4 T. butter/oil mixture over medium heat until well-browned (2-3 min. on a side).
For the tartar sauce, mix together 1/3 C. mayonaise, 1/4 C chopped sweet pickle, 2 T. finely chopped red onion, 1 T. chopped parsley, 1/2 t. Worcestershire sauce and 1.5 T. lemon juice (plus a drop or two of hot sauce, if desired).  Serve with lemon.

name: Scallop/Halibut Ceviche Salad
instructions:
Place 1.25 lb. Fresh sea scallops (sliced in half horizontally, then quartered) in a non-reactive bowl and season with generously with salt.  Add 3/4 C. lime juice, toss, cover and refrigerate for ~1 hr.  Do the same with 1.25 lb. fresh, skinless halibut fillet, cut into 1/2-in. cubes.
Drain the halibut, discarding the lime juice, then do the same with the scallops.  Return to refrigerator.
Combine 4-6 serrano or jalape
o peppers (halved, seeded and finely chopped), 2 shallots (finely chopped), 1/3 C. cilantro leaves (finely chopped) and 1/2 C. lime juice.  Refrigerate.
15 min. before serving, whisk 1/2 C. olive oil into the shallot mixture and pour over the scallps and halibut.  Toss each well, and refrigerate another 15 min.
Line each of eight plates with a leaf of Boston lettuce.  Top with a layer of peeled grapefruit (two needed) and sliced avocado (two needed).  Place a large spoonful each of the halibut and scallops on top, sprinkle with chopped cilantro and serve.

name: Paella a la Disney
instructions:
1.  Remove the backbone and sternum from one cornish game hen, then cut into eighths and season with salt and pepper.  Brown in a large skilet in olive oil.
2.  Combine 1. basmati rice, 1 C. each of fish and poultry stock, 1 C. diced fish and scallops, 8 large shrimp (peeled and deveined), 2 lobster tails (split lengthwise), 8 mussels (scrubbed, beards removed), 8 scrubbed littleneck clams and 1/2 C. green peas in a baking dish.  Arrange the Cornish game hen on top and bake uncovered 60-75 min at 350°F until rice is cooked.
3.  Remove and allow to set of 10 min.  Garnish with another 1/2 C.peas and 1/2 C. sliced pimiento.

name: Salmon Mousse (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 375°F.  Skin and bone 1.5 lb. salmon fillet.  Cut into cubes, place in the bin of a food processor and refrigerate 30 min or longer.
2.  Simmer the fish trimmings in 1 C. water and 1/2 C. dry white wine, 1/2 bay leaf, 1/2 t. thyme, 1/4 C. chopped onion and 2 T. minced shallots for 1 hour.  Strain and cook down to 1/2 C.
2.  Saut
2 minced shallots in 2 T. butter until soft.  Add 1/4 lb. thinly sliced mushrooms and cook another 10 min, until there is no more liquid.
3.  Plunge one large tomato into boiling water for 2 min.  Remove to a paper sack and allow to cool.  Peel, seed and dice, then add to the sauce with the fish broth.  Add salt and pepper to taste, then simmer 5 min.  Add 1/2 C. cream to the sauce mix, bring to a boil and swirl in 2 T. butter and 2 T dill (optional).  Keep warm.
4.  Salt and pepper the salmon, along with 1/8 t. nutmeg and one egg.  Blend in a food processor until smooth, the slowly add 2 C. cream with constant mixing.  Spoon into a 6 C. mold (greased with butter) and cover with a round of wax paper.  Place in a water-filled basin, bring to a boil and bake for 30 min.  Serve with the sauce.

name: Tomato-based seafood stew (serves six).
instructions:
1.  Working in batches, puré e 15 medium tomatoes (quartered) in a food processor.  Strain through a fine sieve and set aside.
2.  Saut
2 coarsely chopped cloves of garlic, 4 chopped shallots, 4 stalks of lemongrass (halved in 2-inch segments), and 1 chopped jalape
o in 4 T. olive oil for 5 min.  Add 1/2 C. vermouth and cook another 3 min. with frequent stirring.  Stir in the tomato puré e and bring to a boil.  Reduce heat and simmer for 30 min.  Strain again through a fine sieve, clean the pot and return the liquid to it.  Stir in 1.5 t. lime zest and 2.5 t. salt.
3.  Steam 3 medium lobsters for 10 min.  When cooled, remove the meat and halve the tails.  Set aside.  Steam open 24 small mussels in the lobster water and set aside.
4.  In a large pot of boiling water, steam 3 julienned carrots for 30 seconds.  Add 1 C. julienned snow peas and steam for another 5 seconds.  Drain and set aside.
5.  Bring the tomato mixture to a boil.  Reduce to a simmer and add 3 red snapper fillets (cut into 1-inch piece).  Simmer gently ofr 2 minutes.  Add 24 sea scallops and simmer another minute.
6.  Meanwhile, place 1/3 C. flour, 1 t. salt and pepper to taste in a paper bag.  Toss 6 soft-shelled crabs in the flour, then brown in olive oil (~3 min. total).
7.  Add lobster and mussels to the stew and cook until just heated.  Divide into six portions and serve.

name: Salmon Leek Pie
instructions:
1.  Cut the white and pale green parts of four leeks into 1/2 in. slices; wash well, drain and pat dry.  Sauté in 2 T. butter until tender (~10 min).  Allow to cool.
2.  Cut 2.25 lb. skinless, boneless salmon into 3/4 in. pieces.  Toss with the leeks, 1/4 C. fresh dill, 1 t. lemon zest, 1.5 t. kosher salt, and pepper to taste; mix well.
3.  In a small bowl, whisk together 1 large egg with 1 t. water.  On a lightly floured surface, roll out one puff pastry sheet into a 19 in. square.  Transfer to a floured baking sheet, then mound salmon filling into the center.  Brush edges with some of the egg/water mix, drape the repaining puff pastry over the top and press the edges together.  Trim with a knife, crimp the edges to seal and cut vents into the top.  Brush the top with the remainder of the egg/water mixture.  Chill pie for 1-3 hr.
4.  Pre-heat oven to 400°F and bake pie until golden brown (~ 30 min).
5.  Blister and peel one red bell pepper and one jalape
o.  Seed and chop well, combine with 16 oz. sour cream and blend until very smooth.  Season with salt and pepper to taste, and serve with the pie.

name: Crackling salmon with truffles (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Thinly slice 2 oz. of fresh or canned truffles, setting aside eight of the best slices.
2.  Skin a 2 lb. center-cut salmon fillet and cut crosswise into eight pieces.  Butterfly each cut of salmon with a sharp knife, arrange one layer of truffles in each and replace top.  Sprinkle with sea salt and pepper.
3.  Spread a kitchen towel on a clean work surface and fill a large bowl with warm water.  Soak eight rice papers individually (make sure there are no holes) for a minute or so, until soft.   Transfer the rice paper to the towel.  Place one good truffle slice to the rice paper along with one 4-in. piece of chives.  Place a salmon piece on top, round side down.  Fold the rice paper, first over the long sides then the short sides; trim as necessary.  Transfer to a tray, seam down, and cover with palsic wrap.
4.  Heat 1.5 T. olive oil in a heavy skillet until hot.  Sauté the wrapped salmon (seam side up) in fours over moderately high heat until golden brown (about 3 min).  Turn and reduce heat to moderate
cook salmon until firm to the touch (4-6 min).  Transfer with a spatula to plates, add more oil and cook the remaining salmon.

name: Paella Fried Rice (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Prepare 4 C. long-grain rice the day before
cover and place in the refrigerator overnight.
2.  Cook 1 t .cumin seed and 1/2 t. red pepper flakes in 1/4 C. extra-virgin olive oil over high heat.  Add 1 large chopped onion and 1 chopped, large red pepper and cook with occasional stirring until soft.  Add 2 cut-up chorizo sausages and cook until browned (another 3 min or so).  Add the refrigerated rice and 1.5 t. salt, then cook until golden (another 3 min.)
3.  Add 10 oz .frozen peas and cook until peas are heated through.  Remove from the heat and add 1/3 C. toasted pumpkin seeds and 1 C. chopped cilantro.

name: Grilled Red Snapper with Avocado (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Combine 4 T. lemon juice, 1 T. grated ginger, 1 T. minced garlic and 1 minced shallot.  Simmer over low heat until reduced.
2.  Combine 1 t. honey, 1 t. tamari, 1 t. tahini and 1/2 C. water.  Whisk into the above.
3.  Broil 2 lb. red snapper fillets 3 min. on each side.  Garnish with 2 sliced avocados and 1/4 C. finely chopped coriander, then top with the sauce.

name: Seared Scallops  in  Mushroom White Wine Sauce
instructions:
1.  Spray 1 lb. sea scallops with olive oil and sear in a heavy skillet over medium heat until browned.  Add 4 C. coarsely chopped mushroom caps and sauté until cooked.  Remove with a slotted spoon.
2.  Add 1/2 C. white wine and 1 T. minced thyme leaves, then cook till reduced by half.  Salt/pepper to taste, then return scallops and stir to coat with sauce.

name: Pink Bourride
instructions:
1.  Combine 2 T. dried breadcrumbs with 2 T. white-wine vinegar and allow to stand 5 min.  Combine with 1 t. Dijon mustard, 3 egg yolks, 12 chopped cloves of garlic, salt and white pepper to taste in a food processor and pulse until smooth.  Gradually add 1.5 C. olive oil, pulsing frequently until the mixture is thick.  Add 1 T. lemon juice and 1 T. hot water and pulse again.  refrigerate (his is a
oli).
2.  In a large ceramic stockpot, saut
4 minced shallots in 4 T. butter for 5 min.  Add 6 C. chicken stock, 1 C. dry white wine and bring to a boil.  reduce heat, add 12 shrimp shells and simmer for 10 min.
3.  Strain into a stockpot, add 5 saffron threads and salt/pepper to taste.  Set aside.
4.  Char 2 red bell peppers, then peel and seed. Puré e in a food processor and set aside.
5.  Halve 12 small artichokes lengthwise and discard the chokes.  Rub with a slice of lemon and cook in boiling water until tender (about 12 min).  Remove artichokes and boil 24 new potatoes for 12 min in the saved water.
6.  Bring 2 C. chicken stock with 2 fennel bulbs (halved and cut into thirds) to a boil over high heat.  Cover and simmer 15 min.
7.  Bring the soup to a simmer over medium heat and poach 2 lb whitefish fillets (cod, scrod, haddock) for 1 min.  Add 1 lb. sea scallops and poach for another 1 min.  Add 1 lb. medium peeled shrimp and poach for 2 min.  Remove the fish and shellfish and arrange on a warm platter.
8.  Stir the red pepper puré e into the fish stock and bring to a simmer.  Add 1 C. heavy cream and simmer for 5 min.   Remove from heat and garnish with 1/4 C. fresh chopped parsley and 1/4 C. chopped fresh cilantro.
9.  Place vegetables, fish and shellfish in soup bowls.  Cover with soup and serve with toast and a
1 lb. shelled and deveined shrimp in 2 t. olive oil over medium heat for 1.5 min.  Add 2 T. slivered almonds and 4 cloves minced garlic; continue till shrimp is cooked.
Stir in 1 T. drained capers, 2 cored and coarsely chopped tomatoes and 1 T. minced fresh parsley, then cook another 1 min.  Add Salt and pepper to taste and serve.

name: Scallops in Bourbon Sauce
instructions:
1.  Combine 2.5 C. cornmeal with 1/2 C. grated parmesan cheese in a small bowl.
2.  Lightly beat 3 eggs  with a dash of salt in a medium bowl.  Roll 16 jumbo sea scallops individually in flour, dip in the egg and roll in the cornmeal mixture.  Set aside.
3.  Preheat broiler in oven.  Peel and halve 2 large Granny Smith (or other tart) apples, remove the cores and slice thinly about 3/4 of the way up, keeping the halves intact.  Place on a baking sheet, cover with a mixture of 2 oz. melted butter with 2 T. bourbon and broil until edges are brown (4-5 min).
4.  Combine 3 oz. each of bourbon, cider and cider vinegar with 2 large shallots (chopped finely) and 1 minced clove garlic.  Reduce over medium heat until nearly dry, then add the juice from half a lemon and lower heat.  whisk in 8 oz. chilled butter in small pieces (do not allow to separate) and add salt and pepper to taste.  remove from heat and set aside.
5.  Fry the scallops in 1/2" of vegetable oil (medium high heat).  Drain.
6.  Place apples on plates, put scallops on apples and serve.

name: Scallops with Shiitakes, Lemon and Parsley
instructions:
1.  Saut
12 large shiitake mushrooms in 3 T. butter until soft.  Add 1.5 lb. scallops and sauté until lightly browned.
2.  Add another T. butter, 1 t. lemon juice,  1/2 t. kosher salt and 2 T. chopped parsley and toss.

name: Tuna Tartar (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Dice 1.5 lb. sashimi-quality bluefin tuna (preferably the fattier belly cut) into 1/8-in. pieces.  Place in a mixing bowl and add 4 T. extra virgin olive oil, 2 t. each minced chives, garlic chives, mint, cilantro and parsley.  Mix well.  Sprinkle with 2/3 of the juice from 2 lemons and mix again.  Taste and adjust seasoning as desired.
2.  Place 1/2 lb. baby arugula in a bowl and mix in the remaining juice from the lemon, along with some salt and pepper.    Place on each of eight cold plates, top with the tuna and serve.

name: Yorkshire Pudding
instructions:
Mix 1 C. flour, 1/2 t. salt, 1 C. milk and 2 eggs.  Beat well until smooth, then refrigerate at least one hour.
Remove from Refrigerator, beat again then place in a greased roasting pan.  Bake 20 min. at 400

name: Oyster stuffing
instructions:
1.  Saut
1 peeled and chopped onion in 2 T. butter or olive oil until soft.  Add 1 C. chopped celery, 1 C. chopped Italian parsley, 2 t. dried thyme, 1 t. dried rosemary, 1t dried sage, salt and pepper to taste and cook another 5 min.
Remove to a large bowl, add 10 C. stale bread, cut into 1/2" cubes, and 3/4 C. chicken broth.  Add 1 pint shucked oysters with liquid  and a dash of Tabasco sauce, toss and let cool.  Stuff bird with mixture.

name: Savory Turkey Stuffing
instructions:
1.  Brown 3 lb. pork sausage in 1/4 C. olive oil.  Add 1 small white onion (chopped), 4 cloves minced garlic, and 4-6 chopped serrano chiles; cook until lightly browned.
2.  Add 2 diced Granny Smith apples, 1/4 C. cider vinegar, 1/2 C. apple cider and cook another 5 min.
3.  Fold in 16 oz. bread crumbs,  2 T. chopped marjoram and salt to taste.
4.  Allow to cool before stuffing turkey.

name: Cranberry sauce
instructions:
1.  Bring 1.25 C. sugar, 0.5 C. raspbery vinegar and 0.25 C. water to a boil in a medium saucepan.  Mix in a 12 oz package (3 C.) of  cranberries, one cinnamon stick and 1 T. orange peels (orange rind only).  Simmer until berries burst, then remove from heat and cool completely.
s Liver and Mushroom Pate (serves six)
1.  Saut
1/2 lb. sliced mushrooms in 1 T. butter.  Ditto 1/2 lb. calf
s liver cut in 1-in. clices and 1/2 medium onion.  Place in a food processor and blend thoroughly.
2.  Mix the above with 1/4 lb. cream cheese, 1/8 t. nutmeg and salt to taste in an electric mixer until very fluffy.  Serve at room temperature on toast.

name: Greek Raita (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Drain 1 qt. plain yogurt overnight.
2.  Add 4 cloves of minced garlic, 1 grated cucumber (drained), 1 T. olive oil, dill, salt and pepper to taste.  Mix well and serve with bread or naan.

name: High-maintenance oysters in aspic (serves eight)
instructions:
1. Shuck 26 oysters, reserving the juice and shells.  Rinse oysters, and strain the juice.
2.  Simmer 2 C. champagne until reduced to one C. (about 30 min).  Add 1 C. fish stock and simmer another few minutes then remove from heat.  Dissolve 1 pkg gelatin in 2 T. warm water.  Ladle in 1 C. of the broth, stirring until smooth.  Add back to the remaining broth, again stirring until smooth.  Pour into a large bowl and set over ice.
3.  Saut
1 medium shallot in 1/2 t. unsalted butter until just soft, then remove from heat.  Chop two of the oysters finely and add to the shallot, along with 3 oz. fromage blanc, and 1/4 C. heavy cream, softly whipped.  Mix in 1.4 t. hot pepper sauce, 1/2 t. salt and pepper to taste.
4.  Wash 1 bunch of watercress.  Pinch off 48 leaves and blanch in boiling saltwater, then rinse and set aside.  Finely chop the remainder of the watercress, stems and all., and fold into the shallot cream.
5.  Pat the oysters dry.  Place 1 t. of shallot cream in each oyster shell, then add an oyster and top with two leaves of watercress.  Coat with the champagne jelly and refrigerate for 5 min.  Repeat until the oysters have been enclosed.  Top with some of the reserved oyster juice and a few drops of lemon.
(serves six)
1.  Remove and meat from the turkey neck, and grind with a meat grinder with the rest of the giblets (or chop very finely with a sharp knife).  Set aside.
2.  Cook 1/2 C. finely chopped onion and 1 stalk finely chopped celery in 2 T. vegetable oil until soft (stir frequently).  Add the minced meat and continue until cooked through and lightly browned.
3.  Remove from heat.  Make a well in the center of the mixture, add 1 large egg (beaten) and quickly stir in.  Mix in 1/4 C. mayonnaise, kosher salt, pepper and Tabasco sauce to taste.  Garnish with chopped parsley and serve on quartered toast.

name: Wild Rice Cakes (makes 10 cakes)
instructions:
1.  Cook  wild rice to make 1.5 C. when done.  Rinse and drain the rice.
2.  Pulse the rice with 2 cloves chopped garlic, 1 beaten egg, 2 T. flour, 6 T. milk, 1/2 t. baking powder, 1/2 t. salt, and 1 T. melted butter in a food processor till combined.
3.  Brown dollops of the batter in butter on both sides.  Serve with smoked fish.

name: Gnocchi (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Scald 1 C. milk, then add 2 T. butter, 2 T. flour, 2 T. cornstarch and 1/2 t. salt.  Reduce heat; add 1 egg yolk and 1/2 C. grated cheddar cheese.  Stir until melted.
2.  Pour into a shallow platter.  When cool, cut into 2-in. strips, baste with melted butter, sprinkle with grated romano or parmesano cheese and poach in boiling water for 1-2 min.  Drain and serve warm.

name: Corn Cakes (makes ~ 25 small cakes)
instructions:
1.  Combine 2/3 C. cornmeal, 1 C. flour and 1/2 t. sugar.  Add 2 beaten eggs, 3 C. fresh or frozen corn, 1/4 C. chopped red bell pepper, 4 T. butter, salt and pepper to taste.  Mix well.
2.  Saut
1-T. dollops of the batter in butter until lightly browned, ~ 1 min. per side.  Serve on a warm platter.
re
1.  Preheat oven to 350°F.  Melt 1/2 stick of butter on a baking sheet, then spread 3 lb. thinly sliced onions on sheet, top with thyme and rosemary sprigs and one bay leaf, sprinkle with salt and pepper, drizzle with 3 T. extra-virgin olive oil, top with another 3 lb. sliced onions and bake until tender and golden, stirring every 30 min. (~2 hr. total cooking time).  Discard herbs.
2.  Pour 1 C. warm water into a small bowl and mix in 2 envelopes of dried yeast,1 t. sugar and 2 T. olive oil.  Let stand until foamy, ~ 10 min.
3.  Blend 2.75 C. flour and 1 t. salt in a food processor.  Add the yeast mixture and 2 T. olive oil and process until dough clumps together (add more flour if dough is sticky).  Turn dough out on counter and knead until smooth and elastic, ~ 5 min.  Coat a large bowl with 1 T. olive oil, add the dough, turn once to coat and cover with plastic wrap, then a kitchen towel.  Let rise until doubled, ~ 1.5 hr.  Punch down and let rise another hour.
4.  Sprinkle a heavy 17x11x1-in. baking sheet with cornmeal.  Roll out on a lightly floured surface.  Transfer to the baking sheet, pressing the edges all the way around along the sides and corners.  Cover with a dry kitchen towel and let rise 1 hr.
5.  Pre-heat oven to 475°F.  Spread onions out on top of dough.  Arrange 20 anchovy fillets and 20 oil-cured, pitted black olives on top and bake 15 min.  Sprinkle top with 1 T. chopped, fresh thyme and serve warm or at Rm. T.

name: Welsh Rarebit (serves four)
instructions:
Melt 2 T. butter, then add 2 T. flour and cook with occasional stirring until golden brown and fragrant.  Stir in 1 T. mustard powder and 1/2 t. cayenne (or both to taste), then whisk in 3/4 . Guinness beer and 2 T. Worchestershire sauce.  When mixture is uniform, stir in 1 lb. of Cheddar cheese (and/or other semi-hard cheese), grated well.
Toast slices of bread, medium on one side, lightly on the other.  Spread the cheese mixture on the lighter side and broil until edges of toast are crisp and the cheese bubbly.  Serve immediately.

name: Sweet potatoes and greens
instructions: Steam 2 lb. yams (peeled and cut into 3/4" cubes) until just tender (~10 min).  Allow to cool in a large bowl.

name: Saut
instructions:
2/3 C. minced shallots in 1/4 C. butter for 1 min in a large skillet.  Add 1 lb. Swiss chard (stemmed and torn into bite-size pieces) and toss until just wilted.
Add yams to skillet and toss until heated.  Season with nutmeg, salt and pepper.

name: Potato Savonnettes
instructions:
1.  Cut 4 peeled Idaho potatoes into 1 in. slabs.  Rinse and pat dry.  Arrange in a single layer in a 12 in. non-stick pan.  Add 2 T. unsalted butter, 1 T. canola oil, 1/4 t. salt and 2/3 C. water.  bring ot a boil for  1 min.
2.  Place the potatoes (still in pan) in an oven pre-heated to 425°F and cook for 30 min.
3.  Return to stove and brown over high heat (if necessary).   Divide up and serve.

name: Mashed Potatoes (with Carrot Puré e)
instructions:
1.  Peel and cube 4 large baking potatoes.  Cover with water in a large saucepan (with trimmed fat from any meat dish accompanying the potatoes), bring to a boil and simmer for ~20 minutes until soft.
Press through a potato ricer.  Combine with 4 T. butter, 1/2 C. milk (or more) and kosher salt, white and black pepper to taste.  Finally, with a heavy whisk whip in 1/4 C. extra virgin olive oil.
If desired, combine with 1 lb. boiled carrots, puré ed with 1 C. beef broth.
o Mashed Potatoes
1.  Peel and cube 4 large baking potatoes.  Cover with water in a large saucepan, bring to boil and simmer for 10-15 min.  Drain and pass potatoes through a ricer.
2.  Roast 20 cloves of garlic and 2 chopped jalape
os with a pinch or two of cayenne.
3.  Stir the garlic and jalape
os into the potatoes along with 12 T. yogurt and 12 T. lowfat milk.  Salt and pepper to taste and serve immediately.

name: Parsleyed Potatoes
instructions:
1.  Cook four large baking potatoes with nine large cloves of garlic (halved).
2.  Pass through food mill and add 2 T. butter, 2 T. chopped fresh rosemary 1 C. canned chicken broth (added slowly) and 1/2 C. Parmesan cheese.  Mix well and serve.

name: Gratin of Potatoes
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 400°F.  Bring 1 C. whipping cream and 1.5 C. half and half to a boil in a large pot.  Add 2 lb. of potatoes (peeled and thinly sliced) and 1.25 lb turnips (peeled and finely sliced).  Simmer for 5-10 min.
2.  Using a slotted spoon, remove the potatoes and turnips to a greased 8"x8" casserole.  Add 1/4 C. (or less) creamy horseradish sauce and 1/2 t. dried rosemary to the cream and mix well.  Pour over the vegetables and top with Parmesan cheese.
3.  Bake  covered for 30 min at 400°F, then remove lid and broil until top is well-browned.  Serve immediately.

name: Potato-Onion Cakes
instructions:
1.  Boil 6 large potatoes (whole) for 20 min., then drain and refrigerate.
2.  Peel and coarsely grate the potatoes, mix in 12 chopped green onions and salt/pepper to taste.  Beat 2 eggs with 2 t. cumin (optional) and mix in with potatoes.  Cover and refrigerate.
3.  Fry batter in vegetable oil over medium-high heat and serve with rosemary steak.

name: Pommes Dauphine
instructions:
1. Boil 2 lb. Yukon Gold potatoes till soft.  Drain the potatoes and mash.  Add 1/2 C. milk, 4 T. butter, salt and pepper to taste, and set aside.
2.  In a pan heat 1 C. water,  1/2 C. butter and 1/2 t. salt till the butter melts.  Add 1 C. flour all at once and beat with a wooden spoon until the flour dough starts to form a "ball".  Let cool for just a minute then beat in two eggs, stirring to incorporate well then add the remaining eggs one at a time till it is glossy and falls from spoon.  (You may not need all of the eggs, so with the last egg inspect the choux first and if it is loose and shiny, don't add the last egg).
3.  Beat the choux into the mashed potatoes.
4.  Heat 3-4
of cooking oil to 350
F in a cast iron skillet.  When hot, form the potato mixture into 1.5-in. balls and drop into the oil, a few at a time (they will go to the bottom, but bob back to the top of the oil if it is hot enough).  Fry until golden brown and remove to deain on a paper towel.  Keep in a warm 300F oven till all are cooked.

name: Mashed Potatoes with Brown Onions
instructions:
1.  Boil 2.5 lb peeled baking potatoes in a saucepan until tender.  Drain and rice.
2.  Stir in 8 T. butter, salt, nutmeg and white pepper to taste and 1/2 C. heavy cream.  Mix well.
3.  Slice a medium onion into medium  slices, dust with flour and deep-fat fry in pre-heated vegetable oil until brown.  Drain, top mashed potatoes with onions and serve.

name: Boston baked beans  (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Soak  2 C. white pea (navy) beans in 2 qts. water for 6 hours.  Drain and place in a large pot, cover with 2 inches of cool water with 1 t. salt.  Bring to a boil and reduce to a simmer, then cook 30-40 minutes until tender.  Drain.
2.  Bring another pot of water to a boil, and preheat oven to 250°F.  Place 1 medium peeled onion in a large casserole with a tight lid along with 4 spareribs or babyback ribs.  Spread the beans on top.
In a small bowl, mix 1/3 C. brown sugar, 2 t. dry mustard and 1 t. black pepper, and add this to the beans.
Add just enough boiling water to keep the beans covered and bake covered for 4-5 hr.
Remove casserole from oven. Season with salt.  Remove the meat from the ribs and trim excess fat, then add the meat back to the beans.
Return the beans to the oven, uncovered, and bake another 1 hr. to thicken sauce.

name: Potato/Cheese Souffl
instructions:
1.  Boil 4 baking potatoes (sliced) for 15 min.  Drain and rice.
2.  Whisk 2 eggs and 1.5 C. ricotta cheese until smooth.  Mix in 1  C. half and half and 1.5 C. Gruy
re, then the potatoes.  Season with salt and pepper.
3.  Place in a greased casserole dish and bake 30 min at 350°F.  Sprinkle with 1/2 C. Gruy
re and heat until cheese is melted.

name: Green beans, peas, chives and parsimmon
instructions:
1.  Cook 1.5 lb string beans and 0.5 lb green peas in salted water for 2-4 min.  Rinse with cold water and drain.
2.  Saut
3 thinly sliced Fuyu (not Hachiya) persimmons (~1 lb) in 3 T. olive oil until browned and softened (about 4 min).
3.  Add beans and peas, along with 1/4 C. chives.  Stir gently until hot.  Serve.

name: Broccoli Casserole (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Saut
2 chopped stalks of celery and 1 chopped onion in 1/2 stick of butter.
2.  Steam 1 bunch of chopped broccoli for 10 min, and cook 1/2 C. rice in 1 C. water for 10 in.  Add to the celery and onion along with 1 can cream of mushroom soup, 1/2 jar of cheez whiz, and 1 C. half-cooked rice.
3.  Pour into a greased casserole dish, top with grated cheese and bake 50 min at 350

name: Curried Broccoi Casserole
instructions:
1.  Saut
1/2 C. celery, 1/2 C. onion in 4 T. butter or olive oil.  Add one bunch of steamed broccoli, 1 can cream of mushroom soup, 1/2 jar Cheez Wiz, 1 C. half-cooked basmati rice and 1 T. curry powder.
2.  Pour into a greased casserole, top with Parmesan cheese and bake 50 min. at 350

name: Zucchini Gratin (serves four)
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.  Slice 3 medium zucchini into 1/4-in. rounds and sprinkle with salt.  Place in a sieve and allow to drain for 1 hr.  Pat dry.  Arrange zucchini in a 10-in. quiche dish, overlapping slightly.
2.  Whisk together 1 T. dry white wine, 1 t. olive oil, 1 t. chopped Italian parsley, 1/4 t. chopped fresh thyme, salt and pepper to taste.  Spoon over the zucchini and bake until tender but not too soft (~25 min.).

name: Jerusalem Artichoke and Sage Gratin
instructions:
1.  Peel 4 lb. Jerusalem arichokes and cut into 1 in. pieces.  In a non-reactant saucepan, simmer in 2 C. milk plus water to cover for 30 min.
2.  Sauté one large onion (chopped) and 2 T. garlic (chopped) and 1 T. fresh sage in 5 t. clarified butter or olive oil until onion is golden.
3.  Combine, add 1/2 C. Parmesan cheese and puré e in a food processor, then spoon into a 2 qt. gratin dish.
4.  Saut
2.5 C. bread crumbs and 2 T. fresh sage in 3 T. butter or olive oil until golden.
5.  Season with salt and pepper, stir and layer over puré e.  (Refrigerate overnight).
6.  Bake 20 min at 425

name: Greek Artichokes with Fava Beans (serves eight)
instructions:
1. Place 12 pearl onions (peeled and halved) and 8 scallions (whites plus 2 in. of the greens, sliced into 1/2-in. pieces) in a large, deep skillet over medium heat for 2 min. to remove excess moisture.  Add 1/2 C. vegetable oil and 1.5 t. kosher salt, then cook until onions become translucent (~ 3 min.).
2.  Add 8 large artichoke bottoms and stems (outer leaves and chokes removed, stems peeled and separated from bottoms) in an even layer in the pan with enough water to come about 3/4 the way up the artichokes.  Reduce heat to a strong simmer, cover and cook 20 min., turning the artichoke pieces once.
3.  Add 1 lb. dry fava beans, 2 thickly sliced carrots and 1/8 C. dill.  Add enough water to just cover the beans, cover and cook until beans are tender (~ 7 min.).
4.  Remove from heat and gently stir in 1/4 C. lemon juice and kosher salt to taste.  Transfer to a platter, drizzle with 1/4 C. extra virgin olive oil and serve warm.

name: Vegetable Pilaf (serves six to eight)
instructions:
1. Combine one fresh red Thai chile, two cloves of garlic, a 1/2 inch piece of fresh, peeled ginger and blend (pulsing) with four T. water until smooth.
2.  Heat 1 T. vegetable oil over medium-high heat and add 1/2 head of cauliflower (cut into florets), 2/3 C. fresh shelled peas, one thinly sliced carrot, 2/3 C. french beans cut into one-inch pieces, 1/5 t. chili powder, 1/2 t. ground cumin, 1/2 t. turmeric, 1 t. garam masala, and the blended chile mixture.  Cook with stirring for about 10 min, until the vegetables are tender.  Place on a serving platter.
3.  Return the pot to the stove, adding 4 t. clarified butter, 1 stick of cinnamon, 1 bay leaf, and 1 thinly sliced onion.  Cook until the onion is softened.  Ass 2 C. basmati rice and stir until coated.  Add the vegetables, 4 C. water and 2.25 t. salt, bring to a boil and simmer for 15 min.  Toss with a fork and serve immediately.

name: Rosemary Jerusalem artichokes
instructions:
Combine 1/4 C. olive oil and 4 t. minced fresh rosemary in a bowl. Salt and pepper.
Scrub 3 lb. Jerusalem artichokes under cold water and cut in half.  Add immediately to oil mixture and toss.  Arange on a baking sheet cut-side down and bake 40 min or until golden.  Remove to serving platter and garnish with parsley.

name: Grilled vegetables (serves six to eight)
instructions: 1.  Combine 1/3 C. white wine venegar, 1/4 C. chopped fresh parsley, 1/4 C. lemon juice, 1 T. vegetable oil, 2 t. dried Italian seasoning, 1 T. chopped fresh oregano (or 1 t. dried oregano) and 4 cloves minced garlic in a large, shallow dish.  Add 1 medium sliced eggplant, 2 quartered sweet yellow peppers and some of whatever else strikes you.  Toss gently to coat, then grill on an open fire until tender.

name: Haitian Rice and Beans (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Place 1 lb. dried red beans and 16 whole cloves in a medium pot and cover by 2 in.  Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until very tender (~ 1 hr.).  Drain, reserving the liquid.  Add water to the liquid to make 8 C. total.
2.  Meanwhile, mash 2 cloves garlic, 2 sprigs parsley and 2 chopped scallions into a paste with a mortar and pestle.
3.  Heat 2 T. vegetable oil over high heat in a kettle with a tight-fitting lid.  Add the scallion paste and sauté until fragrant.  Add the beans, 2 14-oz. cans of coconut milk, and 3 t. finely chopped spanish onion.  Cook another 3 min., stirring occasionally.
4.  Stir in the reserved bean liquid and add 1 t. adobo seasoning.  Cook covered for 30 min.  Stir in 2 T. picalese, 1/2 thinly sliced green pepper and bring to a boil.  Stir in 4 C. long-grain rice with kosher salt and pepper to taste, and cook another 25 min.
5.  Transfer to a serving bowl and serve immediately.

name: Picalese
instructions:
1.  Add 12 habanero or scotch bonnet chiles, 1 small spanish onion and 1 carrot (all thinly sliced) to a jar with a tight-fitting lid.  Add distilled white vinegar to cover and store in the refrigerator for 1-2 weeks.  Use to spice up soups, stews, etc.
e of Peas and Watercress (serves four)
1.  Fill a large saucepan wih water and bring to a boil.  Add 4 C. frozen peas and return to a boil.  Turn off the heat and add the tops (2-3 in.) of two 1-lb. bunches of watercress.  Allow to stand 5 min. until the watercress has wilted.
2.  Drain the peas and watercress, then blend in a food processor with 4 T. butter, 1/2 t. black pepper and 3.4 t. sea salt until smooth.  Serve with bread.

name: Glazed pearl onions
instructions:
1.  Boil 1 lb. pearl onions in a pot of salted water until skins are loosened (3 min).  Drain and cool thoroughly.  Cut off roots and squeeze onions out of outer skins.
2.  Combine onions with 1 C. dry sherry, 0.5 C. raisins, 0.25 C. honey, 0.25 C. wate, 2 T. butter and 1 t. fresh, minced thyme in a large skillet.  Bring to a boil and simmer on low until liquid has evaporated and onions caramelize (~45 min).  Stir frequently.
3.  Season with salt and pepper, remove from heat and add 2/3 C. slivered almonds and 4 T. sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar, plus a little water if too dry.

name: Eggplant Parmesan (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Cut 2 large eggplants lengthwise into 1/4-in. slices.  Layer slices in a collander, salting each layer generously.  Press down with several plates and drain for 2 hr.
2.  Combine a 28-oz can of whole, peeled San Marzano tomatoes, 1 clove of garlic (crushed), 1/3 C. olive oil, salt and pepper to taste and blend in a food processor.
3.  Press out the remaining liquid from the eggplant and dry on paper towels.  Combine 1/2 C. flour with 1/2 C. dry breadcrumbs in a shallow bowl and mix well, and beat 8 large eggs in a separate bowl.  Heat 1/4-in. of canola oil with 3/4-in. of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.  When shimmering hot, dredge the eggplant strips in the flour mixture, then dip in the egg batter and fry until golden brown on both sides, turning once.  Drain on paper towels with crispy bits.
4.  Pre-heat oven to 350°F.  Spread 1 C. of the tomato sauce in the bottom of a baking dish and top with a third of the eggplant slices.  Top eggplant with half of 1.5 lb. fresh mozzarella (sliced into 1.4-in. rounds), a third of 1 C. grated Parmesan cheese and half of 1 C. basil leaves.  Repeat with a second layer of eggplant.  Top with a third payer of eggplant, 1 C. tomato sauce and 1/3 C. Parmesan.  Bake until lightly browned, ~30 min., and allow to stand 10 min. before serving.

name: Tapenade I
instructions:
Wrap 4 lemons in two layers of aluminum foil  and bake 1.5 hrs. at 400°F.
Allow to cool.  Unwrap lemons, cut in half lengthwise and carefully scoop out the flesh.  Remove seeds and pure
with 2 t. sugar.  Season with salt and pepper.
Combine 2 C. Greek or Moroccan olives (dry-cured and pitted), 3 cloves of garlic, 6 oz. anchovy fillets (drained and patted dry), 6 oz. canned tuna in oil (drained), 0.5 C. capers, 1 T. green peppercorns (drained and chopped), 0.25 t. chopped fresh thyme, 2 T. grated lemon zest and 2 T. cognac (optional).  Pulse in a food processor until still coarse, then slowly add 2/3 C. olive oil.
Fill lemon shells with equal portions of the lemon flesh and tapenade.
e 9 oz. pitted black olives, 4 oz. garlic, 4 oz. anchovy fillets and 80 g. drained capers in a food processor until smooth.  Add ~50 mL. olive oil and continue processing until smooth.
Cut off the end of a large cucumber.  Using a long thin knife, scoop out the seeds and fill the hollowed cucumber with the tapenade mixture.
Press a Ritz cracker against the end of the cucumber and slice off a section.  Repeat until the cucumber is used up.  Serve immediately.

name: Polenta and Black Bean Salad with Chile Verde (serves four)
instructions:
1.  In a large, heavy saucepan, combine 2 C. coarse yellow cornmeal with 6 C. water, 1 t. kosher salt and 1/2 t. pepper.  Whisk until smooth.  Bring to a boil over high heat, whisking constantly, and boil 2 min, then reduce to a simmer, incorporate 2 T. butter and 1/4 C. heavy cream with a wooden spoon and simmer 30-40 min until tender (stir every 5 min or so to avoid scorching).
2.  Transfer to a small baking pan and allow to cool to room T.  Cover and refrigerate until firm (4 to 24 hours).
3.  Combine 1.5 C. black beans (drained and cooked), 1/3 C. finely diced onion, 1/2 C. finely diced carrot, and 1/2 C. red pepper (roasted and finely diced).
4.  In a separate bowl, whisk together 3 T. extra virgin olive oil, the juice from one lime, kosher salt and pepper to taste and 1/4 T. cumin.  Pour over the bean mixture and mix thoroughly.  Add 1/4 C. finely chopped cilantro and mix again.
5.  Cut the polenta into squares, etc.  Brush both sides with vegetable oil and grill over high heat, turning, once, until warmed and browned, ~ 4 min on a side.
6.  Nuke some canned chile verde, and serve with a slice of the grilled polenta and the bean salad on the side.

name: Bulgur, Prosciutto and Pea Salad
instructions:
1.  Put 2/3 cup Bulgur wheat in a large bowl and add 3 C. boiling water.  Let stand 1 hr.
2.  Drain wheat and toss in 1 C. cooked peas and 1 C. thinly sliced prosciutto.
3.  Peel and mince one shallot and whisk together with 1 t. Dijon mustard and 2 t. white wine vinegar in a medium bowl.  Slowly whisk in 2 T. olive oil then 1 T. water.  Add salt and pepper to taste.
4.  Pour vinaigrette over salad and top with 1 T. chopped parsley.

name: White Bean and Shrimp Salad
instructions:
1.  Bring 1 C. soaked navy beans, 2 sprigs thyme, 1 bay leaf and 1 crushed clove of garlic to boil in 5 C. water.  Simmer 45 min.
2.  Drain beans and remove thyme, bay leaf and garlic.  Toss in 1 lb. cooked shrimp and 1 C. slivered fennel.  Salt and pepper to taste.
3.  Whisk together 4 t. sherry vinegar, 4 t. olive oil and 2 T. chopped fresh terragon.  Toss into salad and garnish with 2 T. chopped chives.

name: Portuguese  Tuna and Black-eyed Peas (serves four to six)
instructions: 1.  Drain and rinse 1 can of black-eyed peas, then place in a large bowl.  Add 1 can medium can of white tuna (in water), 2-3 green onions (thinly sliced), 1 T. minced garlic (optional), 2 T. extra-virgin olive oil, 1 T. lemon juice, kosher salt and pepper to taste.  Mix well, place in a serving bowl and top with ground paprika.

name: Spicy Grilled Chicken Salad
instructions:
1.  Combine 4 serrano peppers (minced), 2 large cloves of garlic (minced), 1/2 t. grated lime rind and 1/4 C. fresh lime juice.  Marinate with 1/2 lb. chicken breasts > 1 hr.
2.  Cook 2 C. white rice.  Cool and toss in 1 T. chopped parsley, 1 T. chopped cilantro, 1 T. chopped mint and two chopped serrano peppers.
3.  Grill the chicken breasts, slice thinly and add to salad.
oise Salad
1.  Bring 3/4 lb. red potatoes to a boil over high heat.  Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 30 min.  Drain and set aside to cool.
2.  Mix 1.5 t. Dijon mustard, 5 cloves crushed garlic, 1.5 T. red-wine vinegar, 7 T. olive oil, 1 t. salt & 1 t. pepper.  Cut the potatoes into 1/2-in. slices; toss with dressing.
3.  Cook 3/4 lb. stripped string beans (prefereably haricot verts) until just firm.  Drain and cool under running water.  Cut beans in half lenghtwise.
