4.  Peel 1 yellow pepper , then slice into sections remove seeds, and julienne.
5.  Slice 2 very ripe tomatoes coss-wise, remove seeds and chop coarsely.
6.  Mix all the above with 1.5 C. sliced red onion and 1 C. Ni
oise olives (or other black oil-cured olive).
7.  When ready to serve, heat a skillet over high heat for 5 min.  Sprinkle with 1 lb. fresh tuna (cut into 1-in. cubes) with salt and pepper to taste, roll in 1 T. canola oil and sauté for 2 min over high heat.  Remove from heat and toss into the salad.
8.  Line a large platter with lettuce leaves, mound the Ni
oise salad in the middle and serve with a garnish of shredded basil leaves.

name: Avocado Salad (serves six)
instructions: 1. Toss together 3 Haas avocados (diced), 1/4 C. diced scallions, 1 large red bell bepper (diced), and 1 diced red onion.  Add one medium cucumber (peeled and sliced), 2 diced tomatoes, 1/4 C. fresh lime juice, and 2 T. extra-virgin olive oil.  Season with salt and pepper to taste.

name: Mediterranean Lentil Salad
instructions:
Boil 1 C. lentils in 5 C. water in a large saucepan.  Simmer 20 min and drain into a large bowl.
When the lentils are cool, toss in 1 chopped tomato, 1/2 C. chopped black olives (oil-cured are best), 1/2 C. cheese, and (optional) 2 stalks of finely sliced celery.
Whisk together 1/4 C. lemon juice, 2 minced cloves of garlic and 1 t. minced fresh thyme in a medium bowl.  Toss vinaigrette with salad, then salt and pepper to taste and garnish with chopped parsley.

name: Avocado and Crabmeat Salad (serves six to eight)
instructions:
1.  Halve, pit and cut two ripe avocados into wedges.  Soak in 2 T. lime juice.
2.  Core 2 tomatoes and cut into wedges.  Salt lightly.
3.  Lightly mix 1 lb. crabmeat, 1 C. mayonnaise, juice from 1 lime, 1 T. chopped japape
os, 1/4 C. chopped coriander and 3 T. chopped chives.  Spread onto 6-eight plates and top with alternating wedges of avocado and tomato.

name: Lobster and Wild Rice Salad (serves six)
instructions:
Cook 1 C. wild rice in 3 C. water for 45 min.  Cool, then add 2 C. cooked lobster meat, cut into cubes.
Peel and cube 2 ripe avocados, then sprinkle with lemon juice.  Add to rice with 1/2 C. chopped red onion.
Mix 1 T. Dijon mustard with 2.5 T. red wine vinegar.  Slowly whisk in 1/2 C. olive oil to make a mayonnaise.  Add 1/2 T. minced garlic, 2 T. chopped parsley and salt and pepper to taste.  Toss with the lobster/rice mixture and serve at room T.

name: Turkey Breast and Asparagus-Chili Salad
instructions:
Blanch 3 C. asparagus (cut into 1 in. pieces) in salted, boiling water for 1 min.  Drain, rinse and drain again, then pat dry.  Toss with 2 t. chopped fresh parsley, 1.5 t. chopped fresh cilantro, 3 T. chopped fresh mint, 3 thinly sliced scallions (greens only) and 1 minced, seeded habanero.
Heat a heavy skillet over medium heat until very hot.  Sprinkle with 1/2 t. kosher salt and saut
3/4 lb. turkey breast 1.5 min on each side.
Remove turkey, squeeze lime juice over it, slice and top with salad.

name: Laotian catfish soup
instructions:
1.  Cut 1.5 lb. of catfish fillets into 1" by 1/2" strips.  Cover with palstic wrap and refrigerate.
2.  In a deep skillet, saut
12 cloves of garlic (finely chopped), 3 or 4 Thai chilies (finely chopped), 3 kaffir lime leaves (finely chopped), 4" of lemongrass (thinly sliced) and 2 shallots (peeled and finely chopped) in 2 T. vegetable oil over medium heat until very fragrant (~ 5 min.).  Add 1 qt. chicken broth, 2 T. lime juice and two 1/4" slices of galangal (peeled).  Heat to a slow simmer.
3.  Whisk 1/4 C. smooth peanut butter and 2 T. canned coconunt milk in a small bowl until blended, then add to the pot.  Stir in the fish and 14 oz. canned coconut milk, then simmer until the fish flakes when prodded (~2 min).  Stir in 6 T. Thai or Vietnamese fish sauce, 2 T. basil (finely chopped) and 2 T. cilantro (finely chopped) and simmer another 2-3 min.  Serve with rice if desired.

name: Cream of turnip, potato and leek soup
instructions:
1.  Melt 1/2 stick of butter in a heavy Dutch oven.  Add 5 thinly sliced leeks (white and green part only), 1 peeled and cubed turnip, and one large potato (peeled and sliced).  Cook over low heat for 30 min, stirring occasionally.
2.  Stir in 5 C. chicken broth and simmer another 45 min.
3.  Puré e in small batches and return to pot.  Thin with milk to desired consistency, season with alt and pepper and serve.

name: Carolina black bean soup (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Sort and wash 1 lb. dried black beans.  Place in a large Dutch oven, cover with 8 C. water and bring to a boil for 3 min..  Remove from heat, cover and let stand for 1 hr. (do not drain).
2.  Saut
1 C. chopped onion, 1 C. chopped celery, 1 C. chopped green pepper and 1 clove minced garlic in 1 T. olive oil over medium-high heat, stirring constantly until tender.  Add onion mixture to beans, season with salt and pepper and simmer for 1-2 hr, stirring occasionally.
3.  Stir in 24 oz of tomato sauce, simmer another 30 min, then serve.
s Soup (serves eight)
1.  Simmer 3-4 beef short ribs, 1/2 chopped onion, and 1 can stewed tomatoes, and 2 stalks chopped celery with water to cover overnight, covered.
2.  Add 2-3 chopped potatoes, 3-4 carrots, and a package of mixed vegetables.  Simmer on high for 45 min.

name: Turkey Soup
instructions:
1.  Place turkey carcass in a large pot filled with water.  Add any leftover gravy, 1 bay leaf, 1 t. dried thyme, 2 C. chopped celery tops, 1 C. chopped carrots, 1 C. chopped onion, 1 C. chopped leek greens, 6 sprigs fresh parsley and salt/pepper to taste.  Simmer at least one hour.
2. Strain broth into a clean kettle and save any meat.  Skim off fat, and 2 C. diced carrots, 2 C. finely chopped celery, 1/2 C. chopped onion, 1.5 C. leek whites, 2 C. chopped tomatoes, 2 C. cubed zucchini and 1/2 t. chopped garlic.  Simmer for 10 min, then add 1/2 C. Greek orzo (rice pasta), cook another 5 min then add 1 C. corn kernels and leftover meat (chopped).  Cook ~15 min - serve with Parmesan cheese.

name: Melon Soup (serves four)
instructions:
1. Puré e one small honeydew melon, 1/2 C. lime juice, 2 minced jalape
os, 1/2 C. yogurt and 2 t. salt.  Refrigerate until cold.
2.  Toast 1/2 C. sliced almonds in a 325
F oven for 15 min.  Place the chilled soup in bowls and garnish with the toasted almonds and chopped fresh mint.

name: Venetian Seafood Stew (serves six)
instructions:
1. Simmer 2.25 lb. cranberry beans (shelled and washed) in 3 C. fish stock with 1 bay leaf for 30 min.  Discard the bay leaf.
2.  Heat 2 T. olive oil in a large pot.  Add 12 mussels and 1 lb. of either cockles, littleneck or Manila clams and cook until they open (about 10 min.).
3.  Remove the shellfish with a slotted spoon and save, covered.  Add 1 C. fish stock to the pot with 1/4 bulb of sliced fennel, 4 carrots and 1 C. dry white wine and bring to a boil.  Add 1 lb. jumbo shrimp (shelled and deveined) and cook until just pink (about 2 min).  Remove to the shellfish bowl.
4.  Add 1 lb. filleted salmon (cut into 2 in. chunks) and 1 lb. sea bass or red snapper (cut into 2 in. chunks) to the pot and cook another 2 min.  Return the shellfish to the pot, add 6 scallions (trimmed to 3 in.), salt and pepper to taste and bring to a simmer.  Stir in 3 T. olive oil and serve.

name: Butternut Squash (and Chestnut) Soup
instructions:
1.  Saut
2 stalks coarsely chopped celery and 1 chopped onion in 1.5 T. vegetable oil until soft (~8 min) in a large container.  (Stir in 1 can unsweetened chestnuts), 4 C. peeled butternut squash, 1 chopped Granny Smith apple, (1 T. minced ginger root), 1 t. sugar, 1 t .salt, 1/2 t. curry powder and 1/8 t. pepper with 29 oz. (two large cans) of chicken broth.  Simmer for half an hour.
2.  Process with a metal blade until smooth.  Return to the saucepan and stir in 1 5-oz. can of evaporated milk. (and 3 T. bourbon).  Heat thoroughly, then ladle into bowls, garnish with chive, toasted pecans or toasted squash seeds, and serve.

name: Roasted-Pepper Soup (double recipe by making one red and one yellow soup)
instructions: Char four medium red or yellow peppers over an open flame until blackened and blistered.  Place in a paper bag and seal tightly for 20 min. until cool.

name: Stem and seed peppers, then puré e in a food processor until smooth.
instructions: Combine puré e with 4 C. chicken stock and bring to a simmer.  Cook 20 min, seasoning with 1/2 t. ground white pepper, 1/4 t. cardamom,1/4 t. ground coriander and salt to taste.  Garnish with fresh basil leaves and serve. (Serves 4).

name: French Onion Soup I (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Melt 2 T. butter in a large soup pot.  Add 12 thinly sliced onions, season with salt and pepper and stir.  Over low heat, cook onions with occasional stirring until very dark brown (half an hour or more).
2.  Crush 2 plum tomatoes by hand and add to onion mix, along with four branches of fresh thyme ,one bay leaf and 8 C. strong beef stock.  Adjust seasoning and simmer for one hour.
3. Ladle into bowls, add croutons and top with grated Gruyere cheese.  Pass under a broiler and serve.

name: French Onion Soup II
instructions:
1.  Saut
6 lb. thinly sliced yellow onions in 2 T. butter until caramelized (~30 min).
2.  Add 5 C. beef broth, 1 C. apple juice and salt/pepper to taste.  Bring to boil and simmer 30 min, then add 4 T. Cognac.
3.  Ladle soup into oven-proof bowls, cover with 1/4" slices of baguette, sprinkle with grated Gruy
re cheese and broil until cheese is just browned.
ed Onion Soup (serves 6)
Slice 1 baguette into 25-30 1/2-in. slices, then toast them and let cool.  Lay slices out on a baking sheet and top with all but 1/2 C. of 9 oz. grated Ementhaler cheese.

name: Boston Clam Chowder (serves 10 to 12)
instructions:
1.  Remove and set aside the tough muscle of each of 24 clams.  Reserve both the muscle and the softer body portion.  Chop the muscles as finely as possible, and the body less coarsely.  There chould be enough meat for ~1.25 C. total.
2.  Finely dice 1/8 lb. salt pork and cook in a kettle, stiring often, until rendered and crispy.  Add 1 T. butter and 1 C. finely diced onions, then cook until wilted.  Sprinkle with 2 T. flour, then add 4 C. clam broth and 2 C. water, stirring vigorously with a whisk.  Add the chopped clam muscle and 1.5 lb. diced potatoes, bring to a boil and cook 30 min.
3.  Add the soft portions of the clams and cook another 5 min.  Stir in 3 C. milk, 1 C. heavy cream, salt and pepper to taste.  Bring to a simmer, add another 1 T. butter and serve immediately with oyster crackers.

name: Potato and Broccoli Soup (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Nuke 3 large potatoes for 10 min and allow to stand, covered, for 10 min.
2.  Roughly chop 1 bunch of broccoli and 3 yellow onions.  Sauté in a large pot in 6 T. butter until soft.
3.  Add 2 qts. chicken stock, nutmeg salt and pepper to taste.  Add the meat of the potatoes and cook 20 min.
4.  Remove and cool the solids, then blend in a food processor until smooth.
5.  Return solids to the stock, add 1 C. cream and 1 C. grated cheddar cheese.  Heat thoroughly and serve with bread and croutons.

name: Seafood Bisque (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Combine 2 C. dry white wine, a bay leaf, one roughly chopped onion, 2 ribs celery in a large stock pot and bring to a boil.  Add 1.5 to 2 lb. lobster and cook 10 min.  Remove lobster to cool.
2.  Add 12 unshelled shrimp and cook 5 min.  Remove shrimp and allow to cool.
3.  Add 24 well-scrubbed mussels, and cook 5 min or until they open.  Remove to cool.
4.  Add 2 C. water to the wine, bring to a boil and add 12 sea scallops.  cover and steam for 3 min, remove and cool.
5.  Extract the lobster meat, peel and devein the shrimp (saving the shells).  Chop meat into bite-size pieces.  Return all the shells to the broth, add another 2 C. water and bring to a boil.  Simmer another 30 min.
6.  Strain the broth and return to the pot.  Bring to a simmer, then add 4 C. heavy cream, 1 C. milk, 1 t. dried thyme, 1 T. minced fresh parsley and 1/4 t. dried rosemary, bring to a simmer ~5 min until slightly thickened.  Add the seafood and simmer another 2 min.
7.  Add 1 C. fresh spinach and 1/2 C. grated carrots and simmer another 2 min to just wilt the spinach.  Season with salt, pepper and 1/2 t. lemon juice.

name: Poblano Vichyssoise
instructions:
Saute 4 leeks (chopped coarsely) and 1 onion (chopped) in 1 T. olive oil until soft.  Peel and dice 1 lb russet potatoes.  Add to 5 C. water in a pot, along with the leeks and onion.  Add 2 t. salt and simmer covered for 40 min.
Char 4 large poblano peppers over an open flame (~5 min).  Cover in a large bowl and allow to cool.  Peel away the charred outer skin, starting at the stem.  Cut off tops and discard seeds and ribs.
Add 2 C. milk and 2C half-and-half to the potato mixture.  Bring to a boil, stirring constantly, then remove.  Puré e in a food processor with three of the poblanos.  Strain, then add 1 C. sour cream and 1/4 C. lime juice.  Chill well.
Dice the remaining poblano, slice one lime and halve the slices.  Use as garnish.

name: Standard Vichyssoise (serves eight)
instructions:
1. Saut
3 chopped leeks (white part only) and 1 medium onion in 2 T. butter for 5 min.  Add 4 medium, thinly sliced potatoes and 4 C. chicken stock, then simmer 15 minutes or until tender.
2..  Put vegetables through a food mill or blender until smooth, then add 1.5 C. cream,  chopped watercress or chives, salt and pepper to taste.  Serve hot or very cold.

name: Potato Leek Soup variations
instructions:
1.  Bacon fat can be used in place of some of the butter/fat to sauté leeks, etc.
2.  1 C. riesling wine can be added to the leeks - reduce before adding potatoes.
3.  A thyme sprig and garlic are additional spices, along with 2-3 T. of white truffle oil and a small white truffle (added at the end and cut into thin slivers).
4.  The white of the leek should be added before the green (and potatoes).

name: Zucchini-Coconut Soup (serves four)
instructions:
1. Saut
1 chopped onion and 2 cloves minced garlic in 1 t. olive oil for 5 min.  Add 4 medium, diced zucchini and cook another 5 min.  Stir in 1 T. garam masala, a pinch of cayenne, 1.5 t. kosher salt and pepper to taste.  Stir in 1.5 C. chicken broth and 1/2 C. coconut milk and bring to a boil.  Reduce heat and simmer 25 min. or until zucchini is soft.
2.  Transfer the mixture to a food processor and blend until smooth.  Return to the saucepan and heat until just hot. Divide soup into four bowls. Combine 2 t. chopped cilantro, 2 t. chopped fresh mint and 1.2 t. grated orange zest in a small bowl.  Sprinkle the soup with grated coconut and the herb mixture, and serve.

name: Lobster Bisque (serves six)
instructions:
Bring 8 C. water to a boil with 1 t. salt in a Dutch oven.  Add one live 1.5 lb. lobster, head first, cover and cook 11 minutes.  Set on a rack above a plate to catch any drippings.  Remove the tail meat and reserve, chilled.
Peel, devein and 1 lb. medium shrimp.  Add shells to the lobster water, along with 12 white peppercorns, 6 sprigs thyme and the lobster shell.  Simmer 30 minutes, then strain through a cheesecloth.
Wipe out the Dutch oven, add 2 T. butter and when frothy  sauté the shrimp until pink.  Set aside in a bowl.
Add 1 T. butter to the Dutch oven and saut
1 medium onion (diced), 1/2 C. carrots (sliced), 1/2 C. celery (sliced), 3 sprigs thyme and 1.5 t. lemon zest.   Cook over medium heat for 8 minutes, then add 1 C. dry white wine, 1/2 C. dry sherry, a bay leaf and 4 t. tomato paste.  Bring to a boil and simmer 2 minutes.  Remove to a bowl.
Return the lobster water to the Dutch oven and add 1 C. heavy cream, 1 C. chicken broth and 6 T. long grain rice.  Simmer 20 minutes until rice is done.
Working in batches, puré e the shrimp, vegetable/wine mixture and lobser-rice broth in a food processor.  Strain through cheesecloth, pressing out as much liquid as possible, into the top of a double boiler.  Add 1/4 t. cayenne and 1 t. salt.  Reheat gently in the double boiler.
Chop the lobster meat, then 6 baguette slices and rub with a clove of garlic.  Ladle the warmed broth into each of six bowls and top with a baguette slice and a small mound of the lobster meat.  Serve with the uncracked claws on the side.

name: Butternut Squash and Cider Soup
instructions:
1.  Heat a saucepan over low heat.  Add 2 shallots (peeled and minced), 2 cloves garlic (minced) and 1/2 C. water.  Cook until softened (about 2 min).
2.  Add 6 C. cubed butternut squash (peeled and seeded) and 1 C. chicken broth.  Bring to a boil and simmer until squash is soft (about 20 min).
3.  Blend the squash mixture until smooth.  Mix in 1.5 C. apple cider, 1/2 C. sour cream and 1 T. salt.  Reheat or refrigerate until needed.

name: Garlic Pepper Soup (serves six)
instructions:
Combine one large head of roasted garlic, 2 red bell peppers (roasted, peeled, seeded, and coarsely chopped), 1 each poblano, Anaheim, jalape
o and banana pepper (prepared the same way), 1 roasted onion (peeled and coarsely chopped), 1 t. kosher salt, and 1 qt. rich chicken stock.  Bring to a boil and simmer 5-10 min.  Remove from heat and cool slightly.
Puré e in a food processor until very smooth, then return to heat.  Serve with a slice of goat cheese in the centers and garnish with cilantro leaves.

name: Mushroom Bisque (serves four)
instructions:
1. Simmer 1/2 lb. fresh mushrooms and 1 chopped onion in 13 oz. chicken broth until soft.  Cool and puré e in a food processor.
2.  Return to heat and add a little white pepper, then stir in 1-1.5 C. sour cream.  Serve topped with chopped, fresh chives.

name: Squash Soup with Goat Cheese (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 400°F.  Halve 1 medium butternut squash, place cut side down on a baking sheet and bake 1 hr. (or until very soft).  Allow to cool.
2.  Scoop the flesh out of the squash and puré e in a food processor.  Add 3.5 C. chicken broth, salt and pepper to taste, and blend until smooth.
3.  Pour the puré e into a saucepan and place over medium heat.  When hot, ladle into four bowls and crumble 3 oz. goat cheese among them.  Garnish with 8 chopped fresh chives and serve.

name: Roasted Kabocha Squash Soup (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Pre-heat oven to 400°F.  Roast 4 lb. of kabocha squash (halved) in an oiled roasting pan until soft
about 1 hr.  When cool enough to handle, scrape out the flesh. (Kabucha squash are large, bumby and green).
2.  While the squash is roasting, heat 1 C. vegetable oil in a deep saucepan to 375°F.  Fry  21 sage leaves in three batches until crisp (3-5 seconds).  Drain on a paper towel.
3.  Cook 1/4 lb. pancetta (coarsely chopped) in a heavy pot over moderate heat until browned, and drain on a paper towel.  Add 1 T. olive oil to the pancetta fat and cook 1 large onion (chopped) with 2 cloves of garlic (minced) wit hstirring until fragrant (about 1 min).  Add the squash, 3.5 C. chicken broth and 3.5 C. water, then simmer 20 min.
4.  Puré e the soup in batches in a blender and transfer to a large bowl.  return to the pot and reheat, thinning with water to the desired consistency.  Stir in 1 T. red-wine vinegar, salt and pepper to taste.  Serve sprinkled with the fried sage and pancetta.

name: Polish Cabbage Soup (serves six)
instructions:
1. Simmer 1 lb. beef short ribs, 1 chopped onion, 1/2 carrot, 1/2 parsley root and 1 oz. celery root in 2 qts. water, 1 t. salt and 6 peppercorns for 1 hr.  Remove meat and strain the stock, discarding the vegetables.  Skim fat from the stock and return the meat.
2.  Peel and cube 2 large potatoes and reserve under water so as not to discolor.  Halve a head of cabbage, remove the core and cut into 1 in. squares.
3.  Dice or slice 2 onions, 1/2 carrot, 1/2 parsley root, 1 oz. celery root and 1 leek.  Sauté in 2 T. butter for 10 min, then add to the meat broth with the potatoes and and simmer 10 min.  Add the cabbage, 2 halved celery stalks, 2 bay leaves and cook another 10-15 min.  Remove the bay leaves and celery stalks, stir in 2 t. catsup and cook another 5 min.  Add 2 T. each chopped fresh marjoram, parsley and dill, cover and turn off heat.  Allow to stand for 10 min., then stir in 6 T. sour cream and serve with the meat on the side.

name: Puré e the mix in small batches, then return to low heat.
instructions: Heat 1/4 C. olive oil in a small skillet until quite hot.  Add 2 t. mustard seeds and cook until they begin to pop (only about 15 seconds).  Add 8 fresh curry leaves and cook another 5 seconds, then add to soup.

name: Winter Chowder (serves 12)
instructions:
1.  Bring 6 C. water to a boil over high heat.  Add 3 lb. mussels (well scrubbed), cover and steam 3-5 min. until the mussels open (discard any that don
t open).  Remove the mussels and save the broth.  When the mussels are cool, pick out the meat and discard the shells (optional).
2.   Cook 4 slices of bacon (quartered) in a heavy pot over high heat until rendered.  Add 2 yellow onions (minced), reduce heat and simmer with frequent stirring until onion is soft.  Add 4 medium red potatoes (diced), the mussel broth and 1/4 t. saffron threads.  Simmer 15 min.
3.  Add 4 lb. Chilean sea bass (cut into 1-in. pieces), 1.5 t. kosher salt and 1/2 t. ground pepper and simmer 5 min.  Stir in the mussel meat with 4 C. heavy cream.  Adjust seasoning and simmer another 5 min.  Stir in 1/4 C. chopped fresh basil and serve.

name: Matzoh Ball Soup (serves 12)
instructions:
1.  Combine  8 qts. water, two 4-5 lb. chickens (quartered), 2 turkey wings, 2 whole yellow onions (with skin), 2 carrots, 2 stalks of celery, 1 bay leaf, 1.5 T. kosher salt and 1/2 t. peppercorns in a large stockpot.  Simmer over low heat for 3 hr., frequently skimming off the fat.
2.  Remove and cool the chicken.  Strain the broth, discarding the remaining ingredients, and refrigerate overnight.  When the chicken is cool, pick off the meat and refrigerate.
3.  Combine 10 eggs (lightly beaten) with 2 C. matzoh meal.  Stir in 2.5 T. kosher salt, 2 t. ground pepper, 1/4 C. seltzer water, 2 T. chicken broth and 1/2 C. melted butter or chicken fat.  Mix well, cover and chill for 30 min. in the refrigerator.
4.  Bring a large pot of water to a boil.  Form the matzoh mixture into 2-T. sized balls and simmer, turning frequently, for 40 min.  Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a cookie tray.
5.  Remove the stock from the refigerator and discard any fat on the top.  Place in a large pot and bring to a boil.  Reduce to a simmer, add the matzoh balls and cook for 5 min.  Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
6.  Chop the chicken meat into bite-sized pieces, add to the broth and cook another 5 min.  Ladle into bowls and serve immediately.

name: Gazpacho  (serves six to eight)
instructions:
Bring a pot of water to a boil.  Place four large tomatoes in the water for 1 minute.  Remove and allow to cool, then grate through a strainer into a large bowl, discarding the skin and seeds.
Peel, seed and coarsely chop two large cucumbers.  Core, seed and chop 1 red pepper and 2 jalape
os (the peppers can be used either raw or with their skins charred off).  Place in a food processor with 2 cloves of garlic (crushed), 1/2 C. olive oil, 2 slices of well-dried toast (grated), 1/4 C. red or white wine vinegar, 1 T. kosher salt and 1/4 C. chopped cilantro.  Pue
e until very smooth.  Combine with the tomato sauce and chill at least 2 hours in the refrigerator.  Serve with croutons and sprigs of cilantro on top.
s Carrot Soup  (serves six to eight)
1.  Combine 2 C. carrots (peeled and thinly sliced), 2 T. butter, 1/2 t. salt, 1 t. sugar, and 1/2 C. water in a medium saucepan.  Cook over low heat 15-20 minutes until tender.  Set aside.
2.  Melt 2 T. butter in a saucepan then add 2 T. flour and cook 2 minutes.  Gradually stir in 2.25 C. chicken broth and cook until thickened and bubbly.  Stir in 1/2 t. salt, 1/8 t. pepper and dill to taste.
3.  Blend carrots and white sauce together.  Add 1/2 C. milk, blend and re-warm.

name: Ecuadoran Potato Stew  (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Combine 2 t. annatto seeds (from a latino market) with 2 T. vegetable oil in a small saucepan over low heat.  Swirl gently until oil is bright red (~2 minutes) then remove and allow to stand ten minutes.
2.  Peel 3.5 lb. russet potatoes and cut into 3/4-in. pieces.  Place half in a bowl under water, and add the other half to a large heavy pot.  Strain in the annetto oil, add 1 C. chopped onion and cook over medium high heat until softened (3-5 minutes).  Add 1/2 t. cumin (or, alternatively, chile powder) and cook another minute.  Add 7 C. water and bring to a boil, scraping up the browned bits from the bottom.  Cook 20-30 minutes.
3.  Run the cooked vegetables through a potato masher or food mill and return to the water.  Add the remaining potatoes and simmer another 20 minutes.
4.  Stir in 1 C. whole milk, 5.5 oz. coarsely grated queso fresco (from a latino market
or, alternatively, ricotta salata) and bring to a simmer with stirring.  Remove from heat and season with salt and pepper.  Top stew with 2 diced ripe avocados  and serve immediately.

name: Adobo Sauce
instructions: Grind together the following (for chicken, pork or beef to serve four):  2.5 T. olive oil, 2 T. sweet paprika, 1.5 T. dried oregano (crumbled), 1 T. minced garlic, 1 t. ground cumin, 1.5 t. hot red-chile flakes, 1.5 t. finely grated lime zest, 1.5 t. kosher salt and 1/4 t. ground black pepper.  Apply to meat and grill.

name: North African Paste
instructions:
1. Grind together the following (for chicken, pork or beef to serve four): 2 T. coriander seeds, 1 T. caraway seeds, 1 t. cumin seeds,  2 T. minced garlic, 1 t. ground cumin, 1.5 t. hot red-chile flakes, 1.5 t. finely grated lime zest, 2 t. kosher salt and 1 T. black peppercorns. Mix in 5 T. olive oil, apply to meat and grill.
**** London Broil
1.  Combine 1/2 C. olive oil, 1/2 C. soy sauce, 1/4 C. balsamic or red wine vinegar, 8 minced cloves of garlic and 4 T. chopped pickle mix (this is just chopped onion, carrot, jalape
o and habanero peppers soaked in plain vinegar for at least two weeks).  Season a 2-in. thick London broil or sirloin steak generously with black pepper and marinade overnight in the refrigerator.
2.  Grill or broil the steak the next day.  Serve with warmed up marinade.

name: Churrasco Steak (serves 4)
instructions:
Combine 1. C. chopped parsley, 1 C. olive oil, 3 T. sherry vinegar, 1.5 t. cayenne,  6-8 minced cloves of garlic, 1.5 t. black pepper, a dash of cumin and 1/2 t. kosher salt.  Mix wel land refrigerate.  This is chimichurri.
Pour 2/3 of the chimichurrri into a sealable plastic bag with two 1-lb. skirt steaks.  Marinate at least one hour, then grill to desired doneness (they will cook fast).  Season with salt and pepper to taste, and serve with remaining chimichurri sauce.

name: Smoked Brisket
instructions:
1.  Ignite 1 part cherry wood chips and 2 parts apple wood chips in a smoker (do not use lighter fluid).
2.  In a medium bowl, combine 3 T. dry steak seasoning (McCormick
s Grill Mates Montreal or similar brand), 1 T. black pepper, 1 T. white pepper, 1 T. cayenne, 1 T. dried thyme, 1 T. garlic powder, 2 T. meat tenderizer and 3/4 C. dark brown sugar.  Rub the mixture into an 8 lb. brisket (top fat trimmed).  Coat it well, but reserve some of the rub for later.
3.  Place brisket in a 250
F smoker and cook uncovered for 2-4 hr.  Baste every 30 min. alternately with apple juice,
and/or honey.
4.  Recoat the brisket with more rub and add your favorite barbecue sauce.  Wrap the brisket in cellophane and then foil to keep the juices in.  Return to the smoker and cook another 12 hr.
5.  Unwrap the brisket, baste with barbecue sauce return to the smoker, uncovered, for a final 2 hr., basting with the other liquids one time each.

name: Steak and Tomato Kebabs with Avocado Cream (serves six)
instructions:
1. Pit and peel 2 avocados, and place in a food processor.  Add a 2-in. piece of seedles cucumber (peeled and diced), 1/4 C. sour cream, 1/4 C. thinly sliced scallions, 1/4 C. fresh dill (chopped), a few dashes of Tobasco sauce, juice from 1 lime and 1/4 C. water.  Blend till smooth then add kosher salt to taste.  Refrigerate, but bring to rm T before serving.
2. In a small bowl, mix together 1 t. mince garlic, 1 t. dry mustard, 2 t. kosher salt, 1 t. chile powder, 1/2 t. paprika, 1/2 t. ground coriander and 1/2 t. ground cumin.
3.  Cut 2-lb. top sirloin into 1.5-in. cubes.  Place in a medium bowl and coat with the dry rub, then place on skewers with cherry tomatoes (18-24 total) in between.
4.  Brush the cooking grate with olive oil and grill the kabobs over medium heat, turning once (~4 min on a side).  Serve warm with the avocado sauce and beer to drink.

name: Mango-Beef Kabobs (serves four)
instructions:
1. Pare and remove the seed from 1 small, ripe mango, then puré e in a food processor until smooth.
2.  Saut
1 chopped onion and 1 minced clove of garlic in 2 T. vegetable oil.  Add the mango, 2 T. brown sugar, 1 T. curry powder, 2 T. vinegar, 1/2 t. salt and 6 drops Tabasco sauce.  Stir in 1 lb. boneless sirloin steak, cut into 1.5-in. cubes.  Cover and refrigerate at least 24 hr.
3.  Remove beef from the marinade and alternate on skewers with pieces of banana (2 large bananas total) and fresh pineapple.  Grill over medium heat 7-8 min. on a side.  Serve with the marinade (reheated in the microwave) and rice.

name: Asian Barbecued Beef (serves 4)
instructions:
1.  Thinly slice 1.5 lb. rib steak (boneless and trimmed of fat).  Marinate in a mixture of 1/2 C. soy sauce, 1/2 C. water, 1/4 C. sugar (dissolved), 1 T. sake, 1 T. minced scallion, 1 t. minced garlic and 1 t. sesame oil. for 1 hr. at room T., stirring occasionally.
2.  Place  a doubled layer of heavy aluminum foil over a medium-hot grill, poking holes through the foil in several places.  Grill the meat until nicely browned, 2-3 min. per side).  Transfer to a warm platter.
3.  Mix 1/2 C. soybean paste with 1/4 C. water and place in a bowl. Place several leaves of lettuce on a plate and top with 2 thinly sliced jalape
os (or asian hot peppers) and 2 very thinly sliced cloves of garlic.  Serve with the meat and rice.

name: Pakistani Seekh kebabs
instructions:
1.  Combine 6 T. yogurt, 2 T. mint leaves, 1/4 C. cilantro leaves and stems, 1 T. lemon juice, 1/2 t. sugar and 1/4 t. salt in a blender pulse until smooth.  Refrigerate until needed (preferably 2 days or longer).
2.  In a medium bowl, combine 1 lb. fatty ground chicken thighs, 1 egg yolk,  1/2 minced onion, 1/8 C. minced ginger, 2 cloves garlic (minced), 1 T. tomato paste, 1/2 t. each cumin seeds, ground black pepper, garam masala and turmeric, 1/8 C. minced cilantro, and cayenne and salt to taste.  Form into kebabs on a skewer and grill.  Serve with the chutney above on pita bread (warmed on grill) with lemon wedges.

name: Adana turkish kebabs
instructions:
1.  In a medium bowl, combine 1 lb. fatty ground ground lamb, 1/2 minced onion,  1/4 C. minced parsley, 1/8 C. minced red bell pepper, 1/2 t. paprika, 1 t. crushed red pepper flakes, 1 clove garlic (minced), 1 T. white coriander seeds (crushed), and 1 t. salt.  Form into kebabs on a skewer and grill.
2.  Combine 1 C. yogurt, 1 t. minced garlic, 1 T. olive oil, 1 cucumber (peeled, halved, seeded and sliced), 12 mint leaves (cut into ribbons) and 1/4 t. salt in a blender pulse until smooth.  Refrigerate.  Serve on kebabs on warmed pita bread.

name: Syrian beef kebabs
instructions:
1.  In a medium bowl, combine 1 lb. fatty ground beef, 2 T. tomato paste, 1/3 C. minced onion, 1 t. lemon juice, 1/4 t. cinnamon, dash of cayenne, 1/2 T. allspice, and 2 T. pine nuts.   Knead well into a paste, then form into individual kebabs on a skewer (3-4 kebabs to a skewer).  Place on a sheet pan and refrigerate at least one hour, then  grill over medium heat untl well-browned.
2.  Toss 3 C. chopped romaine lettuce with one cucumber (chopped, halved ,seeded and sliced), 1.5 T. olive oil, 1 t. lemon juice and salt/pepper to taste.  Serve with the grilled kebabs on pita bread (warmed on the grill).

name: Vietnamese pork kebabs
instructions:
1.  In a medium bowl, combine 1 lb. fatty ground pork (like pork shoulder), 2 stalks lemon grass (white and pale green part only, minced), 2 minced shallots, 1 clove of garlic (minced), 1 T. fish sauce, 1 T. sugar, 1/2 t. turmeric, and 1/2 t. ground pepper, and knead well into a paste.  Place on skewers and grill over medium heat.
2.  Mince 1 serrano chile and 1 clove garlic.  Combine with 3 T. sugar and mash until a fine paste.  Transfer to a bowl and add 2/3 C. water, 1 T. lime juice, 1 T. rice vinegar and 5 T. fish sauce.  Refrigerate until needed.
To serve, place kebabs on lettuce leaves with fresh mint and cilantro sprigs and top with the nuoc cham sauce.

name: Buffalo Wings (serves 4)
instructions:
1.  Combine 1 C. hot sauce (Tabasco, habanero, etc.), 1/2 C. fresh lime juice, 1/2 C. soy sauce and ground black pepper to taste.  Place in a plastic bag with 2 lb. chicken wings (tips removed) and marinate overnight in the refrigerator.
2.  The next day, grill 2-3 minutes to a side til golden brown.  Serve with Ranch dressing.
s Jerk Chicken (serves eight)
Combine 14 scallions (finely chopped, including the tender greens), 2 scotch bonnet chiles (chopped, including seeds), 1/3 C. red wine vinegar, 3 T. soy sauce, 1.5 T. Trinidad-style yellow hot-pepper sauce, 1/2 C. (???) ground allspice, 2 T. grated fresh ginger, 1/4 t. grated nutmeg, 1 t. ground cinnamon, 2 T. vegetable oil, 1 T. kosher salt and 1 t. ground pepper in a food processor and puree until smooth.
Combine the juice from 3 limes with 1 T. kosher salt.  Rub 8 chicken legs with the mixture.  Marinate the legs in the jerk overnight in the refrigerator.
Lightly brush the cooking grate with vegetable oil.  Grill the chicken legs indirectly over medium heat for ~40 min, turning occasionally.  Serve warm.

name: Grilled Lamb with White Bean Salad (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Puree 1 small yellow onion (chopped), 6 minced cloves of garlic, 4 chopped plum tomatoes, 1/2 C. chopped fresh parsley, 1/2 C. fresh rosemary, 2 T. Dijon mustard, 1 C. dry red wine, 1 t. kosher salt and 1/2 t. pepper in a food processor.  Pour the mix out into a large bowl.
2.  Trim the fat from two racks of lamb (1.5-2 lb. each) and cut the meat away from the ends of the ribs (
).  Marinate in the puree for 4-6 hrs.
3.  Bring 1 C. dried cannellini beans to a boil in 4 C. water.  Cover, remove from the heat and allow to rest for 1 hr.  Drain and rinse the beans, then return to the pot with 4 C. chicken broth, 1 small yellow onion (quartered), 1 carrot and 1 celery stalk (each in 2-in. pieces), 1.5 t. dried oregano, and pepper to taste.  Bring to a boil, then simmer and cook until beans are tender (1.5-2 hr.).  During the last 10 min of cooking, season with kosher salt to taste.
4.  Remove and discard the onion, celery and carrot - save stock for another day.  Drain the beans and transfer to a medium bowl.  Add 1/4 C. extra-virgin olive oil, 1 T. red wine vinegar, 2 T. fresh minced parsley, 1 C. fresh, diced tomato and 1/2 C. thinly sliced black or green olives.  Mix well and serve at room T.
5.  Remove the lamb from the marinade and cover bones with aluminum foil to prevent charring.  Brush the cooking grate with vegetable oil and cook lamb directly over medium heat, turning once (10-15 min on a side).  Allow to rest for 10 min, then cut into chops and serve warm with the bean salad.
oise (serves four)
1.  Vinaigrette
whisk 3/4 C. extra-virgin olive oil, 1/4 C. sherry vinegar, 2 T. minced shallots, 1.5 T. finely chopped fresh oregano, kosher salt and pepper to taste.
2. Hard-boil four eggs and quarter lengthwise. Boil six red potatoes ~10 min (till just tender) and halve when the potatoes have cooled.  Boil 10 oz. green beans for 2-3 min, then drain and cool.
3.  Place 4 sushi-grade tuna steaks and the potatoes in a bowl and coat well with some of the vinaigrette.  Grill the potatoes over medium heat, turning once, until golden.  Grill the tuna directly over medium-high heat 2 min on a side (for rare).  Cut the tuna across the grain into 1/4-in. slices.
4.  Arrange butter lettuce leaves on plates (or in bowl) with 12-16 Ni
oise olives and 10-15 pear tomatoes.  Arrange the eggs, beans and potatoes on the side and fan the tuna slices across the top.  Top with vinaigrette and serve at room T.

name: Prosciutto Shrimp (appetizers for four)
instructions:
1.  Combine 16 jumbo shrimp (raw, peeled and deveined) with 1 T. minced garlic, 1.5 T. chopped fresh dill an 1/4 t. pepper in a medium bowl and mix well.
2.  Cut prosciutto slices in half (lengthwise), wrap around each shrimp and place on skewers in fours.
3.  Grill directly over medium heat, turning once, until shrimp has just turned opaque (about 2 min on a side).  Serve warm or at room T.

name: Required: an untreated cedar plank approximately 16 in. by 8 in.
instructions:
1.  Fully immerse the plank in water and soak for at least 1 hr.  Rinse well.
2.  Place a whole salmon fillet, w/ skin, onto a baking sheet with sides.  Mix 1/4 C. white wine, finely shredded zest from one lemon, 1/3 C. chopped fresh dill, kosher salt & pepper to taste.  Pour mixture over salmon, cover w/ plastic wrap and chill 30 min.
Remove salmon and place on the plank skin side down.  Grill indirectly over high heat until just opaque at the thickest part (~ 30 min.).  Serve w/ sauvignon blanc.

name: Tuscan Artichokes (serves six)
instructions:
Cut 3 mdeium artichokes into quarters, lengthwise, droppin immeidately into a bowl of water with the juice from one lemon.  Cut out the hairy choke part of each piece.
Preheat oven to 450°F.  Mix 1/2 C. olive oil, 3 T. white wine vinegar, 1/4 C. white wine, 3 cloves of garlic (thinly sliced), 1 T. chopped parsley, 1 T. chopped thyme, kosher salt and pepper to taste in  a large, oven-proof saut
pan.  Arrange the artichokes in one layer, face down, and bring to a boil over high heat for about 30 seconds.  Place immediately in the oven and roast for about 15 min, turning once so the other cut side of the artichokes are cooked.  Drain and allow to cool to rom T.
Grill over high heat 4-5 min on a side until browned.  Serve warm.

name: Grilled Pizza (serves four)
instructions:
1.  In a medium bowl, combine 1 pkg. yeast, 1/2 t. sugar and 1 C. warm water and allow to stand until foamy.  Add 2 C. flour, 1/4 C. white cornmeal, 3 T. whole wheat flour,  2 t. kosher salt, 1 t. pepper and 2 T. olive oil.  Combine with fingers, then transfer to a clean surface and knead until smooth (~10 min).  Shape into a ball, place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap and allow to stand in a warm place until the dough doubles in size (1-2 hr).
2.  Punch down the dough, divide into quarters and transfer to a lightly floured work surface.  Roll out each piece of dough into an 8-in. round (~1/8-in. thick), lightly brush each side with olive oil and place on a baking sheet.
3.  Grill the crusts over medium heat until grill marks appear (~2 min).  Return crusts to the  baking sheet, cooked side up, and baste with olive oil.
4.  Top the pizzas and return to the grill, cooking indirectly with frequent rotating until the cheese has melted.

name: Grilled chicken breasts (2)
instructions:
3/4 C. kalamata olives
4 C. fontina cheese
sliced tomato
1 C. thinly slice red onion
2 T. chopped fresh parsely
1/2 C. shredded fontina cheese
8 basil leaves
2 T. freshly grated Pecorino cheese
6 T. chopped tomatoes

name: Garlic-roasted Potatoes on Rosemary Skewers (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Halve 1 lb. red or white new potatoes.  Coat well with 2-3 T. extra-virgin olive oil mixed with 1 T. minced garlic, kosher salt and pepper to taste.
2.  Strip (and save for other uses) most the leaves off  6 sturdy rosemary branches, leaving some leaves at the thinner end.  Finely chop 2 T. of the rosemary leaves, add to the potatoes and toss well.
3.  Thread the potato halves onto the skewers, spreading evenly, and grill on medium heat 25-30 min until theskins are crisp.
4.  Reduce 1/3 C. balsamic vinegar to ~2 T. over high heat.  Layer ~1/3 C. olive oil on a serving plate, add salt and pepper to taste, then add the reduced vinegar.  Place the potatoes on the plate, turning once to coat, and serve immediately.

name: Vegetable Kabobs
instructions:
1.  Combine 1/2 C. commercial Italian dressing (oil-free), 1 t. parsley flakes and 1 t. dried basil in a small bowl.  Cover and chill.
2.  Alternate squash, cherry tomatoes, zucchini, onions and mushrooms on kabobs.  Grill over medium coals, basting with the dressing mixture.  Serve with rice.

name: Deb's no-fail bread
instructions:
1.  Mix 2 pkg yeast with 2 C. warm water and allow to sit 5 min.
2.  Add 3 C. flour and mix well.  Blend in 1/2 C. sugar, 1 t. salt, one egg, and 2/3 C. shortening.
3.  Knead in just enough flour to make the dough manageable and let rise (covered) in a greased bowl until doubled in volume.
4.  Punch down and let rise again.  After second rising, either:
a.  Place in each of two greased bread pans, let rise (covered) for ~ 2 hr. and bake 	45 min at 350°F;
or:	b.  make into rolls, place on a greased cookie sheet, let rise (covered) for 20 min. 	and bake 20 min. at 375
s No-Knead Bread
1.  Microwave 1 5/8 C. water for 40 seconds.  Add 1/4 t. yeast and a pinch of sugar, mix wel land let stand 10 minutes.
2.  In a large bowl combine 3 C. flour, and 2 t. salt.  Add yeast mixture and stir until blended (dough will be sticky).  Cover with plastic wrap and let stand 12-18 hr at warm room temperature, about 70 degrees.
3. Dough is ready when its surface is dotted with bubbles. Lightly flour a work surface and place dough on it; sprinkle it with a little more flour and fold it over on itself once or twice. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let rest about 15 minutes.
4. Using just enough flour to keep dough from sticking to work surface or to your fingers, gently and quickly shape dough into a ball. Generously coat a cotton towel (not terry cloth) with flour, wheat bran or cornmeal; put dough seam side down on towel and dust with more flour, bran or cornmeal. Cover with another cotton towel and let rise for about 2 hours. When it is ready, dough will be more than double in size and will not readily spring back when poked with a finger.
5. At least a half-hour before dough is ready, heat oven to 450 degrees. Put a 6- to 8-quart heavy covered pot (cast iron, enamel, Pyrex or ceramic) in oven as it heats. Carefully slide your hand under towel and turn dough over into pot, seam side up (it may look like a mess, but that is O.K. Shake pan once or twice if dough is unevenly distributed).  Cover with lid and bake 30 minutes, then remove lid and bake another 15 to 30 minutes, until loaf is beautifully browned. Cool on a rack.
For pumpernickel bread use 1.5 C. whole wheat flour and 1.5 C. rye flour, plus 1/4 C. unsweetened cocoa, 2 T. sugar, 1.5 T salt, 1 T. caraway seeds, 1/4 C. molasses and 1/4 C. butter. Prepare as above.

name: Potato Onion Bread (serves six).
instructions:
1.  Boil 1/2 lb. potatoes.  Mash well and allow to cool.
2.  Dissolve 1 pkg. yeast in 1 C. warm water.  Add 1.5 T.honey, 2.5 T. dry milk powder, 2 t. salt, 1.5 T. vegetable oil,  potatoes and 3 oz. minced onion.  Stir in 1.5 C. flour and mix well.
3.  Add 2. C. more flour, turn out and knead well.  Place in a lightly oiled bowl and cover with a wet towel.  Put in a warm place and allow to rise for an hour or two.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F.  Punch down dough, divide in half and knead each half with enough flour to allow it to hold a round shape.  Continue kneading, rolling the edge under to make a mushroom-cap shape.
5.  Place both ball on a buttered baking sheet and let rise (lightly covered with plastic wrap) in a warm place for 30 min.  Brush the loaves with an  egg wash (1 egg beaten with 2 t. water).
6.  Make three 1/4 in. slices in each round and bake 40 min.  Cool 1 hr. before slicing.

name: Sourdough Starter
instructions:
1.  Dissolve 1 T. sugar in 1/2 C. warm water.  Add 1 pkg active dry yeast (NOT EXPIRED) and let stand 10 min.
2.  Stir in 2 C. warm water and 2 C. all-purpose flour.  Mix until smooth, then cover with cheesecloth and plastic wrap.
3.  Allow to stand at room T., stirring 3 or 4 times a day.  It should sour in 5-10 days, and then can be kept refrigerated, sealed, for a month or more.
4.  To use, bring to room T., add 2 C. water, 2 C. flour and 2 T. sugar.  Mix well and allow to stand for two hours.  Use half of this, and refrigerate half for use next time.

name: Traditional Scones
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 425°F.  Combine 2.5 C. flour, 1 T. baking powder, 1/2 t. salt and 1/4 C. sugar in a large bowl.  Cut in 8 T. cold butter with a fork or food processor until uniformly crumbly.
2.  Mix in 2/3 C. milk and mix into the dough.  Form into a bowl and knead 10-12 times.
3.  Cut dough in half.  Pat each half into a 6-in. circle on a lightly floured surface and cut into 1/6ths.  Place wedges on a cookie sheet, separated slightly, and bake 12 minutes, or until medium brown.

name: Cheese Scones (makes 3 dozen)
instructions:
1.  Pre-heat oven to 400°F.  Mix 2.5 C. sharp cheddar cheese with a little chopped dill and set aside.
2.  Mix 4 C. flour, 2 T. baking powder and 1.5 t. salt.  Cut in 3/4 lb. butter with a food processor, then add 4 beaten eggs, 1 C. heavy cream (less 1 T.) and the cheese.  Mix well (but lightly - do not overmix).
3.  Transfer to a well-floured work surface.  Pat and roll into a 12-in. x 12-in. square 1-in. thick.  Mix 1 egg yolk with the remaining 1 T. cream and brush the dough.  Cut into 4-in. squares, then each square into quarters on the diagonal.  Place the triangles on a greased baking sheet ~2 in. apart and bake 20 min. until golden.

name: Buttermilk biscuits
instructions:
Preheat oven to 425°F.  Grease a metal cookie sheet.  Combine 2. C. flour, 2 t. baking powder, 1/2 t. baking soda, 1/2 t. cream of tartar, and 1 T. sugar.  Cut in 1/2 C. vegetable shortening, then add 2/3 C. buttermilk.  Combine well.
Roll out into a 1-in. thick sheet on a lightly floured surface and cut into 2-inch circles.  Bake 15-20 minutes, until browned.

name: Port/Mustard Sauce
instructions:
1.  Saut
3 T. minced shallot in 2 T. butter over moderately low heat, stirring, until softened.  Add 1/3 C. tawny port and 2/3 C. dry red wine, and boil the mixture until it is reduced by two thirds.
2.  Add 1 C. beef broth and boil the mixture until it is reduced by half.  Strain the mixture through a fine sieve into a small saucepan. Whisk in 1.5 t. Dijon mustard, bring the mixture to a boil, and add 2 T. beurre manie (1 T. each flour and softened butter, kneaded together), a little at a time, whisking until the sauce is smooth. Simmer the sauce, whisking occasionally.  Season with salt and pepper to taste.

name: Port and Stilton Sauce
instructions: 1.  Blend 4 oz. Stilton cheese and 6 T. mascarpone cheese with 3 T. port in a food processor or blender. Heat gently in a saucepan until melted and bubbling; add 3 T. warmed pine nuts and seasoning to taste.

name: Port Wine Sauce
instructions: 1.  Deglaze a roasting pan (beef or bird) 1ith 1 C. port, heating over medium-high heat and scraping loose browned bits on the bottom of the baking pan. Transfer to a saucepan and bring to a boil, stirring frequently until sauce is reduced to the consistency of heavy cream.  Remove from heat. Just before serving, whisk in butter until blended.

name: Port Sauce
instructions:
1.  In a saucepan bring 1 minced shallot, 3/4 cup blackberries (or blueberries), 2 C port and 1 T. sugar to a boil.  Reduce mixture to 1/2 cup, then strain and set liquid aside.
2.  Bring 2 C. beef stock to a boil in a separate pan to reduce by half.  Whisk blackberry and Port wine reduction into reduced beef stock, thickening with 1 tbsp cornstarch disolved in water if necessary.  Whisk in 1 tbsp softened butter.
Note: can use orange with orange zest instead of blackberries.

name: Shiitake Gravy
instructions:
1.  Mix 0.5 C. flour with 0.5 C. dry sherry in a small bowl.  Over a low flame.
2.  Melt 3 T. butter over medium-high heat and saut
12 oz. stemmed and sliced shiitake muchrooms with 1 t. chopped fresh rosemary for 5 min..
Remove drippings from roasting pan and add canned chicken broth to give 5 C.  Add to mushrooms along with flour paste and whisk smooth.  Bring to a boil with regular stirring and simmer 10 min or until thickened.

name: Blue Cheese Dressing (serves eight)
instructions:
Combine 2 T. vinegar, 10 T. olive oil, salt and pepper to taste with 1/2 lb. blue cheese (at room T).  Combine well, crumbling the cheese into small pieces.Spicy Mayonnaise
Beat 1 T. Dijon mustard, 1 egg yolk, and 2 T. white-wine vinegar in a mixing bowl with a whisk.
Slowly add 2 C. vegetable oil, beating constantly.  Add salt and pepper to taste.

name: Deglaze the roasting pan with 1 C. tawny port (or just heat in a saucepan).
instructions:
Add 4 T. flour to 1/4 C. fat (from roasting pan or butter) and brown.  Add port mixture slowly), alongwith 4 C. giblet stock or chicken broth.  Heat for 10 min.
Add 2 T. fresh lemon juice and serve in a gravy boat.

name: Hollandaise/Bearnaise Sauce (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Melt 1 stick of butter in a double boiler.
2.  Mix well 3 egg yolks, 1 T. lemon juice, a dash of cayenne and a little salt.  Blend into the butter with constant stirring and continue until thickened.
To make a bearnaise sauce, use 1 minced shallot (pressed dry in a paper towel), 1 t. chopped parsley and 1/2 t. chopped terragon instead of the lemon juice in the Hollandaise recipe.
A
oli (serves 12)
1.  Mince 6-8 cloves of garlic and press with 1/2 t. kosher salt until smooth.
2.  Add 3 egg yolks and blend well.  SLOWLY blend in 1.5 C. olive oil with constant beating to make an emulsion.  When all the oil is added, add 1 T. lemon juice.  Serve with fish or shellfish, or on toast.

name: Saffron-Orange A
instructions:
oli
Stir 1/8 t. crumbled saffron threads with 1 T. hot water in a small bowl.
Transfer to a food processor with 1/4 C. fresh orange juice, 1 T. fresh lemon juice, 3/4 t. salt, 1/4 t. black pepper and 1 egg yolk.  Pulse until mixed well.
With motor running, add 3/4 C. olive oil in a thin steady stream.  Make sure the oil emulsifies as it is added.  Transfer to a bowl, cover and chill until used.

name: Vinaigrette (serves six)
instructions: 1.  Dissolve 1 t. kosher salt in 2 T. red wine vinegar.  Add 1/4 t. pepper andwhisk in 1/2 C. olive oil.  Options: add some minced onion or garlic, or chopped basil or terragon.

name: Sauce Mornay (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Melt 2 t. butter with 1.5 t. flour.  Cook for 2-3 min, then add 1/8 t. cayenne, 1/8 t. nutmeg, salt and pepper to taste.
2.  Remove from heat and add 1/2 C. grated Gruyere cheese.  Stir until melted.

name: Spirit ham/pork glaze
instructions: 1.  Combine 1 C. red wine, 1 C. bourbon whiskey, 1 C. brown sugar 6 bruised cloves and 2 T. orange zest.  Baste roasting ham or pork tenderloin occasionally with mixture.

name: Alfredo sauce
instructions: 1.  Melt 1/2 C. butter with 2/3 C. heavy cream until butter is melted.  Remove from heat and add 1 C. grated Parmesan cheese, salt and pepper to taste, then stir until blended and fairly smooth.  If necessary, reheat BUT BE CAREFUL not to curdle the mixture.

name: Red wine reduction
instructions:
1.  In a medium saucepan, saut
1 Granny Smith apple (chopped), 2 cloves of garlic (crushed), 1 stalk celery (chopped), 1 carrot (chopped), 1 Spanish onion (chopped), one shallot (chopped) in 2-3 T. olive oil until caramelized.  Add 1 bottle burgundy (or other dry red) wine and 2 C. ruby port, then reduce over medium heat to ~ 1 C.
2.  Strain the reduction, then add 1 C. chicken stock.  Whisk in 4 T. butter, 1 T. at a time, until thickened.  Season with salt and pepper to taste, and add a little Roquefort cheese, if desired.
**** Apple pie
1.  Prepare pie crust per pastry sheet and chill.
2.  Peel and slice 6-8 Golden Delicious, Granny Smith or other cooking apples.
3.  Mix apple slices with 1/2 C. sugar, 3 T. lemon juice, cinnamon, black currants and nutmeg to taste.
4.  Roll out half of pastry and place in a 10" pie plate.  Mix 2 T. each flour and sugar in bottom and cover with apple mixture.  Top with pats of butter.
5.  Put on top crust, sealing edges with water.  Cut vents and baste with a mixture of milk and eggwhite.  Bake 50 min at 425

name: Grease a springform pan with butter and shake in 3 T. fresh breadcrumbs.
instructions:
Cream 2/3 C. sugar with 12 T. butter,  Add 2 eggs and beat until very smooth.  Add 6 oz. Maillard sweet chocolate (melted in a double boiler), 1/2 C. ground almonds,
2 C. flour and mix well.
add a third egg and mix thoroughly.  Pour into the springform pan.
Press sour cherries (24 oz. jar, drained) into the batter in concentric circles.
Bake 50 min at 350°F, remove and allow to cool.
Heat 1/2 C. cream to boiling.  Add 2 T. instant coffee and 8 oz. of chocolate and mix well.  Allow to cool until spreadable, then top tort, chill and reglaze.

name: Pumpkin Pie
instructions:
1.  Make a standard 9
pie crust.
2.  Preheat oven to 435°F.  In a large bowl, combine 16 oz. canned pumpkin, 3/4 C. sugar,1/2 t. salt, 1 t. cinnamon, 1/2 t. ground ginger, 1/4 t. ground cloves  and 10 oz. tofu.  Mix until creamy.
3.  Pour the filling into the uncooked crust and bake for 15 min.  Lower heat to 350°F and bake for another 40 min.  Allow to cool before serving.

name: Chocolate Amaretto Cheesecake
instructions:
1.  Blend 1.5 C. cream cheese, 1 C. sugar, 1 C. cottage cehese, 1/4 C. unsweetened cocoa, 1/4 C. flour, 1/4 C. amaretto and 1/4 t. salt, then process until smooth.  Add 1 egg and process until just plended.
2.  Remove mixture from processor and mix in 2 T.  grated semisweet chocolate.    Crumble six chocolate wafers into the bottom of a 7-in. spring-form pan and gently cover with the cheesecake mixture.
3.  Refrigerate for at least 8 hr.

name: Tarte Tatin
instructions:
1.  Combine 2 C. flour and 1 t. salt in a mixing bowl.  Add 1 C. cold butter in small pieces and cut with a fork until butter is reduced to pea-sized pieces.
2.  Whisk together one egg yolk and 2 T. water.  Add to the flour mixture and mix, gently pressing the dough into a ball. Refrigerate 1/2 to 1 hr.
3.  Pre-heat oven to 375°F.  Peel, quarter and core 6 large Granny Smith apples OR peel and halve 6 firm, large pears.  Toss fruit with 2 T. fresh lemon juice and set aside.
4.  Gently heat 3/4 C. sugar in a 10 in. skillet.  When the sugar begins to melt, stir with a wooden spoon.  Continue until all the sugar has melted and begins to turn a golden color.
5.  Remove pan from heat and arrange the fruit compactly in the skillet (alternate apple wedges peeled side up and down, pears with the cut side up).  Top with pats of butter, return to the heat and cook until the sugar has caramelized and the juice nearly evaporated.
6.  Roll out half of the refrigerated pastry dough (save the other half) on a well-floured surface into a circle about 1/4 in. thick.  Trim to about 1/2 in. larger than the skillet.  Gently place the dough on top of the fruit and tuck the edges around the fruit.  Bake 25-30 min. at 375°F.
7.  Remove the tarte from the oven when the crust is browned.  Run a paring knife around the edge of the tarte to looosen it, and invert onto a serving dish.  Allow to cool somewhat, cut into wedges and serve warm.
Note:  8 bananas can be used as fruit also (cut in half then cut length-wise), in which case 1 C. brown sugar should be used for the glaze, and 1 C. toasted, chopped walnuts should be added to the glaze.
This can also be done with sliced pears, topped with toasted pecans and maple syrup.

name: Port Sauce for cheesecake
instructions:
1.  Dissolve 1/2 C. sugar in  2 C. port.  Reduce to 1 C. over medium heat.
2.  Mix 1 T. water and 2 t. cornstarch in a small bowl.  Whisk into port mixture and boil until slightly thickened.  Cool and serve (can be refrigerated).
s Mud Cake
1. Cream 2 C. sugar with 2 sticks of butter.  Mix in 1.5 C. flour, 1 t. vanilla, 1/2 t. salt, 4 eggs and 1/3 C. cocoa.  Cook 30 min. at 350°F.
2.  For icing, melt 2/3 C. butter and add 1/3 C. cocoa and 1/2 C. dry milk in a little water.  Heat thoroughly, then add powdered sugar until thick.
3.  When the cake is cool, top with icing and serve.

name: Cranberry-Mince Pie (serves six)
instructions:
In a food processor (or by hand) mix 2.5 C. flour with 6 T. chilled vegetable shortening and 6 T. chilled, unsalted butter until the mixture looks like coarse meal.
With the motor running, add 6-7 T. ice water.  Process until the dough pulls away from the sides of the processor.  Divide in two, flatten into disks between wax paper or plastic wrap, and refrigerate at least 1 hr.
Boil 4 C. whole cranberries in 2 C. water with 6 T. sugar and a pinch of salt.  Simmer uncovered for 10 min.  Set aside.
Cook 1/2 C. finely chopped venison, 1/4 C. beef suet (finely chopped), 12 oz. dark brown sugar, 1/2 C. molasses, 2 C. apple cider, 1.5 C. currants and 1.5 C. raisins over medium low heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar is dissolved (~5 min).  Add 2 apples (cored and diced) and the zest from 1 orange, and cook another 10 min.  Add 1.5 C. chopped walnuts, 1/2 C. brandy, 1/2 t. each of ground cinnamon, ground mace, nutmeg, alspice and salt, and cook another 15 min.
Roll out half of the pie crust and place in a 9-in. pie plate and refrigerate.  Pre-heat oven to 450°F.
In a bowl, combine the mincemeat, cranberry sauce and 1 apple (cored and chopped).  Spread into pie crust and cover with the other half of the dough (moisten the edges of the lower part of the crust before pressing together, so as to seal the crust).  Trim excess dough and cut a number of air vents into the top.  Roll out the trimmed dough, moisten the bottom and place on top of the pie, pressing into place.
Bake pie 10 min at 450°F, then lower temperature to 350

name: Standard Cheesecake
instructions:
1.  Mix 1/2 stick melted butter with 1.5 C. Graham cracker crumbs.  Press into a 9-in. pie plate and bake 5 min. at 350°F.
2.  Cream 9 oz. cream cheese with 1/2 C. sugar.  Add 2 eggs, 1/2 t. vanilla and mix well. Pour into crust and bake 20 min. at 350°F.  remove and allow to cool.
3.  Mix 12 oz. sour cream with 3 T. sugar, 1/2 t. cinnamon and 1/2 t. vanilla.  Spread on top of first layer and bake 5 min. at 325°F.  Chill at least 2 hr. before serving.
s Cheesecake
1.  Mix 1.5 C. Graham cracker crumbs with 1/2 stick of melted butter.  Press into a  9-in. springform pan and bake 5 min. at 350°F.
2.  Mix 1 pkg. cream cheese, 1 can sweetented condensed milk and 1/4 C. lemon juice.  Pour into the pie crust and chill.
3.  Strain juice from a can of blueberries, thicken with 2 T. cornstarch until thickened like pudding.  Add the berries and top the pie.  Refrigerate before serving.
s Pecan Pie (serves two)
1.  In a food processor (or by hand) mix 2.5 C. flour with 6 T. chilled vegetable shortening and 6 T. chilled, unsalted butter until the mixture looks like coarse meal.
2.  With the motor running, add 6-7 T. ice water.  Process until the dough pulls away from the sides of the processor.  Divide in two, flatten into disks between wax paper or plastic wrap, and refrigerate at least 1 hr.
3.  Roll out half the pie dough into a round and place in a 9-in. pie plate.
4.  Beat 3 eggs, then add 2/3 C. sugar, 1/3 t. salt, 1/3 C. melted butter and 1 C. dark Karo syrup.  Mix well, then add 1 C. pecan halves.
5.  Pour into the pie crust and bake 45 min at 375

name: Meatless Mince Pie
instructions:
Simmer 1.5 C. raisins (chopped), 4 diced granny Smith apples, the grated rind and juice from 1 orange and 1/2 C. apple cider.  Simmer until soft.
Add 3/4 C. sugar, 1/2 t. each cinnamon and cloves, and 2-3 finely crushed soda crackers.  Stir until well blended, then allow to cool.
Add to a pie crust in a 9-in. pie plate and bake 10 min. at 450°F, then 20 min. at 350
F.
Strain juice from a can of blueberries, thicken with 2 T. cornstarch over medium heat until the texture of pudding.  Add the berries and top the pie.  Refrigerate.

name: Kahlua
instructions:
1.  Dissolve 2 oz. freeze-dried coffee and 4 C. sugar in 2. C. boiling water.  Allow to cool.
2.  Add 1 C. brandy, 1 C. vodka, and a split banana bean.  Stire well, cap and age 30 days (don
t try to double the recipe).

name: Kathy
instructions:
s Tenured Cider (serves two)
1.  Simmer all day:
2 qts. apple cider
1/2 t. cinnamon
1/2 t. mint leaves
1/4 t. nutmeg
1/2 C. powdered sugar
2-3 sliced oranges with cloves inserted
2.  Add 1/2 -1 C. brandy and serve warm.

name: Margaritas
instructions:
1.  Mix in a blender:
1 C. tequila
1/2 C. lemoin or lime juice
2 T. triple sec
2 T. sugar
6-8 ice cubes

name: Garlic Cheese Grits
instructions: 1.  Preheat oven to 350°F.  Mix 3 C. cooked grits, 2 eggs, 1/4 C. milk, 3/4 C. grated Monterey Jack cheese, 1/4 C. sweet onion (minced), two cloves garlic (minces) and salt/pepper to taste.  Place in a buttered 1 1/2 qt. casserole and bake 1 hr.

name: Shrimp and Grits I (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Peel, devein and rinse 1 lb. of  large shrimp; pat dry.
2.  Dice six slices of  bacon and fry until just brown.  Drain and set aside.
3.  Add peanut oil to the bacon drippings to cover the bottom of the pan and heat well.  Saute the shrimp until just pink, then add 2 C. sliced mushrooms and saute another 3 min.  Add 1 C. sliced scallions, one crushed clove of garlic, 4 T. lemon juice, a dash of Tabasco sauce, 2 T. fresh parsley, and salt/pepper to taste; simmer another 10 min.  Serve with grits (below).
Make 1 C.  grits according to the package.  Add 1/4 t. white pepper, a pinch each of cayenne, and nutmeg, 1 C. grated cheddar cheese, 2 T. butter and salt to taste.

name: Shrimp and Grits II (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Peel, devein and rinse 1 lb. of  large shrimp; pat dry.  Add the shells to 2 C. water and simmer until reduced to 1/2 C.
2.  Saut
3/4C. chopped onion, 1/2 C. chopped green pepper, and 2 cloves garlic in 4 T. butter for 3 min.  Add 1 C. diced tomatoes and 1/2 t. dried thyme.  Bring to a simmer and cook 2-3 min.
3.  Stir in 1 T. flour and mix well, then add the shrimp and stir constantly until they turn pink (about 2 min.).  Add the strained shrimp stock, cook 2-3 min., then add 1 T. tomato paste and stir until blended.
4.  Add 1/3 C. heavy cream, 2 t. Worcestershire sauce and 2 dashes of Tabasco (and more stock if needed to coat the shrimp well).  Heat but do not allow to boil.
5.  Make 1 C.  dried grits per the package.  Add (1/4 t. white pepper, a pinch each of cayenne and nutmeg,) 1 C. grated cheddar cheese, 2 T. butter and salt to taste.

name: Potato Latkes (makes 24 latkes)
instructions:
1.  Scrub and dry 2 medium russet potatoes.  Grate and toss with 2 T. lemon juice.
2.  Add 1 small, minced onion, 4 T. matzoh meal, 2 large eggs (beaten lightly), 1/2 C. milk and salt and pepper to taste.  Mix well.
3.  Preheat oven to 200
soup-spoon sized dollops of the potato mixture in 2 T. vegetable oil until browned, ~ 8 min on a side.  Transfer to a warm platter in the oven and continue until the mixture is used up.  Serve with caviar, sour cream and smoked fish.
s Egg Scramble (serves 8)
1.  Saut
1/4 C. chopped green onion and 1 C. diced Canadian bacon (or other breakfast meat) in 3 T. butter in a large pan.  Add 12 eggs and scramble until just set.
2.  Melt 2 T. butter and add 2 T. flour, 1/2 t. salt and 1/8 t. pepper, then cook 5 min.  Add 2 C. milk with continuous stirring and mix until smooth.  Finally, mix in 1 C. grated cheddar cheese and cook until melted.
3.  Fold the cooked eggs and 1/2 C. chopped mushrooms into the cheese sauce.  Pour into a 12-in. x 7-in. baking dish and top with buttered crumbs and paprika.  Bake uncovered at 350

name: Standard Pancakes (4-6 servings)
instructions:
1.  Heat a griddle or large skillet over medium-low heat.  Combine 2 C. flour, 1 T. sugar, 2 t. baking powder, and 1/4 t. salt.  Beat 2 eggs into 1.5 C. milk, then stir in 2 T. melted (but cooled) butter.  Gently stir in dry ingredients and mix well.
2.  Place 1 T. vegetable oil on the griddle.  When it begins to shimmer ladle batter onto griddle and cook.

name: Sourdough Pancakes
instructions:
Preheat oven to 250°F.  Sift 1 C. flour, 2 T. sugar, 1.5 t. baking powder, 1/2 t. baking soda, 1/2 t. salt into a small bowl.  In a larger bowl, whisk together 2 eggs, 1 C. sourdough starter, 1/2 C. milk and 2 T. vegetable oil.  Stir in four, mix well.
Heat a heavy skillet over moderate heat - once hot baste with vegetable oil.  Add batter in 1/4 C. batches, turning after bubbles appear and
on the surface.  Repeat until the batter is used up, basting the skillet between each batch of pancakes.  Keep pancakes warm in the oven until serving.
Place 1 T. vegetable oil on the griddle.  When it begins to shimmer ladle batter onto griddle in heaping tablespoonfuls, and cook.

name: High-Maintenance Pancakes (4 servings)
instructions:
Beat together 1 C. ricotta cheese, 1 C. sour cream (or plain yogurt), and 3 egg yolks in a large mixing bowl.  Combine 1 C. flour, a dash of salt and 1 T. sugar.  Beat 3 eggwhites until fairly stiff but not dry.
Heat a griddle or large skillet over medium-low heat.  Stir the flour mixture into the cheese mixture, blending well but not beating.  Stir in 2 T. lemon juice and 2 t. lemon zest, then gently fold in the beaten eggwhites (They should remain somewhat distinct in the batter).

name: Potato Pancakes (4 servings)
instructions:
1.  In a large mixing bowl, beat 2 large eggs, then add 3 C. potatoes (grated and a large skillet over medium drained) ,1/4 C. grated onion, 1 t. salt and 1/4 t. pepper.  Mix well.  Stir in 2-4 T. matzo meal and allow to stand 30 seconds to absorb moisture (add more matzo as necessary to make batter thick enough).
2.  Place heat and add 2 T. vegetable oil.  When oil shimmers, add batter in 1/8 C-fuls.  Brown well, turn and repeat.  Drain on paper towels and add more salt to taste.

name: Buttermilk Biscuits (serves six)
instructions:
1.  3 C. flour, 1 T. baking powder, 3/4 t. cream of tartar, 1/2 t. salt, 1.5 T. sugar and 1 t. baking soda.  Cut in 1 C. shortening, then add 1 C. buttermilk and mix well.
2.  Knead briefly, then roll out, cut into biscuits, place on a well-greased baking sheet and bake at 425

name: Better Tortilla Espa
instructions:
ola (serves six)
1.  Add 2.5 C. of potatoes (peeled and diced), 1.5 C. chopped onion, 1 chopped red pepper and 2 chopped jalape
os with a little salt to 1.5 C. hot oil over medium-high heat.  Lower heat and cook vegetables until tender but not colored (~45 min.).  Drain vegetables in a large collander over a bowl and allow to cool 5 min.
2.  Lightly beat 10 large eggs and stir in vegetables with 1 t. vegetable oil, salt and pepper to taste.  Return 1 T. of oil to the pan and then add the egg mixture, pressing the potatoes flush with the eggs.  Cook over low heat for 15 min. or until almost set.  Invert tortilla onto a large flat plate and slide back into skillet cooked side up.  Cook another 15 min. until set.  Serve warm.

name: Gruyere Cheese Toast (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Melt 4 T. butter and add 6 T. flour. Cook for 3 min. then add 1 C. milk, stirring constantly.  Season with salt and pepper to taste, then add 1.5 C. dry white wine, 1/8 t. cayenne, 1/8 t. nutmeg and 1 t. minced garlic.  Stir to blend.
2.  Add 1 egg yolk and bring to a boil with constant stirring.  Add 1/2 lb. Gruyere cheese (grated), and allow to cool.
3.  Toast 6 pieces of toast., top with the cheese and broil until just browned.

name: Shortbread (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Cream 1 C. salted butter with 1/2 C. sugar.  Add 2.5 C. flour and work with hands until well-mixed and smooth.
2.  Pat out on a cookie sheet, score and mark, and bake at 300

name: Potato Pancakes (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Peel and grate 3 large potatoes, then press out on a paper towel.
2.  Place potatoes in a bowl, add 1 T. flour, 1 t. whipping cream, 1 egg and salt to taste.  Mix well.
3.  Form patties and fry over medium heat in a well-buttered skillet, ~ 5 min per side.

name: Firangi Curry
instructions:
1.  Roast 2 T. caraway seeds, 4 T. coriander seeds, 4 T. broken cinnamon bark, 2 T. whole cloves, 2 T. aniseed, 2 T. fenugreek, 4 T. cumin seeds and 2 T. cardamom pods in a dry skillet until they begin to pop (~5 min over medium heat).
2.  Grind the roasted hard seeds very fine and add 4T chile powder, 2T paprika and mix well.  This is your curry powder (Don't. buy "curry powder" in the store.  It has the turmeric already added - see below - and this must be pre-cooked to avoid an unpleasant "raw" flavor in the stew.)
3.  In a large pot, sauté one finely chopped medium onion in 3 T. corn oil until it begins to soften.  Add 2 crushed garlic cloves, 1-2" of ginger root (peeled and grated), 1 t. turmeric, two chopped jalape
os and (if you're brave) one or two chopped habanero peppers (the peppers should be stemmed and seeded).  Sauté over medium heat another 2-3 min or until the peppers are soft.
4.  Add one can of stewed tomatoes, one coarsely diced potato and 2-4 T. of the curry powder (save the rest for another time in a zip-lock bag).  Simmer.
5.  The alternatives at this point are to add either the meat of your choice (shrimp and lamb are my favorites - the lamb should be well-trimmed and browned first) or vegetables (peas, chickpeas, broccoli, eggplant, cauliflower, okra or whatever you pleasure).  The entire stew should be simmered for at least two hours.
Serve with Basmati rice (prepared with a little turmeric), and naan (see below).

name: Kapila's Vegan Curry
instructions:
1.  Whisk together 1/2 C. unprocessed peanut butter and 1/2 C. hot coconut milk to give a uniform mixture.  Add 1/4 C. cider vinegar, 2 T. tamari sauce, 2 T. blackstrap molasses and 1/4 - 1/2 t. cayenne.  Blend well.
2.  Cut of the tough lower stem of 1 bunch broccoli and shave the remaining stalk.  Cut diagonally into thin slices and coarsely chop the flowerlets.  Heat 1 T. peanut oil in a wok.  Add 1 t. freshly grated ginger root, and 2 cloves crushed garlic.  Salt lightly and sauté over medium heat for 1 min.
3.  Add 1 lb. tofu (cut into small cubes) and sauté another 5-8 min.  Mix into sauce.
4.  Wipe wok with a paper towel, add an other 2 T. oil and sauté another 1 t. grated ginger and 2 cloves crushed garlic.  Add 2 C. thinly sliced onions and pepper to taste.  Saut
until soft, then add broccoli, 1 C. coarsely chopped peanuts, 2-3 T. tamari sauce and stir-fry over medium heat until broccoli is just tender.
5.  Heat the peanut sauce over very low heat.  Pour over the saut
, toss gently, sprinkle with 2 freshly minced scallions and serve over rice.

name: George's Mom's curry
instructions:
1.  Make a paste of 1.5 t. red chile powder, 1/2 t. cumin, 1/5 t. mustard seeds..
2.  Make another paste of garlic (two whole pods), ginger (2 inches) with yoghurt.
3.  Saut
1/2 lb. thinly sliced onions in vegetable oil., add the chile paste and saut
10 min.  Add the garlic/ginger paste, then two tomatoes (finely chopped),  1 lb. cubed meat (pork or beef), turmeric and salt to taste and sauté another 15 min.
4.    Add 1.5 t. balsamic vinegar, place in a pressure cooker and cook until cooker hoots.  Turn down the heat and cook for a few more minutes. Add vinegar and/or sugar to fine tune the taste.

name: Firangi Naan
instructions:
1.  Mix 1 pkg. yeast, 1/2 C. warm water and 1 T. sugar.
2.  Mix 3 C. all-purpose flour, 1/4 C. cream of wheat (the secret ingredient, believe it or not), 1/4 C. vegetable oil, and 1/2 C. plain yogurt in a large bowl.  Add the yeast mix plus another 1/2-3/4 C. water as needed to make the dough the consistency of pizza dough.  Knead well on a floured surface.
3.  Let the dough sit in a cool place, covered with a damp cloth, for 2-3 hr.
4.  Preheat oven on "broil".  Twist off a 2" ball of dough, and roll out or stretch by hand into a thin sheet 6-8" across.  Broil on a cookie sheet or piece of heavy-duty aluminum foil on the top rack of the oven for 1 min. on each side (or until the naan puffs up and browns).  Repeat until the dough is used up.

name: Indian Lamb Patties (makes 16)
instructions:
1.   Soak 4 oz. skinned chana dal (or split yellow peas) in 1 C. water for 1 hr.  Place in a large, heavy saucepan and add 2 lb. ground lean lamb, 20 whole cloves, 2 t. peppercorns, two 3/4 in. sticks of cinnamon, 12 cardamom pods, 6 bay leaves and 2 t. salt.  Bring to a boil then cover and simmer gently for 1 hr.  Raise the heat to medium high and boil off all the liquid with continuous stirring.  Drain off any fat, but return all juices to the pan.  Remove from heat and allow to cool.
2.  Blend in a food processor to a fine paste (may take as long as 30 min).  Add 1 T. grated ginger and 2 eggs and mix well in the food processor.  Place in a mixing bowl and refrigerate overnight.
3.  Mix 1 chopped onion, 2 T. chopped mint and 2 t. very finely chopped hot, green chiles.  Divide into 16 portions.  Divide the meat into 16 portions also.  Roll each portion of meat into a ball, stuff with a portion of the onion/chile mix and seal and   flatten into a 2.5 in. patty.
4.  Heat 1/8 in. of vegetable oil in a large frying pan over a medium-high flame.  Fry patties 3-4 min on a side until slightly crisp and medium brown.  Drain, garnish with onion rings and sprigs of fresh mint, and serve with mint sauce and naan.

name: Cornish Hen Biryani (serves four)
instructions:
Lightly roast 2 t. saffron threads in a dry pan, then crumble, and add 3 T. hot milk.  Place in a small cup and allow to stand 3 hr.  Place 2 C. basmati rice in a large bowl, cover with water, add 1 t. salt and allow to stand 3 hr.
Place 1 2-in. piece of ginger (peeled andcoarsely chopped), and 5 cloves of garlic (peeled) in a blender with 2 T. water.  Blend to a smooth paste.
Place 2 C. low-fat yogurt in a bowl and mix in 1/2 C. chopped mint leaves, 1/2 C. chopped coriander and 1 or 2 jalape
os (thinly sliced).
Mix 4 2-in. sticks of cinnamon, 12 cardamom pods, 10 cloves and 2 bay leaves and divide into two equal portions.  Heat 3/4 C. vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.  Working in two batches, fry 2 medium onions (halved lengthwise and very thinly sliced) until crisp (~ 3 min. per batch).  Remove each batch with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Pour off all but 2 T. of the oil.  Add the first batch of the spices, stir briefly and add 1/2 t. cumin seeds.  Stir again and,1 finely chopped onion and cook til the edges begin to brown.  Stir in the ginger mixture in and cook for 1 min.  Add 2 Cornish hens (legs halved and breasts quartered) in 2 batches and cook until browned.
Add both hens to the skillet over low heat; stir in half the yogurt mixture, 1.5 t. salt, 1/8 C. toasted slivered almonds, 1/8. C. golden raisins, 2 T. fresh lemon juice, 1 t. ground cumin, 2 t. cayenne and pepper to taste, 1/4 C. water.  Bring to a simmer, cover and cook 10 min.  Uncover and add half the fried onions.  Cook, stirring often, until sauce is thickened and the meat just tender.  Add 1 t. salt and pepper.
Preheat oven to 25°F, and bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.  Place the hen mixture in a casserole dish.  Stir in half of the saffron milk into the remaining yogurt and spread over the hen mixture.  Drain the rice and place in boiling water with the remaining half of the spice mixture.  Boil 7-8 mion. until almost tender.  Drain rice and place over the yogurt.  Quickly make a well in the center of the rice and pour in the remaining saffron milk.  Cover with a damp dishcloth, then foil, then a lid.  Bake 30 min. Uncover and bake another 10 min.
To serve,stir the mixture to combine, place on a large platter and garnish with the remaining fried onions, 1/8 C. toasted slivered almonds and 1/8. C. gold raisins.

name: Vegetable Somosas
instructions:
1.  Crush 1 t. garlic (chopped), 1 t. coriander root (chopped) and 1 t. whole black peppercorns in a mortar to give a paste.  Add 1 T. cornstarch and 2 T. water.
2. Add 2 T. oil to a wok, then the above paste and stir-fry in order: 1 sweet potato (diced), 1 large onion (diced), 2 carrots (diced), 2/3 C. corn, 1/2 t. salt, 1 t. curry powder, 1 T. light soy sauce and 1 t. sugar.
Place a small amount of  the stir-fry on one of 4 large spring roll sheets (cut into three long strips) and fold into triangles.  Deep fry until golden brown.

name: Curried Venison
instructions:
1.  Combine 1 T.  vegetable oil with 1 T. curry powder in a bowl and use to coat 1.5 lb cubed leg of venison.  Cover and refrigerate overnight.
2.  Grind 2 minced onions, 2 minced cloves of garlic and 1 in. fresh, peeled ginger root until smooth in a mortar and pestle.
3.  Warm 1 t. oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium-low heat.  Add the onion mix and a pinch of turmeric, then cook until soft and golden (~5 min.).  Whisk 2 T. curry powder into 1/2 C. beef broth - add to onion mix with one stick  cinnamon.
Cook for 15 min, then stir in 2 C. coconut milk, 1 t. salt, 1/2 t. sugar and 8 small, peeled boiling potatoes.  Cook 25 min.  Brown the marinated venison and add to the stew.  Simmer 10 min and serve with rice.

name: Murgh Makhani I (Velvet Butter Chicken)
instructions:
1. Remove skin from 6 lb chicken breasts. Prick all over with fork. Make l slashes 1/2" deep, 1" apart on meat. Rub with 2 1/2 tsp meat tenderizer and 1/3 c lemon juice (rub into slashes). Let rest for 30 min.
2. Blend 2 cloves garlic, 1 t. toasted cumin seeds , 1 T. ginger root, 1/2 t. cardamom, 1/2 t. red pepper and 1 T. paprika into a smooth paste. Marinate chicken for 4 hours at RT or overnight in the refridgerator.  Turn several times. (chicken should be RT to cook).
3. Heat oven to 500-550. Brush chicken with ghee and roast for 25-30 min.
4. Puree 3 C tomatoes with 4 whole jalape
os and 2 T. ginger root.
5. Cut chicken into pieces, then brown 2-3 min in 8 T butter.  Add 4 T cumin and 1 T paprika and sauté another 15 sec.
6. Add puree to chicken and cook 5-8 min until begins to thicken.  Add 2 t. salt, 1.5 C. cream and stir to coat chicken. Heat at med-low until fat begins to separate (~10 min). Stir in 2 T butter, 2 t. garam masala and cumin seeds. Turn off heat, cover and let stand for 30 min. Before serving, heat and stir in 1/4 C. cilantro.

name: Murgh Makhani II (Velvet Butter Chicken)
instructions:
1.  Mix 1/4 pt. yogurt, 1 t. ginger, 1 t. salt and 1/4 t. red coloring.  Rub into 3 lb. chicken breasts, cut into pieces and marinate overnight.
2.  Place in an ovenproof dish and brush with vegetable oil.  Bake at 375°F for40-50 minutes.  Save the liquid, if any.
3.  Saut
1 stick cinnamon, 6 cloves, 6 green cardamom pods and 1 bay leaf in 2T. butter for 1 minute.  Add 1/4 pt sour cream, 1/4 t. crushed saffron, and 1/4 pt. cream.  Cover and simmer gently for 5-6 minutes.
4.  Add chicken, 2 t. ground almonds and salt to taste.  Dissolve 1/4 t. cornstarch in water and add to the chicken.  Cover and simmer for 3-4 minutes.  Remove from heat.   Serve with naan.

name: Murgh Makhani III (Velvet Butter Chicken)  Serves 4
instructions:
1.  Blend 1 T. yogurt, 1 T. lemon juice, 1 t. salt and 1/2 t. garam masala until smooth. Add 3 lb. chicken breasts (chopped in pieces) and mix well to coat the chicken. Allow to marinate for 1-2 hr. Thread the chicken onto skewers and grill over medium heat until browned on all sides. Remove from the fire and keep warm.
2.  In a ceramic mortar bowl grind 1 T. minced ginger, 1 T. minced garlic into a paste using a pestle. In a skillet over medium heat add 1 C. cream, the ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 C. tomato paste and 1/2 t. salt, then bring to a boil. Simmer with frequent mixing for 10 minutes.
