Transfer the cutlet to the bread crumbs. Scoop crumbs from around the cutlet and pile them on top, completely covering the cutlet. Do not press on the crumbs. Shake the baking sheet for a few seconds. Then flip the cutlet and return it to the bread crumbs. Shake the baking sheet, then pick up the cutlet with your fingertips and gently shake off excess crumbs, being careful not to fold or crease the cutlet.
Clarify 2 lb. butter and heat to high (375 to 400 degrees). Carefully lay each cutlet in turn onto the butter, starting near you and draping it away from you to avoid accidentally splashing yourself with hot oil. As fast as you can, wipe your fingers clean on the kitchen towel. Swirl the pan, allowing the fat to splash over and around the cutlet for exactly 30 seconds. Using the fork or a thin, slotted spatula, pick up the cutlet from one edge and carefully flip it. Continue to cook, swirling. The cutlet should start to puff and inflate. Keep cooking while swirling until the cutlet is golden brown and crisp, about 1 to 1 1/2 minutes.
Pick up the cutlet from one edge with a fork and transfer to a paper towel-lined plate. Blot gently with an extra paper towel, then transfer to a roasting rack and place in oven to keep warm. Strain the foam off the butter and repeat.
Serve cutlets immediately with lemon wedges, lingonberry jam or cranberry sauce.

name: Tournedos Rossini for Two
instructions:
1.  Season two filets mignons with salt and pepper.  Combine ¼ C. veal stock (or 1 T. veal demi-glace mixed with 3 T. water) with 1 T. truffle juice and 1 t. chopped back truffles.  Set aside with the filets.
2.  In a large skillet over medium heat, melt 1 T. butter with 1 T. vegetable oil and quickly fry two slices of bread and set aside.  Add another 1 T. butter and sauté the filets over high heat, 4 minutes on a side for rare, 5 minutes for medium.  Set aside.
3.  Using the same skillet, sauté two slices of foie gras over high heat for 1.5 minutes a side and place on top of the filets.
4.  Remove the fat from the skillet, but while it is still hot add 2 T. Madeira and deglaze the pan.  Add the veal stock mixture and bring to a quick boil.  Remove from heat and whisk in 1 T. butter, with salt and pepper to taste.  Cut the toast to the shape of the filets and place on plates.  Top with the filets and foie gras.  Add a slice of black truffle and spoon the sauce over all.  Serve immediately.

name: Prime Rib with David Burke’s Sauce
instructions:
Remove prime rib (3-rib portion prime rib - ~6-8 lb. and trimed of excess fat; preferably dry-aged) from the refirgerator 2 hr. before cooking.  Preheat the oven to 375°F.  Combine 1/4 C cayenne, 1/2 C. kosher salt, white and black pepper to taste in a small bowl.  Rub the mixture over the entire surface of the meat.  Place meat on a wire rack in a roasting pan, place in oven and cook 13-15 min per pound (or until a meat thermometer reads 125°F).  Allow to rest 15-20 minutes before carving.
In a medium pot, combine 2 C. Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 C. ketchup, 2 T. Tabasco sauce and heat over medium heat.  Reduce for 20-25 min with occasional stirring until thickened.  Whisk in 3 T. butter and 2 t. sesame oil.  Allow to cool before serving.

name: Batsheva’s Brisket
instructions:
1. Loosely dice 4 large onions and set them aside in a big bowl.
2. Pat a 2-lb. first-cut brisket dry and pour a very generous amount of salt, pepper and paprika on the less fatty side. Pour 1 t. olive oil into a high-sided pan with a lid to coat the bottom. Turn the stove to medium-high and place the fattier side down in the pan for 7 minutes. When the meat is browned, flip it so the spiced side is down and generously sprinkle more salt, pepper and paprika onto the fatty side. Let cook uncovered for 4 more minutes.
3. Pour in the entire bowl of onions; they should cover the meat and reach up to the top of the pan. Cover with lid and turn down to a simmer.
4. Cook for 3 to 4 hours, or until tender.
5. Slice and serve brisket immediately with rice and side vegetables.

name: Dorothy’s Pot Roast (serves six)
instructions:
Heat oven to 340°F.  Rub a 4 lb. beef chuck roast liberally with kosher salt and coarsely ground pepper.  Sear beef over high heat in 4 T. olive oil in a dutch oven until crusted (4-5 minutes per side).  Remove to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium and add 4 T. butter to the pan.  When melted, sauté 2 red onions (quartered), 4 carrots (peeled and cut into 2” pieces), 3 stalks celery (in 2” pieces), 1 rutabaga (peeled cut into pieces), 8 cremini mushrooms (halved), 2 parsnips (cut into pieces) and 1 head of garlic (top cut off to expose cloves) until they start to color (8-10 minutes).
Add 1 can tomato paste and cook until slightly darkened (~5 minutes).  Add 2 bay leaves, 3 sprigs rosemary and 2 C. red wine, then cook down until it is a thick gravy.
Return meat to dutch oven, add 4 C. beef broth and cover.  Bake 2.5 hr.
Remove from oven, take off cover and allow to cool 10 minutes.  Remove meat and slice.  Remove bay leaves and rosemary, and squeeze garlic cloves into gravy.  Place sliced meat in shallow bowls, top with vegetables and gravy, and serve.

name: Julia’s Beef Bourguignon
instructions:
1.  Pre-heat oven to 450 °F.  Simmer 6 oz. bacon in water for 10 minutes in 1.5 qt. water.  Drain and dry.  Sauté the bacon in 1 T. olive oil until lightly browned.  Remove and drain the bacon.
2.  Cut 3 lb. of rump roast into 2-in. cubes.  Blot dry on paper towels.  Reheat the bacon oil to medium-hot and brown the beef on all sides, then set aside.
3.  In the same oil, brown 2 sliced carrots and 1 sliced onion.  Drain.
4.  Place meat and bacon in a casserole.  Sprinkle with 1 t. pepper, 2 T. flour, then toss.  Place in oven for 4 minutes, remove and stir, then return to oven for another 4 minutes.  Lower oven to 325 °F.
5.  Stir in the browned vegetables and one bottle of a dry, full-bodied, young red wine (e.g., Beaujolais, Bordeaux-St. Emilion or Burgundy), 2-3 C. beef bouillon or stock (enough to just cover the beef), 1 T. tomato paste, 2 cloves crushed garlic, two sprigs of thyme and one of bay leaf.  Cover and place in the lower third of the oven.  Simmer for 2.5-3 hours, until meat is tender.
6.  Brown 18-24 pearl onions (peeled) in 1.5 T. butter and 1.5 T. oil over medium-high heat, turning occasionally until well-browned.  Place in stew about one hour before it is finished cooking.
7.  Sauté 1 lb. quartered mushrooms in 2 T. butter and 1 T. oil over medium-high heat.
8.  When the meat is tender, pour out the liquid into a saucepan and skim off the grease.  Thicken and reduce over medium heat to ~2.5 C.  Add back to meat, stir in mushrooms and baste over low heat for 3-5 minutes.  Garnish with parsley and serve in the casserole with boiled potatoes separately.

name: Restaurant Pork Chops with Polenta (serves four)
instructions:
1.  For polenta, bring 6 C. chicken stock to a boil in a large, heavy saucepan.  Whisk in 2 C. corn meal in a steady stream and stir constantly over medium heat until the polenta begins to thicken (~10 minutes).
2.  Add 6 oz. soft goat cheese and stir another 2 minutes.  Stir in 2 T. butter and salt and pepper to taste.  Cover and turn off heat.
1.  Whisk together ¾ C. maple syrup, 1 T. dark brown sugar and ½ t. ground cinnamon in a ceramic bowl.  Warm a small saucepan over medium heat, then add ½ C. pecans and ~2 T. of the maple sauce and cook for a few minutes.  Transfer to a plate and allow to cool, then chop roughly and set aside.
2.  Season 4 1 1/4-in thick pork chops aggressively with salt and pepper, then drizzle with oil and grill to desired doneness, basting occasionally with the maple sauce.
3.  Allow chops to rest 5 minutes.  Meanwhile, slice 2 green apples (cored) into thick rounds, season lightly with salt and pepper, drizzle with oil and grill till soft, basting occasionally with the maple sauce.
4.  Sprinkle chops with pecans and chopped, candied ginger, then serve with apple slices and polenta.

name: Spicy Tomato Sauce for Polenta
instructions:
1.  Heat 2 T. olive oil in a heavy saucepan.  Add 1 C. minced onion and sauté til softened.
2.  Add 3 cloves garlic (minced), alt and pepper to taste and cook another minute.  Add 2 C. tomato sauce and simmer another 5 minutes.
3.  Chop 1 T. capers, ¼ C. black olives and 4 anchovy fillets (rinsed and dried).  Add to sauce and cook another two minutes.

name: Pork Tenderloin with Shallots and Prunes
instructions:
1.  Dissolve 3 T. kosher salt and 3 T. brown sugar in 2 C. cold water, then add ¼ t. allspice berries (crushed), ¼ t. ground black pepper, 2 bay leaves and 2 sprigs of thyme.  Place one pork tenderloin in a plastic or ceramic container and cover with the liquid.  Refrigerate overnight.
2.  Pat the meat dry and allow to come to room temperature.  Preheat oven to 400°F.
3. Simmer 4 oz. pitted prunes in ½ C. dry red wine until softened (~5 min).  Remove from heat, then stir in ½ t. grated ginger, ½ t. grated orange zest; steep 10 min.
4. Heat 1 T. olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat, then brown the tenderloin.  Transfer the tenderloin to a roasting pan and roast 15 min.  Let rest for 10 min.
5. Melt 1 T. butter in the skillet, then add 3 shallots (minced), ½ t. chopped thyme, salt and pepper to taste and cook 5 min. while stirring with a wooden spoon.  Add 1.5 C. chicken broth, raise the heat and simmer 2 min.  Stir in the prune mixture, simmer another 2 min then add 1 T. Madeira and 2 t. potato starch suspended in 2 T. water.  Cook another minute or so until thickened
6. Transfer meat to a serving platter, slice and cover with sauce.  Serve with baked yams.

name: Tom’s Terrine (makes 12 to 14 servings)
instructions:
1. Sauté 1 C. finely chopped, large onion in 2 T. unsalted butter in a 10-inch heavy skillet, covered, over moderately low heat, until soft (~10 min). Add 2 garlic cloves, finely chopped, and 1 T chopped fresh thyme and cook with stirring another minute or two.  Transfer to a large bowl set in a bowl of ice.
2. While onion cools, pulse 1 T. kosher salt, 1 t. black peppercorns, 1/2 t. whole allspice or 1/4 teaspoon ground allspice, 1 bay leaf and 1/4 t. freshly grated nutmeg  in a spice grinder until finely ground.  Add to onion mixture, then whisk in 1/2 C. heavy cream, 2 large eggs and 3 T. Cognac or other brandy until combined well.
3. Pulse 1/2 lb chicken livers, trimmed, in a food processor until finely chopped, then add to onion mixture along with 1 lb ground fatty pork shoulder, 1/2 lb ground fatty veal (preferably veal breast).  Mix together well with hands or a wooden spoon. Stir in 1/2 lb baked ham, cut into 1/2-inch cubes.
4. Line bottom and long sides of terrine mold crosswise with about 6 to 9 strips of bacon, arranging them close together (but not overlapping) and leaving a 1/2- to 2-inch overhang. Fill terrine evenly and compactly with ground-meat mixture. Cover top of terrine lengthwise with 2 or 3 more bacon slices to cover completely, and fold overhanging ends of bacon back over these. Cover terrine with plastic wrap and chill at least 8 hours to marinate meats.
5. Put oven rack in middle position and preheat oven to 325°F.  Discard plastic wrap and cover terrine tightly with a double layer of foil, then bake terrine in a water bath until thermometer inserted diagonally through foil at least 2 inches into center of terrine registers 155 to 160°F (1 3/4 to 2 hours). Remove foil and let terrine stand in mold on a rack to cool (~30 min).
6. Put terrine (still in mold) in a cleaned baking pan and cover with a piece of parchment or wax paper.  Place another mold of same size (or a piece of heavy cardboard cutto fit) on parchment and weigh down with 4-5 cans of anything for 1 hr.  Chill terrine in pan with weights at least 24 hours.
7. To serve, place mold in a pan filled with 1” hot water and let stand 2 min.  Run a knife around the inside edge of the terrine and tip terrine mold (holding terrine) to drain excess liquid.  Place a cutting board over terrine and flip onto cutting board.  Gently wipe outside of terrine with a paper towel. Transfer terrine to a serving platter and let stand at room temperature for 30 min before serving.
8. Cut into 1/2-inch-thick slices as needed and serve with mustards and cornichons, bread or crackers.

name: Chicken Paprikash (1 hr 20 min)
instructions:
1. Season 3 lb. skin-on chicken pieces lightly with salt and pepper.  Melt 2. T butter and 2 T. canola oil over medium heat in a large skillet or pan with a tight-fitting lid.  Add 1 coarsely chopped yellow onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 2 minutes. Add ¼ C. sweet paprika, stirring to combine, and cook for another 2 minutes.
2. Push the onion mixture to one side of the pan and add the chicken pieces, skin side down, and cook until lightly browned, about 2 minutes. Turn the chicken pieces over to coat with the onion-paprika mixture and cook until browned, about 5 minutes. Cover the skillet, reduce the heat to medium-low and cook gently for about 1 hour, turning the pieces occasionally.
3. Use tongs to remove the skin from the chicken pieces and discard. Turn the pieces to recoat with the sauce, and check the temperature of the chicken; the chicken is ready when an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the meat reads 165 degrees. Remove from the heat, transfer the chicken to a serving bowl and cover to keep warm.
4. In a small bowl, whisk together the 1 C. sour cream and 1 T. flour. Add the mixture to the skillet, return it to a simmer over medium-low heat and whisk until the sauce thickens. Pour over the chicken in the bowl, garnish with parsley, if using, and serve hot.

name: Fried Chicken
instructions:
1.  Combine 2 T. salt and a couple of grinds of pepper with 2 C. buttermilk in a large bowl or container. Add chicken pieces, cover and refrigerate 8 hours or overnight.  Drain, rinse with cold water and pat dry.
2.  Combine 1 t. salt and 1 t. black pepper. Sprinkle half the mixture evenly over chicken.
3.  In a large plastic bag, combine: 1 C. flour , 1/3 T. salt, ¼ T. thyme, ¼ T. basil, 1/6 T. oregano, ½ T. celery salt, ½ T. black pepper, ½ T. dried mustard, 2 T. paprika, 1 T. garlic salt, ½ T. ground ginger and 1.5 T. white pepper (can add 1 t. baking powder for crunchier crust).  Add two pieces chicken and shake well to coat. Shake off extra flour, and set aside. Repeat with remaining chicken and allow to stand 1 hr.
4.  Fit a large (10- or 12-inch) cast-iron skillet with lid, and fit with a candy thermometer.  Add 2 C. peanut oil and 3 bacon strips, then heat to 360 °F.  Discard bacon.
5.  Using tongs, add chicken pieces, skin side down (work in batches if necessary to avoid crowding pan). The oil will drop to about 325 degrees, where it should stay; adjust heat so that oil is bubbling gently around the pieces. Cover and cook 6 minutes; uncover and cook 9 minutes. Turn chicken pieces; cover and cook 6 minutes. Uncover and cook another 5 to 9 minutes, depending on size of pieces.
6.  Drain on paper towels or paper bags. Let cool at least 20 minutes before serving.

name: Bobby Flay’s Roast Chicken (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven (on Roast) to 425°F.  Rinse and pat dry 4 chicken breasts (bone-in).  Season aggressively with sea salt.  Combine 2 T. paprika, 2 t. chile powder, 2 t. ground mustard, 2 t. ground fennel seed and 1 t. black pepper.  Rub into chicken.
2.  Heat 3 T. olive oil over medium heat in a large oven-safe skillet.  When shimmering, add the chicken breasts skin-side down.  Cook unattended for 6-8 minutes.  Turn the chicekn over and place the pan in the oven.  Cook another 12-15 minutes.
3.  While the chicken is roasting, combine 1 C. fresh mint leaves, ½ C. parsley leaves, 4 cloves of garlic (mashed) and 1 serrano chile (deseeded and chopped), then pulse to a paste in a blender.  Add 1 T. honey and 1 T. dijon mustard, then pulse again.  With blender on low, slowly add ½ C. olive oil and blend until emulsified (add a little water if it is too thick).
4.  Spoon mint sauce over chicken breasts and serve with roast potatoes, thinly sliced lemon and sauteed greens.

name: Velvet Chicken (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Beat 2 eggwhites lightly in a medium bowl until bubbly.  Add 4 T. cornstarch and 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts (evened in shape) and marinate 30 minutes.
2.  In a small bowl, mix 2 T. dijon mustard, 2 T. whole grain mustard, 1 t. dried mustard, 1 T. grated horseradish, and ½ C. crème fraiche (1 C. heavy cream w/ either 3 T. buttermilk or ½ C. sour cream, covered and left out overnight).
3.  Melt 3 T. butter in a skillet over medium heat.  Add the chicken breasts and sauté lightly for 30 sec on each side (without browning).  Remove, blot dry and wipe pan.
4.  Add mustard/crème fraiche mixture to skillet along with 1 C. chicken broth, whisk together and bring to a simmer.  Add breasts and simmer 1 min, then turn breasts, cover and simmer another 2 min.  Turn off heat and leave covered another minute.
5.  Remove chicken to a wam platter and bring sauce to a boil.  Simmer with 1 t. chopped thyme until thickened, then serve over chicken.

name: Chicken Tagine (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Combine 3½ T. sweet paprika, 1 t .garlic powder, 2 t. cinnamon, 3 T. ground coriander, 1 T. ground turmeric, 1 T. ginger powder, ½ T. ground cardamom and 2½ t. ground allspice.  Dry sauté over low heat to release fragrance and allow to cool.
2.  Preheat oven to 350°F. Season 8 chicken thighs with kosher salt, freshly ground black pepper and 2 T. of the spice mixture and 2 T. olive oil.  Allow to stand.
3.  Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil, and set a large bowl of ice water to one side.  Boil 1 head of cauliflower (cut into florets) for 3 min, then blanch in the ice water.
4.  Sear the chicken in 2 T. olive oil over medium heat, starting skin-side down until browned.  Remove to a large dutch oven.  Brown the cauliflower in the remaining fat and add to the chicken.
5.  Lower heat and sauté 1 large white onion (diced), 3 cloves garlic (minced), 1 T. grated ginger and a pinch of saffron.  Cook until onions are translucent (~5 min).  Add 8 oz. San Marzano tomatoes and 2 C. chicken stock and simmer till reduced by half.
6.  Pour sauce over chicken and cauloflower, cover the pot and bake 20 min at 350°F.
7.  Stir in 2 preserved lemons (roughly chopped) and 1 C. green olives, then cover and cook another 20 min.  Garnish with cilantro, then serve in pot alongside couscous.

name: Perfect Chicken Salad (serves 8)
instructions:
1.  Sauté 2 chicken breasts (flattened) in a cast-iron skillet.  Allow to cool, then cut into small pieces.
2.  Combine 2/3 C. mayonnaise with ¼ C. sour cream, juice from ½ lemon, 2/3 C. pecan piees, 3 T. parsley, tarragon OR chives, salt and pepper taste.  Mix well with chicken pieces and serve on bread or lettuce.

name: Chicken Marbella (serves four)
instructions:
1.  In a large bowl, combine two chickens (quartered), half a head of garlic (peeled and pureed), 1/8 C dried oregano, salt and pepper to taste, ¼ C. red wine vinegar, ¼ C. olive oil, ½ C. pitted prunes, ¼ C. pitted Spanish olives, ¼ C. capers (with juice) and 3 bay leaves.  Cover and marinate overnight in the refrigerator.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F.  Arrange chicken in a single layer in a baking pan and spoon marinade over it evenly.  Sprinkle chicken with brown sugar, then add ½ C. white wine to pan.  Bake 50 minutes to 1 hour, basting frequently.
3.  Using a slotted spoon, transfer chicken ,prunes, olives and capers to a serving platter.  Baste with pan juices and sprinkle generously with chopped parsley.  Serve with the remaining pan juices in a sauce boat.

name: Chicken Tetrazzini (serves 6-8)
instructions:
1.  Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.  Spoon hot water over 1 oz. dried porcini mushrooms, and let stand for 20-25 minutes.  Strain and set aside.
2. Place 4 medium poblano peppers on a small sheet pan and set on the highest rack of a broiler so that the skin blackens and blisters, turning every now and then, ~15-20 minutes.  Remove, cover with two plates and allow to rest.
3.  Place 5 dried guajillo chilies in a medium pot over high heat and allow to cook until fragrant, then lower the heat and add 1.5 C chicken stock, 1.5 C. whole milk, 3 cloves garlic (thinly sliced) and 1 medium shallot (thinly sliced).  Allow to cook at a slow simmer for 10 minutes.  Allow to cool slightly, then place in a blender with 6 oz. Cheddar cheese (grated) and blend until smooth.  Set aside.
4. Remove the skin and seeds from the roasted poblanos, then slice into thin strips.
5. Pre-heat oven to 400°F.  Cook 1 lb. spaghetti per package instructions to al dente.  Strain and rinse until cool.
6. Return spaghetti to the cooking pot and toss with poblano strips, mushrooms, the meat from one rotisserie chicken, the juice from one lemon, kosher salt and black pepper to taste.  Transfer to a casserole and cook 20 minutes, until the cheese has melted and turned golden.  Garnish with fresh parsley and serve.

name: Chicken Pot Pie (serves 6)
instructions:
1.  Using a fork, mix 2.5 C. flour and 1 t. salt with ½ C. chilled vegetable shortening and 6 T. chilled, butter until the mixture looks like coarse meal.
2.  Add 6-7 T. ice water and mix thoroughly, without over-working the dough.  Divide in two, flatten into disks between wax paper or plastic wrap, and refrigerate 1-2 hr.
3.  Sauté 1 clove garlic (smashed) in 1 T. cooking oil over low heat.  Once it begins to sizzle, add 6 oz. bacon cut into strips and 1 medium onion (chopped) and sauté until bacon is golden, stirring often.  Remove the garlic and add 8 oz. button mushrooms (thickly cut).  Cook until mushrooms are browned and slightly softened.
4.  Combine ¼ C. flour, 1 t. dried thyme, ½ t. paprika, salt and pepper to taste in a plastic bag.  Add 1 lb. boneless chicken thighs, cut into bite-size pieces and shake.
5. Heat oven to 400°F.  Add 2 T. butter to the onion mix and add the chicken pieces.  Sauté until golden, then add 2.5 C. chicken stock, ¼ C. Madiera (or Marsala or dry sherry) and 1 T. sherry vinegar and simmer, scraping the bottom of the skillet ~5 minutes.  Turn off heat and stir in 2 T. chopped parsley.
4.  Transfer chicken mixture to a 9-in. pie plate.  Roll out pastry dough and drape over chicken.  Press around edges and cut a few vent holes in the pastry dough.  Brush with 1 egg, beaten with 1 T. water.  Place in the oven in a large pan, and bake 20-30 minutes until crust is browned.
5.  Remove from oven and allow to cool at least 10 minutes, then serve.
N.B.  Leftover chicken or turkey can be used.  Add the flour mixture to the onion mixture while the latter is cooking, then add the leftovers (cut into bite-size pieces) and sauté until heated through.

name: Russ’ Fish Cakes with Tartar Sauce
instructions:
Preheat oven to 300°F, and sprinkle a baking pan with olive oil and salt.  Lay 1 lb. of striped bass (or other fish cutlets) on pan skin-side down.  Bake until just cooked (20-30 min), and allow to cool.
Skin and bone the fish and break into small pieces in a small mixing bowl.    Add 2 T. fresh parsley (chopped) and 1 C. cup mashed potatoes, mixing gently.  Whisk together 1 egg, 1/4 C. mayonaise and 1 T. Dijon mustard.  Lift and fold the mixture into the fish (but don’t over-mix).
Pat the fish into 8 3-in. cakes and coat with dried breadcrumbs.  Sauté in 4 T. butter/oil mixture over medium heat until well-browned (2-3 min. on a side).
For the tartar sauce, mix together 1/3 C. mayonaise, 1/4 C sweet pickle relish, 2 T. finely chopped red onion, 1 T. chopped parsley, 1/2 t. Worcestershire sauce, 1 t. celery seed, and 1.5 T. lemon juice (plus a drop or two of hot sauce, if desired).  Serve with lemon.

name: Seared Scallops in Mushroom White Wine Sauce
instructions:
1.  Spray 1 lb. sea scallops with olive oil and sear in a heavy skillet over medium heat until browned.  Add 4 C. coarsely chopped mushroom caps and sauté until cooked.  Remove with a slotted spoon.
2.  Add 1/2 C. white wine and 1 T. minced thyme leaves, then cook till reduced by half.  Salt/pepper to taste, then return scallops and stir to coat with sauce.

name: Provençal Shrimp
instructions:
Sauté 1 lb. shelled and deveined shrimp in 2 t. olive oil over medium heat for 1.5 min.  Add 2 T. slivered almonds and 4 cloves minced garlic; continue till shrimp is cooked.
Stir in 1 T. drained capers, 2 cored and coarsely chopped tomatoes and 1 T. minced fresh parsley, then cook another 1 min.  Add Salt and pepper to taste and serve.

name: Tuna Tartare (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Dice 1.5 lb. sashimi-quality bluefin tuna (preferably the fattier belly cut) into 1/8-in. pieces.  Place in a mixing bowl and add 4 T. extra virgin olive oil, 2 t. each minced chives, garlic chives, mint, cilantro and parsley.  Mix well.  Sprinkle with 2/3 of the juice from 2 lemons and mix again.  Taste and adjust seasoning as desired.
2.  Place 1/2 lb. baby arugula in a bowl and mix in the remaining juice from the lemon, along with some salt and pepper.    Place on each of eight cold plates, top with the tuna and serve.

name: Lemongrass Mussels (serves two)
instructions:
1. Heat 2 T. coconut or safflwer oil in the bottom of a large pot until hot.  Add 1 finely chopped shallot, 1 stalk lemongrass (trimmed and finely chopped) and 1 jalapeño or habanero (finely chopped) and cook until soft.
2. Add 1 C. unsweetened coconut milk and 2 lb. fresh mussels (well-rinsed), cover and cook 5-7 minutes until mussels have opened (discard those that don’t open).  Remove to a serving bowl.
3. Remove pot from heat, and add zest from 1 lemon, 1 T. lemon juice, ½ t. asian fish sauce and 1 C. whole cilantro leaves.  Taste/adjust with lemon juice and fish sauce.
4. Serve with bread for sopping.

name: Fried Scallops (serves six)
instructions:
1. Combine ½ C. flour with ½ C. fine cornmeal and 1 t. baking powder.  Dry 2 lb. scallops, then dredge in the mixture.
2. Place vegetable oil to a depth of ¼ inch in a large cast iron fry pan and heat to 375°F.  Add scallops in batches and cook until golden, about 3 minutes on each side.  Drain and eat right away.

name: Fried Fish (serves six)
instructions:
1. Cut 2 lb. very fresh striped bass or blufish into 2-3-in. squares.
2. Combine 1 C. flour with ½ T. kosher salt, 2 T. black pepper and 2 T. Old Bay spice.  Separately, whisk 3 eggs and ¼ C. half and half.  Place 1.5 C. panko in a dish.
3. Dredge fish in flour mixture, then egg mixture, then panko, shaking off excess each time.  Place on a plate.
4. Place vegetable oil to a depth of ¼ inch in a large fry pan and heat to 375°F.  Add the fish in batches, cooking 2-3 minutes on a side until golden brown.  Drain and serve.

name: Crab Louis (serves six)
instructions:
1. Wash 2 heads Boston lettuce, dry and refrigerate in a towel.
2.  Peel 1 English cucumber, halve lengthwise and seed.  Cut into bite-size pieces and drizzle with ¾ t. kosher salt in 3 T. vinegar.  Let stand to marinate.
3.  Bring 4 eggs to a boil, covered, in cold water.  Once boiling, turn off heat and allow to stand 6 min., then drain and let stand in ice water.
4.  Whisk together 1 egg yolk, ¼ t. Dijon mustard, ¼ t. salt and 1+ T. fresh lemon juice.  Whisk in 1 C. extra-virgin olive oil.  Whisk in a little water in the end if it is too firm.
5.  Drizzle 1 shallot, finely minced, with a little lemon juice and a pinch of salt.  Purée 1 clove with ¼ t. salt and add both shallot and garlic to the mayonnaise with 2 T. very dill pickles, finely chopped, and ½ T. capers.  Refrigerate if not using right away.
6.  Loosely pile lettuce on each of four plates.  Drain cucumbers and divide among plates, along with 1 lb. crabmeat.  Add a few dashes of Tabasco sauce to the crab, then halve the eggs and add to salad along with 1 avocado (diced).  Top with dressing (going mainly on the crab) and garnish with chopped chives.

name: Cranberry Jalapeño Chutney
instructions: 1.  Slowly melt ¼ lb. butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.  Add ½ C. diced red onion, ½ C. diced red pepper and 3 minced jalapeños and cook 2 minutes.  Add ½ C. cider vinegar, 2 C. brown sugar, ¼ C. black currants, ¼ C. golden raisins, 3 C. fresh cranberries, 1 t. allspice and ½ t. powdered ginger.  Cook another 10 minutes, stiring occationally.  Serve on baked sweet potatoes with sour cream.

name: Calf’s Liver and Mushroom Pate (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Sauté 1/2 lb. sliced mushrooms in 1 T. butter.  Ditto 1/2 lb. calf’s liver cut in 1-in. clices and 1/2 medium onion.  Place in a food processor and blend thoroughly.
2.  Mix the above with 1/4 lb. cream cheese, 1/8 t. nutmeg and salt to taste in an electric mixer until very fluffy.  Serve at room temperature on toast.

name: Chicken Liver Paté I
instructions:
1.  Sauté 1 onion (chopped) in 2 T. butter until soft over medium-high heat.  Add 1 lb. fresh chicken livers and brown on both sides (~2 min each side).
2.  Place liver and onion with juices in a blender.  Add 1 T. ground pepper, ¼ t. ground allspice, ¼ t. ground coriander, 1 T. salt, 2 T. brandy and ½ C. cream.  Purée until smooth, then place in a serving bowl and refrigerate 2-3 hours.  Serve on toast.

name: Chicken Liver Paté II… with Bacon Onion Jam
instructions:
1. Sauté two medium shallots (finely minced) in 4 T. butter over medium-high heat until translucent but not browned.
2. Add 1 lb. chicken livers, 1 T. fresh thyme and 1/3 C. madiera or port, then bring heat to high and cook with occasional stirring ~ 5 minutes.
3. Remove livers to a blender, add 3-6 T. heavy cream, 4 T. melted butter and kosher salt to taste.  Blend well, then pack into a bowl and refrigerate 2 hr. or until firm.
4.  Cook ¾ lb. slab bacon (cubed) over medium heat in a dutch oven, stirring occasionally, until the fat is rendered (12-15 minutes).  Discard all but 1 T. of the fat.
5. Add 4 white onions (chopped), 1.5 t. mustard seed, 2.5 T. brown sugar, ¼ C. balsamic vinegar and 3 T. water.  Stir well, then cover and lower the heat to a simmer and cook undisturbed for 15-20 minutes.  Remove the top, stir again then partly cover and allow to cook another hour or so until most of the liquid is gone and the onions have achieved a dark brown jamminess.  Add salt and pepper to taste.
6. Allow the mixture to cool a bit, then remove to a bowl and allow to cool completely.  Serve on toast with the pate.

name: Turkey Giblet Paté (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Remove and meat from the turkey neck, and grind with a meat grinder with the rest of the giblets (or chop very finely with a sharp knife).  Set aside.
2.  Cook 1/2 C. finely chopped onion and 1 stalk finely chopped celery in 2 T. vegetable oil until soft (stir frequently).  Add the minced meat and continue until cooked through and lightly browned.
3.  Remove from heat.  Make a well in the center of the mixture, add 1 large egg (beaten) and quickly stir in.  Mix in 1/4 C. mayonnaise, kosher salt, pepper and Tabasco sauce to taste.  Garnish with chopped parsley and serve on quartered toast.

name: Passaladière
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 350°F.  Melt 1/2 stick of butter on a baking sheet, then spread 3 lb. thinly sliced onions on sheet, top with thyme and rosemary sprigs and one bay leaf, sprinkle with salt and pepper, drizzle with 3 T. extra-virgin olive oil, top with another 3 lb. sliced onions and bake until tender and golden, stirring every 30 min. (~2 hr. total cooking time).  Discard herbs.
2.  Pour 1 C. warm water into a small bowl and mix in 2 envelopes of dried yeast,1 t. sugar and 2 T. olive oil.  Let stand until foamy, ~ 10 min.
3.  Blend 2.75 C. flour and 1 t. salt in a food processor.  Add the yeast mixture and 2 T. olive oil and process until dough clumps together (add more flour if dough is sticky).  Turn dough out on counter and knead until smooth and elastic, ~ 5 min.  Coat a large bowl with 1 T. olive oil, add the dough, turn once to coat and cover with plastic wrap, then a kitchen towel.  Let rise until doubled, ~ 1.5 hr.  Punch down and let rise another hour.
4.  Sprinkle a heavy 17x11x1-in. baking sheet with cornmeal.  Roll out on a lightly floured surface.  Transfer to the baking sheet, pressing the edges all the way around along the sides and corners.  Cover with a dry kitchen towel and let rise 1 hr.
5.  Pre-heat oven to 475°F.  Spread onions out on top of dough.  Arrange 20 anchovy fillets and 20 oil-cured, pitted black olives on top and bake 15 min.  Sprinkle top with 1 T. chopped, fresh thyme and serve warm or at Rm. T.

name: Mashed Potatoes (with Carrot Purée)
instructions:
1.  Peel and cube 4 large baking potatoes.  Cover with water in a large saucepan (with trimmed fat from any meat dish accompanying the potatoes), bring to a boil and simmer for ~20 minutes until soft.
Press through a potato ricer.  Combine with 4 T. butter, 1/2 C. milk (or more) and kosher salt, white and black pepper to taste.  Finally, with a heavy whisk whip in 1/4 C. extra virgin olive oil.
If desired, combine with 1 lb. boiled carrots, puréed with 1 C. beef broth.

name: Garlic and Jalapeño Mashed Potatoes
instructions:
1.  Peel and cube 4 large baking potatoes.  Cover with water in a large saucepan, bring to boil and simmer for 10-15 min.  Drain and pass potatoes through a ricer.
2.  Roast 20 cloves of garlic and 2 chopped jalapeños with a pinch or two of cayenne.
3.  Stir the garlic and jalapeños into the potatoes along with 12 T. yogurt and 12 T. lowfat milk.  Salt and pepper to taste and serve immediately.

name: Fake baked beans (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Cover 1 lb. white beans in water by 2 inches.  Refrigerate overnight, or bring to a boil and cool for 1 hr.
2.  Drain and rinse beans, then place in a cooking pot.  Stick 2 whole cloves into a small onion (peeled and halved lengthwise) and add to pot with 2 cloves of garlic (crushed) and one bay leaf.  Cover with water by 1 inch and simmer partly covered until beans are tender (1-2 hr).  Add boiling water as need to keep beans moist.
Remove onion, garlic and bay leaf, then stir in 1 t. salt.  Mix 1/4 C. ketchup, 1/4 C. molasses, 3 T. apple cider vinegar, 1.5 t. dry mustard powder, Tabasco sauce to taste, and 1/4 t. black pepper in a small bowl  Pour mixture over beans and stir well.  Add a slice of bacon to the pot and return to a simmer.  Cook until thickened, 30-45 minutes.  Remove the slice of bacon.
Cook 5 pieces of bacon and drain.  Transfer beans to serving dish, top with cooked bacon and chopped red onion, and serve.

name: Potato/Cheese Soufflé
instructions:
1.  Boil 4 baking potatoes (sliced) for 15 min.  Drain and rice.
2.  Whisk 2 eggs and 1.5 C. ricotta cheese until smooth.  Mix in 1  C. half and half and 1.5 C. Gruyère, then the potatoes.  Season with salt and pepper.
3.  Place in a greased casserole dish and bake 30 min at 350°F.  Sprinkle with 1/2 C. Gruyère and heat until cheese is melted.

name: Purée of Peas and Watercress (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Fill a large saucepan with water and bring to a boil.  Add 4 C. frozen peas and return to a boil.  Turn off the heat and add the tops (2-3 in.) of two 1-lb. bunches of watercress.  Allow to stand 5 min. until the watercress has wilted.
2.  Drain the peas and watercress, then blend in a food processor with 4 T. butter, 1/2 t. black pepper and 3.4 t. sea salt until smooth.  Serve with bread.

name: Wrap 4 lemons in two layers of aluminum foil and bake 1.5 hrs. at 400°F.
instructions:
Allow to cool.  Unwrap lemons, cut in half lengthwise and carefully scoop out the flesh.  Remove seeds and pureé with 2 t. sugar.  Season with salt and pepper.
Combine 2 C. Greek or Moroccan olives (dry-cured and pitted), 3 cloves of garlic, 6 oz. anchovy fillets (drained and patted dry), 6 oz. canned tuna in oil (drained), 0.5 C. capers, 1 T. green peppercorns (drained and chopped), 0.25 t. chopped fresh thyme, 2 T. grated lemon zest and 2 T. cognac (optional).  Pulse in a food processor until still coarse, then slowly add 2/3 C. olive oil.

name: Tapenade II
instructions:
Purée 9 oz. pitted black olives, 4 oz. garlic, 4 oz. anchovy fillets and 80 g. drained capers in a food processor until smooth.  Add ~50 mL. olive oil and continue processing until smooth.
Cut off the end of a large cucumber.  Using a long thin knife, scoop out the seeds and fill the hollowed cucumber with the tapenade mixture.
Press a Ritz cracker against the end of the cucumber and slice off a section.  Repeat until the cucumber is used up.  Serve immediately.

name: Perfect French Fries (serves four)
instructions:
Peel 4 large russet potatoes (some say the older and softer the better) and cut into 1/4 x 1/4-in. thick batons.  Rinse in a large bowl with cold running water until water becomes clear.  Cover with water and add ice, then refrigerate 30 min. to 2 days.
In a 5-quart pot or Dutch oven fitted with a candy or deep-frying thermometer, (or in an electric deep fryer), heat 2 quarts peanut oil over medium-low heat until the thermometer registers 325° F.  Make sure that you have at least 3 inches of space between the top of the oil and the top of the pan.  Drain ice water from cut fries and wrap potato pieces in a clean dishcloth or tea towel and thoroughly pat dry. Increase the heat to medium-high and add fries, a handful at a time, to the hot oil.  Fry, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are soft and limp and begin to turn a blond color, about 6 to 8 minutes. Remove fries from the oil and drain on paper towels. Let rest for at least 10 minutes or up to 2 hours.
When ready to serve, reheat the oil to 375° F.  Transfer the blanched potatoes to the hot oil in small batches and fry again, stirring frequently, until golden brown and puffed, 3-5 minutes.  Transfer to paper lined platter and sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste. Serve immediately.  (If a more “nubbly” surface is desired, toss fries lightly with corn starch before second frying.)

name: Pot of Beans (2 hr)
instructions:
1. In a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot set over medium-high heat, combine 1 lb. pinto beans (picked over and rinsed) with enough water to cover by 2 inches. Add ½ yellow onion (peeled), 3 cloves of garlic (peeled), 2 bay leaves and 1.5 t. salt.  Include a ham hock, if desired.
2. Bring the water to a boil. Boil the beans for 10 minutes, then reduce the heat to low so the liquid is barely bubbling, cover, and simmer until the beans are very tender, 60 to 90 minutes or longer. (Periodically check and add more hot water if needed to keep the beans submerged.) Test at least five beans from different parts of the pot to make sure they are tender. Remove and discard the bay leaves, garlic and onion. Taste, and season with more salt, if needed.
3. When the beans are tender, cook them uncovered for another 10-20 minutes to reduce and concentrate the broth.

name: Lentil and Pecan Stuffed Acorn Squash (1 hr, 20 min)
instructions:
1. Line a large plate with a clean dish towel. In a frying pan over medium heat, melt 1 stick (8 oz.) butter. Add 10 fresh sage leaves (sprigs of thyme) and fry until the butter is no longer bubbling (~1 minute). Remove from the heat and, using a slotted spoon, remove the herbs and transfer to drain on the towel. Sprinkle the leaves with salt. Scrape the butter, including the browned bits on the bottom of the pan, into a large bowl.
2. Position a rack in the middle of the oven and preheat to 350 degrees.  Using a sharp knife, cut 3 acorn squashes in half and use a spoon to scrape out the seeds and stringy pulp.  Place the squash halves, cut side down, on an large, ungreased, rimmed baking sheet or cast-iron skillet and bake for ~30 min, or until tender.
3. While the squash is baking, in a medium saucepan over medium heat, heat 1 T. cooking oil until shimmering. Add 1 small onion (thinly sliced), 4 cloves of garlic (finely minced), 5 sprigs of thyme (leaves only), 1 t. salt and ¼ t. pepper. Cook, stirring frequently, until tender (~ 7 minutes). Add ½ C. white wine or hard cider and reduce until the pan is almost dry, about 3 minutes. Add 1 C. brown lentils and cook, stirring, until coated in the onion mixture, 1 to 2 minutes.
4. Add 2 C. water, increase the heat to high and bring to a boil. Decrease the heat until the liquid is gently simmering and cook until the lentils are tender, about 20 minutes.
5. When the squash is cooked, remove it from the oven but leave the heat on. Let the squash cool a bit, then scoop most of the flesh from into the bowl with the brown butter.  Leave a thin layer of squash flesh next to the skin so the squash shell stays intact.
6. Add the cooked lentil mixture, add ½ C. pecan pieces (lightly toasted) and the juice and zest from 2 oranges to the squash pulp, and mix until well-blended. Add more salt, if needed.
7. Scoop the squash mixture into the squash shells, sprinkle with 1 C. grated parmesan cheese and return to the oven for 10 to 15 minutes.  Bake until warmed throughout, then broil to lightly brown the cheese.
8. Garnish with the fried sage and serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Niçoise Salad
instructions:
1.  Bring 3/4 lb. red potatoes to a boil over high heat.  Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 30 min.  Drain and set aside to cool.
2.  Mix 1.5 t. Dijon mustard, 5 cloves crushed garlic, 1.5 T. red-wine vinegar, 7 T. olive oil, 1 t. salt & 1 t. pepper.  Cut the potatoes into 1/2-in. slices; toss with dressing.
3.  Cook 3/4 lb. stripped string beans (prefereably haricot verts) until just firm.  Drain and cool under running water.  Cut beans in half lenghtwise.
4.  Peel 1 yellow pepper , then slice into sections remove seeds, and julienne.
5.  Slice 2 very ripe tomatoes coss-wise, remove seeds and chop coarsely.
6.  Mix all the above with 1.5 C. sliced red onion and 1 C. Niçoise olives (or other black oil-cured olive).
7.  When ready to serve, heat a skillet over high heat for 5 min.  Sprinkle with 1 lb. fresh tuna (cut into 1-in. cubes) with salt and pepper to taste, roll in 1 T. canola oil and sauté for 2 min over high heat.  Remove from heat and toss into the salad.
8.  Line a large platter with lettuce leaves, mound the Niçoise salad in the middle and serve with a garnish of shredded basil leaves.

name: Curried Egg Salad (serves four to six)
instructions:
Boil 4 eggs for 5 minutes.  Allow to cool, then peel and chop.  Season with salt and pepper.
Place eggs in a bowl, then add 1 T. greek yogurt, ¼ t. olive oil, 2 t. lemon juice, ½ t. lemon zest, ½ t. sriracha sauce, ¼ t. sherry vinegar and ½ t. curry powder.
Serve spread on Wasa toast topped with thinly sliced cucumber.

name: Perfect Potato Salad (serves four to six)
instructions:
Boil 2 lb. (3-4 medium) russet potatoes (cubed) in salt water until tender (~8 minutes).
Meanwhile, combine 1  rib celery (minced), 2 T. red onion (minced), 3 T. Heinz sweet pickle relish (or Cascadian Farm), 1/3 C. Hellmann's mayonaisse, ¾ t. powdered mustard, ¾ t. celery seed, 2 T. fresh parsley (minced), ½ t. salt and ¼ t. black pepper.
Drain the potatoes and transfer to a large bowl.  Add 2 T. distilled white vinegar and toss lightly with a rubber spatula.  Allow to stand 20 minutes, then gently fold in the remaining ingredients. Cover with plastic wrap and chill at least one hour.

name: Canlis Salad
instructions:
1.  Rinse and refrigerate one head of romaine lettuce.
2.  Fry 4 slices bacon until nearly crisp.  Drain bacon.  Remove all but 1 T. fat and, over low heat, toast 1 C. cubed French bread, turning occasionally, until crisp.
3.  Place a whole egg at Rm. T. in a coffee cup.  Bring a teapot to a boil, cover the egg with boiling water and cook 60 seconds.  Remove egg and rinse until cool.
4.  In a large salad bowl, whisk ½ C. olive oil with the juice from one lemon.  Add the egg and continue whisking vigorously until emulsified.  Salt and pepper to taste.
5.  Remove the outer leaves from the lettuce and chop coarsely.  Add to bowl with ¼ C. finely sliced scallion, ¼ C. fresh mint, and 1 T. oregano leaves.  Toss well, then top with 12 cherry tomatoes (halved) and ½ C. grated Romano cheese.

name: Simple coleslaw (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Bring 1 C. apple cider to a boil in a saucepan, then cook until reduced to ½ C.  Allow to cool, then add ¼ C. cider vinegar.
2. Shred 3 C. cabbage and one large carrot.  Combine with 1 t. celery seed, 1 T. sugar, salt and pepper to taste and the cider mixture.  Toss well and serve in a large bowl.

name: Colcannon (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Pre-heat oven to 350°F and grease an oven-proof casserole dish with butter.
2. Boil ½ of a small head of cabbage (thinly sliced and chopped) in salted water until tender (~2 min).  Remove the cabbage to a colander and shake well.
3.  Cook 5 large baking potatoes in the cabbage water until tender.  Drain and pass through a ricer.  Add 5 T. butter, then the cooked cabbage and 1 bunch of green onions (sliced).  Season with salt and pepper.
4. Spread mixture in the casserole, sprinkle with 1 C. grated white cheddar and bake 30 minutes.

name: Frederik Bille Brahe’s Salada Rosso
instructions:
1. Clean a head of radicchio by cutting off the bottom and separating the leaves into a container partly filled with ice-cold water. Try not to break the leaves when separating and washing. Dry the leaves in a salad spinner and dry on a tea towel.
2. Cut off the top and bottom of the a blood orange and remove the peel. Then, slice off the white pith from the exterior, leaving just the flesh of the oranges. Cut into 3 pieces by slicing from the sides toward the core and remove the pith at the center. Cut each slice into 10-12 smaller pieces.
3. Make the crumble: Cut 1 oz. dry, day-old bread into roughly inch-square cubes, toss with a little olive oil and spread on a sheet tray. Bake at 375 degrees for roughly 10 minutes. Toast 12 pecan halves in the oven, for 6-8 minutes. The bread and nuts should be golden brown and crispy. Once cool, mix them together in a blender until you have a medium-coarse crumble.
4. Make the almond cream: In a high-speed blender, blend 2 oz. sliced almonds and ½ C. water for a few minutes. Pass through a muslin cloth into a pot and heat the liquid to nearly boiling . Let it sit on the heat until it thickens, then set aside and let it cool in the pot for about 15 minutes. Put half of the purée back into the blender with the two garlic cloves and blend until the texture is consistent, then fold it into the unblended half of the purée. Leave in the fridge until serving.
5. Combine  ½ cup rose petal vinegar or red wine vinegar, 2 t. brine from preserved lemons, 1 t. balsamic vinegar, ½ teaspoon mirin, ½ t. liquid shio koji, ½ C. rapeseed or sunflower oil and 1½ T. honey  in a bowl with a whisk or a hand blender. Set aside.
6. Assemble the salad (you can make one big dish or two smaller ones): Start by laying out the leaves, curved side down, on a cutting board. It’s helpful to group larger leaves together and smaller leaves together. Add a generous spoonful of the almond cream into the core of every third leaf. Then dress all the leaves generously with the dressing, trying to cover each one. Next, add small chunks of orange to some of the leaves. Add a sprinkle of the crumble, flaky salt, black pepper and orange zest to all of the leaves. Now, add a dollop of the almond cream to a plate and lay the largest leaves, or two together, on top of the dollop, creating a bowl. Add leaves on top, starting with the largest and finishing with the smallest, until you’ve made a mountain. Finally, add a sprinkle of crumble and a little orange zest on top to finish.

name: Red Lentil Soup
instructions:
1. In a large pot, heat 3 tablespoons oil over high heat until hot and shimmering. Add one large onion (chopped) and 2 cloves garlic (minced), and sauté until golden, about 4 minutes.
2. Stir in 2 T. tomato paste, ½ t. cumin, 1 t. salt, ¼ t. black pepper and 1 T. chili powder, and sauté for 2 minutes longer.
3. Add 1 qt. chicken broth, 2 cups water, 1 C. lentils and 1 large carrot (peeled and diced).  Bring to a simmer, then partially cover pot and turn heat to medium-low. Simmer until lentils are soft, about 30 minutes. Taste and add salt if necessary.
4. Using an immersion or regular blender or a food processor, purée half the soup then add it back to pot. Soup should be somewhat chunky.
5. Reheat soup if necessary, then stir in juice from ½ lemon and 3 T. cilantro (chopped).  Serve soup drizzled with good olive oil and dusted lightly with chili powder if desired.

name: Persian-Style Lentil Soup (1 hr)
instructions:
1. In a large Dutch oven or pot with a lid, heat 3 T. olive oil over medium-high heat until it shimmers. Add 1 medium yellow onion (diced), and cook, stirring occasionally, until it starts to brown, about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in ¾ C. red lentils and ½ C. basmati rice, coating them in the fat. Add 1 6-oz. can tomato paste, 1 t. cumin, 1 t. dried mint, ½ t. salt, ¼ t. pepper and ¼ t. cinnamon, then 2 C. chicken stock, and stir to dissolve the tomato paste and evenly disperse the spices. Add another 4 C. chicken stock, stir well and bring to a boil. Lower the heat, cover and simmer until the rice and lentils are cooked, 20-25 minutes. (If soup thickens more than you’d like, add water, 1/4 cup at a time.) Taste, and adjust the seasonings, if needed.
3. Stir in about 1 C. curly parsley (chopped) and remove from the heat. Just before serving, stir in ¼ C. lemon juice. Divide the soup among bowls, top with the remaining parsley and serve hot.

name: Mom’s Soup (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Simmer 3-4 beef short ribs, 1/2 chopped onion, and 1 can stewed tomatoes, and 2 stalks chopped celery with water to cover overnight, covered.
2.  Add 2-3 chopped potatoes, 3-4 carrots, and a package of mixed vegetables.  Simmer on high for 45 min.

name: Gratinéed Onion Soup (serves 6)
instructions:
Slice 1 baguette into 25-30 1/2-in. slices, then toast them and let cool.  Lay slices out on a baking sheet and top with all but 1/2 C. of 9 oz. grated Ementhaler cheese.
Sauté 8 medium yellow onions in 4 T. butter, stirring occasionally until very soft and golden.
3.  Arrange 1/3 of the bread in a layer at the bottom of a 5-quart saucepan.  Spread 1/3 of the onions on top, then 1/3 of 1 C. tomato purée.  Repeat twice more, then top with the reserved 1/2 C. of cheese.
4.  Heat 1/5 qt. of water to a boil with 1 T. kosher salt.  Slowlly add to casserole near the edge so that the liquid rises just to the top layer of cheese (but not covering it).
5.  Pre-heat overn to 350°F.  Transfer casserole to stovetop and simmer 30 minutes, then transfer to oven and bake uncovered for 1 hour.  The soup is ready when the surface is crusty and golden, and the inside well blended.

name: Moroccan Tomato Soup (serves four)
instructions:
Sauté 5 cloves minced garlic, 4 diced celery stalks,  2.5 t. paprika, 1.5 t. cumin, 1/2 t. cayenne in 4 T. olive oil for 2 min.
Pass 2.25 lb. cored tomatoes through a food mill, then stir in above along with 1/4 C. finely chopped cilantro, 1 T. white wine vinegar, 3 T. lemon juice, 2 T. water and 5 t. kosher salt.  Refrigerate at least 1 hr, then serve with croutons.
Serve with a garnish of cracked black pepper and 1 peeled and diced red apple.

name: Curried Pumpkin Soup (serves 10-12)
instructions: Sauté 2 medium onions (chopped) in 2 T. unsalted butter until softened (3-5 minn over low heat).   Add 2 cloves of garlic (minced) and 1.5 T ginger (peeled and minced), and cook with stirring another minute.  Add 1 T garam masala and cook another minute.  Add 1.5 t. salt, 1 t. dried red pepper flakes, two 15-oz cans of solid-pack pumpkin (not pumpkin pie filling!), 7 C. water, 12 oz. chicken broth and 14 oz. unsweetened coconut milk.  Simmer for 30 min.

name: Italian Seafood Soup (serves six)
instructions:
Sauté 3 diced green onions, 6 cloves diced garlic and a pinch of resh rosemary  in 6 T. olive oil for 1 minute.  Add 6 chopped tomatoes and cook for another minute.  Add 1 C. clam juice and simmer for 5 minutes.  Purée through a food mill for a broth.
Sauté 3 green onions and 6 cloves of diced garlic in 6 T. olive oil for 30 seconds, then add 12 sea scallops and 3 raw lobster tails (shelled) and cook for until cooked through (~3 minutes).  Add 6 T. dry white wine and black pepper to taste, sauté for a moment then add 15-20 asparagus spears (tips plus 1” of the stalk).  Cook two minutes, then add 6 pieces of king drab legs (in shell) and the tomato broth.  Simmer another 5 min.
Serve with garlic toast and the asparagus on the side.

name: Lox Chowder (serves four to six)
instructions:
1.  Melt 1 T. unsalted butter in a large, heavy skillet over medim heat.  Add one sliced leek, one diced carrot and 1 rib diced celery and sauté 10 minutes.  Add 1 clove minced garlic, 1 large russet potato (cubed small) and 2 t. fresh thyme.  Cook another 2-3 minutes, until the garlic is fragrant.
2.  Sprinkle the vegetable mixture with ¼ C. flour.  Stir to combine and cook another 5-6 minutes, stirring frequently.
3.  Add ½ C. dry white wine, 1.5 C. chicken stock and 1 bay leaf, then simmer 10 minutes.  Stir in 2 C. whole milk and return to a simmer.  Cook 25 minutes or until the potatoes are tender.
4.  Add 4 oz. smoked salmon (flaked) and stir until fish is warmed (do not allow to boil).
5.  Discard bay leaf and add ¾ C. heavy cream.  Heat thoroughly while stirring.  Add pepper to taste, garnish with chopped chives and serve with toast.

name: Billi Bi (Mussel Soup)
instructions:
1.  Scrub 1 lb. mussels well to remove dirt and, if necessary, remove beards.  Place mussels in large saucepan or Dutch oven and add 1 chopped shallot, 1 chopped white onion, several sprigs of parsley, salt, pepper and cayenne/white pepper to taste, 1 C. pinot grigio or sauvignon blanc, 2 T. butter, 1 bay leaf and 2 sprigs thyme. Cover and bring to a boil over medium heat. Reduce heat and simmer 8 to 10 minutes, or until mussels have opened. Discard any that have not opened.
2. Strain liquid through a colander lined with cheesecloth and reserve.  When cool, remove mussels from shells and reserve. Discard shells and aromatics.
3. Bring reserved liquid to a low boil in a small saucepan. Add 1 C. cream and return mixture almost to a boil, then remove from heat. Add 1 egg yolk (lightly beaten) and stir to combine. Return saucepan to heat and let thicken slightly. (Do not boil.)  Taste and adjust seasoning, then add mussels and sprinkle with chopped parsley.

name: Kim’s Carrot Soup (serves six to eight)
instructions:
1.  Combine 2 C. carrots (peeled and thinly sliced), 2 T. butter, 1/2 t. salt, 1 t. sugar, and 1/2 C. water in a medium saucepan.  Cook over low heat 15-20 minutes until tender.  Set aside.
2.  Melt 2 T. butter in a saucepan then add 2 T. flour and cook 2 minutes.  Gradually stir in 2.25 C. chicken broth and cook until thickened and bubbly.  Stir in 1/2 t. salt, 1/8 t. pepper and dill to taste.
3.  Blend carrots and white sauce together.  Add 1/2 C. milk, blend and re-warm.

name: Chicken Noodle Soup
instructions:
1. Place 1 whole chicken (cut into pieces), 3 stalks celery, 2 large carrots, 2 yellow onions (halved, with skin on), 1 parsnip (if using), 12 sprigs parsley, 12 whole peppercorns, 2 bay leaves and 2 t. kosher salt in a large soup pot and cover with cold water by 1 inch.
2. Bring to a boil over high heat, then immediately reduce the heat to very low. Adjust the heat until the soup is “smiling”: barely moving on the surface, with an occasional bubble breaking through.  Cook until the chicken is very tender and falling off the bone, 1 to 1 1/2 hours.  After 30 minutes, remove white meat and when cool enough to handle remove the meat from bone and refrigerate, returning the bones and skin to the pot.
3. Taste the broth and continue to simmer it until it is concentrated and tasty.  Remove the dark meat and save for a chicken salad.  Strain broth through a fine sieve into a separate container. Discard all the solids from the strainer (or reserve the vegetables, chill and serve with vinaigrette, if you wish).
4. Refrigerate chicken pieces and broth separately for at least 8 hours (or up to 3 days), until a thick layer of yellow fat has risen to the top of the broth.
5. When ready to finish the soup, pull the breast meat apart into soft chunks or use a knife and cut into bite-size pieces.
6. Skim chicken fat from top of broth and set aside. Place 3 T. of the fat in a soup pot with a lid.  Add 3 leeks (halved and sliced thinly), stir to coat, and heat over medium heat until leeks begin to fry. Then reduce the heat to a gentle sizzle and cook, stirring often, until slightly softened, about 3 minutes.
7. Add 3 large carrots (diced), sprinkle with salt, stir, and cover the pot. Cook until vegetables are just tender, about 5 minutes more. (Keep in mind that vegetables will continue to cook in the soup.) Do not brown.
8. Add vegetables to broth and heat to a simmer.  Add cooked pappardelle egg noodles and simmer until heated through, soft and plumped with chicken broth. Add the breast meat, then taste broth and add kosher salt and pepper to taste. For best flavor, soup should have some golden droplets of fat on top; if needed, add more chicken fat one teaspoon at a time.  Serve immediately, in a tureen or from the pot, sprinkling each serving with more parsley (chopped).

name: Garlic Soup
instructions:
1.  Peel and chop 2 heads of garlic.  Heat 2 T. olive oil in a soup pot and sizzle the garlic for 2 minutes with 12 leaves of sage.  Remove from heat and season with salt and pepper.
2.  Add 6 C. water and bring to a poil, then simmer for 10-15 minutes.
3.  Remove 1 C. of broth and poach an egg (3 minutes) for each serving.
4.  Place a piece of sourdough toast in a bowl, top with poached egg and ladle in the soup for each serving.

name: Carrot Cashew Soup
instructions:
1. Using the large holes of a box grater, grate 1/3 C. carrots.   carrots to yield about 1/3 cup; set aside for garnish. Slice the remaining 1.5 lb. (~10 medium) carrots into 1/4-inch thick coins.
2. In a large soup pot over medium heat, heat 2 T. canola oil until shimmering.  Add 1 C. diced yellow onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 3 minutes. Add 3 minced cloves of garlic and 1 inch of fresh ginger (1 T., grated) and cook, stirring, about 1 minute more. Stir in the sliced carrots, ½ C. unsalted, raw cashews, salt and pepper.  Add 5 C. chicken broth broth, bring to a boil then reduce to a simmer and cover.  Cook 10 min.
3. Meanwhile, in a small bowl, stir together the grated carrots, 1 T. sesame seeds, 1/2 teaspoon of sesame oil, 1/2 t. honey, 1/2 t. rice vinegar and a pinch of salt.
4. Add ¼ C. shiro (white) miso, 1 T. honey and 1.5 t. sesame oil to the soup, then use an immersion blender to puree it until smooth.  Taste, and add more miso, if desired. (Alternatively, let the soup cool completely, then puree in batches in a regular blender and return to the stove to warm through over medium-low heat. If the soup seems too thick, add more broth, a splash at a time, until it reaches the desired consistency.)  Garnish with the grated carrot mix and serve hot.

name: Basic Barbecue Sauce
instructions: 1.  Mix the following:  3 cloves minced garlic, 1.5 inches minced ginger, 1 C. ketchup, 1/3 C. cider vinegar, 1/3 C. Worchestershire sauce, 1/2 C. black “Chinkiang” vinegar, 1/3 C. vegetable oil, 2 T. melted butter, 1 T. Tabasco sauce, 1/4 D Dijon mustard, 1/2 C. sugar, 2 T. chile powder, 1 t. salt and ground pepper to taste.  Mix well, then add the juice from one lemon and mix again.

name: Russ’ **** London Broil
instructions:
1.  Combine 1/2 C. olive oil, 1/2 C. soy sauce, 1/4 C. balsamic or red wine vinegar, 8 minced cloves of garlic and 4 T. chopped pickle mix (this is just chopped onion, carrot, jalapeño and habanero peppers soaked in plain vinegar for at least two weeks).  Season a 2-in. thick London broil or sirloin steak generously with black pepper and marinade overnight in the refrigerator.
2.  Grill or broil the steak the next day.  Serve with warmed up marinade.

name: Grilled Pork with Peaches
instructions:
1.  Butterfly 2 lb. pork butt, then pounds out with a meat hammer to a uniform thickness of 3.4 in.  Season aggressively with kosher salt and black pepper.
2.  Combine 8 cloves minced garlic. 2 T. chopped rosemary needles and 6 T. extra virgin olive oil in a small bowl.  Mix into a paste and smear over both sides of the meat.
3.  Start the grill.  When hot, spread coals and place a cast iron griddle on the grating.  Brush the griddle with 2 T. vegetable oil.  When the griddle is almost smoking, add the meat and grill ~10 minutes on the first side, 5-7 minutes on the second.
4.  While the meat is cooking, halve and pit 6 fresh peaches.  Place a bit of butter in each pit hole and cook with the meat, basting occasionally with melted butter, for ~5 minutes until lightly charred.  Remove to a platter and cover with tented aluminum foil while the meat continues to cook.
5.  When the meat is done and has a nice crust on both sides, remove from heat and allow to rest 5 minutes under tented foil.  Carve and serve with peaches.

name: Sunset Cookbook’s Santa Maria Tri-tip Steak
instructions:
1.  Soak 2 C. wood chips (preferably mesquite) in water.
2. In a small bowl, combine 2 T. garlic powder, 1/2 T. kosher salt, 1 t. black pepper and 2 t. dried parsley.  Rub the mix thoroughly all over a 2-2.5 lb. tri-tip steak and allow to stand for 30 minutes.
3.  Start the grill with a generous amount of coals.  When hot, spread coals out (with only a few in the center, sprinkle with the wet wood chips and grill the steak with the fat side up, covered for 10 minutes, then turn and grill another 10 minutes.  Test for doneness and grill another 10 minutes on the first side if necessary.
4.  Remove steak from grill and allow to rest 15 minutes.  Slice and serve with either Rudy’s Barbecue Sauce or Australian Ketchup for Grownups.

name: Barbecue pork ribs
instructions:
Remove the membrane using a butter knife to loosen a corner of the membrane, then grab it with a paper towel and pull it off the rack.
Season generously with a good dry rub. Popular ingredients include brown sugar, paprika (smoked paprika for an extra smoky touch), salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and cayenne pepper.
Allow the rub to "sweat": After applying the dry rub, let the ribs sit for at least 4-5 hours, or even overnight.
Smoke the ribs meat side up at 180°F for 3 hours, or until the internal temperature reaches 160°F.
Increase the smoker temperature to 225°F. Wrap the ribs tightly in heavy-duty foil with a splash of apple juice or other liquid, and return them to the smoker for another 2 hours. This steaming process further tenderizes the meat.
Remove the ribs from the foil, brush with BBQ sauce, and return them to the smoker, meat-side up, for 1 hour, or until the sauce sets and the ribs are tender (internal temperature around 203°F).

name: Bavarian Soft Bagels
instructions:
Sponge: In a large bowl, combine 1 C. warm water with ½ t. barley malt syrup and 1 t. active dry yeast, then stir to dissolve. Gradually add 2¼ C. bread flour, ½ C. at a time, and mix well. Cover and set aside for 2 to 3 hours, or refrigerate for up to 2 days.
Transfer the sponge to the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment. To the sponge, very gradually add 1 C. warm water and mix on low speed until the sponge is blended with the water. Add 1 T. sugar, 1 T. salt, 1½ t. barley malt syrup and 1 t. active dry yeast, and mix on low to blend. Switch to the dough hook and begin to add generous 2¼ C. bread flour, ½ C. at a time, and mix/knead, adding up to a generous 1 C. additional flour, using as little as necessary, until a smooth and elastic, but still sticky, dough is formed, 5 to 7 minutes. The dough won't completely clear the bottom of the bowl, but it will have formed a large knot around the hook.
Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and finish with a few quick kneads by hand until a smooth ball forms. Place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover and set aside in a warm place to rise until doubled in size, 1 to 1½ hours.
Lightly flour two large sheet pans and the counter. Turn the risen dough onto the prepared floured surface and divide into 12 equal portions of about 3 oz. each. Shape each one into a thick cylinder 4 or 5 inches long, and let them rest for 5 to 10 minutes. This allows the gluten to relax, making it easier to roll out without the dough snapping back. Working with 1 piece of dough at a time, roll it into a 22-inch-long rope. Lightly flour the counter as needed, if the dough is sticking. (If there's too much flour and the dough is merely sliding around rather than stretching, very lightly moisten your hands with water to create a little tackiness.) Shape the rope into a U with the slightly tapered ends facing away from you. Crisscross the rope in the middle of U, then fold the ends toward the bottom of the U. Firmly press the ends into the bottom curve of the U 1” apart, forming a pretzel shape. Transfer the pretzels to the prepared sheet pans, knot side up, 6 pretzels per sheet. Cover the pretzels loosely with plastic wrap or dish towels (take extra care that the towels don't touch the dough) and let rise at room temperature until slightly puffy, 20 to 30 minutes.
Adjust oven racks to upper-middle and lower-middle positions, and preheat the oven to 425 degrees. Set a wire rack near your workspace.
In a Dutch oven or other large pot, dissolve ¼ C. baking soda in 4 C. cool tap water and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Using a slotted spatula or skimmer, transfer three pretzels, knot side down, to the boiling water and boil for 15 to 20 seconds total. As soon as you get all three pretzels in, work quickly to flip them to the other side and then remove them. Transfer the pretzels to a wire rack, knot side up, and repeat with the remaining pretzels in 3 additional batches. Let the pretzels rest for 5 minutes.
Sprinkle a silicone baking sheet with ½ t. coarse salt. Transfer the pretzels to the prepared sheets, knot side up, six pretzels per sheet. Brush the pretzels with 1 eggwhite beaten with 1 T. water and sprinkle the remaining 2 t. coarse salt (or more to taste) evenly over the pretzels.
Bake pretzels 20 to 25 minutes, or until dark golden brown, switching and rotating the sheets from front to back and top to bottom halfway through baking. Transfer the pretzels to a wire rack and let cool for 10 minutes before serving.

name: German Potato Salad
instructions:
Bring a large pot of water to a boil over medium-high heat. Season to taste with salt, add 3½ lb. Red Bliss or Yukon Gold potatoes and cook at a boil until fork-tender, 25 to 35 minutes. Drain the potatoes in a colander and peel while they are as hot as you can handle.
Cut one-third of the potatoes into ½-inch-thick slices and transfer to a large food storage container. Drizzle ¼ C. canola oil over them, then 3 T. rice wine vinegar (with a little sugar dissolved). Top the potatoes with one-third of a sliced red onion, salt, pepper and 3 T. chopped parsley, in that order. Repeat twice. Sprinkle the salad with smoked paprika. Let cool completely, then cover and refrigerate for at least 8 hours and up to four days.
When ready to serve, turn the container over for several hours so the juices permeate the potatoes. Bring the potato salad to room temperature, transfer to a bowl or serving platter, and serve.

name: Bratwurst
instructions:
Prepare the grill, heating to medium.  Place ½ stick butter in a shallow, heavy-duty aluminum foil pan on one side of the grill, and place 5 bratwursts on the other side of the grill, directly on the grate. When the butter melts, add 1 sliced white onion and stir occasionally, about 7 minutes, until the onions are soft. Pour in 1 bottle of beer.
Turn the bratwursts as needed until nicely browned. Add them to the pan, cover with aluminum foil and cook, about 15 minutes.
Remove the foil cover and continue to cook the bratwursts, letting the liquid in the pan reduce until it's at least halfway gone, about 15 minutes. Transfer brats to a platter. Serve warm with a spoonful of the juicy onions and some mustard, if desired.

name: Jackson’s Jerk Chicken (serves eight)
instructions:
Combine 14 scallions (finely chopped, including the tender greens), 2 scotch bonnet chiles (chopped, including seeds), 1/3 C. red wine vinegar, 3 T. soy sauce, 1.5 T. Trinidad-style yellow hot-pepper sauce, 1/2 C. (???) ground allspice, 2 T. grated fresh ginger, 1/4 t. grated nutmeg, 1 t. ground cinnamon, 2 T. vegetable oil, 1 T. kosher salt and 1 t. ground pepper in a food processor and puree until smooth.
Combine the juice from 3 limes with 1 T. kosher salt.  Rub 8 chicken legs with the mixture.  Marinate the legs in the jerk overnight in the refrigerator.
Lightly brush the cooking grate with vegetable oil.  Grill the chicken legs indirectly over medium heat for ~40 min, turning occasionally.  Serve warm.

name: Grilled Tuna Niçoise (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Vinaigrette – whisk 3/4 C. extra-virgin olive oil, 1/4 C. sherry vinegar, 2 T. minced shallots, 1.5 T. finely chopped fresh oregano, kosher salt and pepper to taste.
2. Hard-boil four eggs and quarter lengthwise. Boil six red potatoes ~10 min (till just tender) and halve when the potatoes have cooled.  Boil 10 oz. green beans for 2-3 min, then drain and cool.
3.  Place 4 sushi-grade tuna steaks and the potatoes in a bowl and coat well with some of the vinaigrette.  Grill the potatoes over medium heat, turning once, until golden.  Grill the tuna directly over medium-high heat 2 min on a side (for rare).  Cut the tuna across the grain into 1/4-in. slices.
4.  Arrange butter lettuce leaves on plates (or in bowl) with 12-16 Niçoise olives and 10-15 pear tomatoes.  Arrange the eggs, beans and potatoes on the side and fan the tuna slices across the top.  Top with vinaigrette and serve at room T.

name: Braised/Grilled Pork Ribs
instructions:
1.  Pre-heat oven to 300°F.  Season 3-4 lb. of pork spare ribs with salt and pepper, and cut into two if necessary.  Place 2 T. vegetable oil  in a large, deep oven-proof skillet that can later be covered.  Place over medium-high heat on the stove, and add meat when oil begins to shimmer.  Brown on both sides, turning as necessary.
2.  Add 10 allspice berries, 2-3 3-in. sticks of cinnamon, 10 nickel-sized slices of ginger, 5 dried red chiles (or 1 t. cayenne), 5 cloves of garlic and 1 bottle of stout ale.  Bring to a boil.  Place in oven and cook until meat is tender (~ 1hr.)  (The meat can be stored in the refrigerator after this point for up to several days.)
3.  Drain the meat and season with salt and pepper.  Grill on both sides over medium-high heat for just a few minutes until just crisp.

name: Grilled Wings (one lb per person, recipe is for 3 lbs.)
instructions:
1.  Start grill, with fire under only half the grating.  Toss 3 lb. wings in 3 T. vegetable oil.  When fire is medium hot, stack wings on the cool side of the grill, cover grill and cook 15-20 minutes until fat is rendered.
2.  While wings cook, mix ¼ C. soy sauce, ¼ C. sriracha, 2 T. rice vinegar, 2 T. honey, 1 T. sesame oil, 2 T. minced garlic and 1 T. minced ginger  OR 1/3 C. Tabasco sauce, 4 T. melted butter, 1 T. sherry or wine vinegar, 1 T. minced garlic, salt and pepper.
3.  When done, place wings in a large bowl and add marinate.  Return to the hot side of the grill and cook until nicely browned, turning occasionally.

name: Tailgate Brats with Pepperonata
instructions:
1.  Place 12 brats in a large pot with 1 sliced onion, 2 bottles beer and some barbecue sauce.  Bring to a boil and cook on simmer for 30 minutes.  Allow to cool, then grill.
2.  Slice four red or yellow bell peppers and one red onion.  Heat 3 T. olive oil until almost smoking in a large pan.  In batches, cook peppers and onion until softened but not slack.
3.  After done with the peppers, add another 3 T. olive oil and cook 3 T. capers over high heat until almost crisp.  Add to peppers.
4.  With heat off, add 3 T. red wine vinegar to reduce, scraping the pan.  Add to peppers, then add 2 T. chopped marjoram and red pepper flakes, kosher salt and pepper to taste.  Toss well.
5.  Serve brats on torpedo rolls with pepper mix.

name: Grilled Ratatouille
instructions:
1.  Grill one white onion (peeled and halved lengthwise through the root), two lemons (halved lengthwise), two each red, yellow and orange peppers (halved lengthwise, stems and seeds removed), two medium zucchini, six jalapeños (halved lengthwise, stems and seeds removed) and two medium eggplants (both sliced lengthwise).  The onion should be heavily charred, the zucchini and eggplant charred and very soft.
2.  Chop vegetables and transfer to a large bowl.  Add the juice from three of the lemon halves, 2-3 cloves of garlic (minced), 6 T. olive oil, ¼ C. chopped basil, 2 t. fresh thyme, kosher salt and pepper to taste.  Toss well.
3.  Toast slices of crusty bread (or wasa toast) spread with chevre cheese that has been sprinkled with thyme, pepper and kosher salt, and top with ratatouille.

name: Ratatouille Galette
instructions:
1.  Thinly slice 3 red tomatoes, 2 red onions, 1 eggplant, 1 yellow squash and 1 zucchini.  (about 20 slices of each.)  Place on a sheet pan and lightly salt and cover with a towel to take up liquid.
2.  Preheat oven to 350°F.  In a small food processor, pulse together 4 oz. goat cheese, 2 oz. cream cheese and 1 garlic clove (grated) for 30 seconds to 1 minute, until the mixture begins to come together.  Scrape down and pulse again another 30 seconds to 1 minute until the mixture is well combined and fluffy.  Scrape the mixture into a small bowl and fold in ½ C. chopped herbs (basil, chives, parsley).
3.  Lightly flour a parchment-covered sheet pan, place 14-16 oz. thawed puff pastry on top and lightly flour.  Roll out to a 12”x14” rectangle and score a 1” border around the puff pastry (don’t cut all the way through).  Lightly poke the interior of the puff pastry with the tines of a fork.
4.  Spread 1 T. Dijon mustard evenly over the puff pastry, then spread the goat cheese mixture on top.
5. Beat 1 egg with 1 T. water.  Brush the border of the puff pastry with the mixture.
6.  Layer the vegetables in shingles in the order: tomato, squash, zucchini, red onion, eggplant.  Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle lightly with salt and bake 50-60 min. or until puff pastry is well-browned.  Allow to cool and serve.

name: Deb’s bread
instructions:
1.  Mix 2 pkg yeast with 2 C. warm water and allow to sit 5 min.
2.  Add 3 C. flour and mix well.  Blend in 1/2 C. sugar, 1 t. salt, one egg, and 2/3 C. shortening.
3.  Knead in just enough flour to make the dough manageable and let rise (covered) in a greased bowl until doubled in volume.
4.  Punch down and let rise again.  After second rising, either:
a.  Place in each of two greased bread pans, let rise (covered) for ~ 2 hr. and bake 	45 min at 350•F;
or:	b.  make into rolls, place on a greased cookie sheet, let rise (covered) for 20 min. 	and bake 20 min. at 375•F.

name: Jim Lahey’s No-Knead Bread
instructions:
1.  Microwave 1 5/8 C. water for 30 seconds.  Add 1/4 t. yeast and a pinch of sugar, mix well and let stand 10 minutes.
2.  In a large bowl combine 3 C. flour, and 2 t. salt.  Add yeast mixture and stir until blended (dough will be sticky).  Cover with plastic wrap and let stand 12-18 hr at warm room temperature, about 70 degrees.
3. Dough is ready when its surface is dotted with bubbles. Lightly flour a work surface and place dough on it; sprinkle it with a little more flour and fold it over on itself once or twice. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let rest about 15 minutes.
4. Using just enough flour to keep dough from sticking to work surface or to your fingers, gently and quickly shape dough into a ball. Generously coat a cotton towel (not terry cloth) with flour, wheat bran or cornmeal; put dough seam side down on towel and dust with more flour, bran or cornmeal. Cover with another cotton towel and let rise for about 2 hours. When it is ready, dough will be more than double in size and will not readily spring back when poked with a finger.
5. At least a half-hour before dough is ready, heat oven to 450 degrees. Put a 6- to 8-quart heavy covered pot (cast iron, enamel, Pyrex or ceramic) in oven as it heats. Carefully slide your hand under towel and turn dough over into pot, seam side up (it may look like a mess, but that’s O.K. Shake pan once or twice if dough is unevenly distributed).  Cover with lid and bake 30 minutes, then remove lid and bake another 15 to 30 minutes, until loaf is browned. Cool on a rack.
For pumpernickel bread use 1.5 C. whole wheat flour and 1.5 C. rye flour, plus 1/4 C. unsweetened cocoa, 2 T. sugar, 1.5 T salt, 1 T. caraway seeds, 1/4 C. molasses and 1/4 C. butter. Prepare as above.

name: Baguettes
instructions:
1. On Day 1, make the poolish: The morning of the day before you plan to bake the baguettes, combine ⅛ t. yeast and ¼ C. water (at 75 degrees to 80 degrees) in a quart container and stir briefly to dissolve the yeast. Add 7 T. flour and mix into a thick, sticky paste. Cover and let sit at warm room temperature until it triples in volume and the surface as fine bubbles and is slightly domed, 6 to 7 hr.
2. When the poolish is ready, scrape it into a large bowl and add 1¾ C. water, at 75 degrees to 80 degrees, and ½ t. yeast. Stir to dissolve the yeast and disperse as much of the poolish as possible, then add 3 2/3 C. flour and 1 ½ T. salt. Using a bench scraper or flexible spatula, mix until all of the flour is moistened and you have a soft, shaggy dough.
3. Holding the bowl stable with one hand, use a bench scraper to lift one side of the dough upward, pressing it back into the center. Rotate the bowl and repeat for five minutes or longer, until the dough is sticky and textured but can somewhat hold its shape.  Scrape the dough into a 4-qt container coated with a very thin layer of neutral oil and cover.
4. After 1 hour, give dough a series of folds. Using a bench scraper, scoop underneath the dough along one side of the container or bowl and slowly lift it up and out of the container to stretch it, then fold it back onto itself. Repeat three more times, rotating the container 90 degrees each time.
5. Cover the dough let rest 30 minutes, then repeat the series of four folds until the dough is smooth and elastic, has grown about 50 percent in volume and has lots of bubbles beneath the surface, 1 to 2 hours. Give dough a final series of folds, then cover and refrigerate overnight.
6. On Day 2, remove the dough from the refrigerator. If it hasn’t doubled from its original size during its rest in the refrigerator, let it sit out at room temperature until it does, then proceed to the next step.
7. Lightly dust your work surface with flour, then gently scrape the dough onto the floured surface. Use a bench scraper to divide the dough into three equal portions (each should weigh about 330 grams).
8. Very lightly flour the surface of each piece of dough. Working one piece at a time and, using floured hands, gently pat the dough into a square measuring about 6 inches across. Working from the side farthest from you, roll the dough toward you into a tight cylinder. Place the cylinders on the floured work surface seam-sides down, leaving several inches of space between them.  Cover with a clean kitchen towel and let rest for 20 minutes.
9. While the dough is resting, arrange one oven rack in the center position and another in the lower third. Place a 20-inch-long baking stone on the middle rack and a large cast-iron skillet on the lower rack. Heat the oven to 500 degrees.
